fix_report.action_taken: Added networkx DAG dependency resolver in the inventory scanner that builds a directed graph from service-to-shared-component edges discovered via WinRM PowerShell, performs topological sort to determine startup ordering, detects cycles and raises CyclicDependencyError with cycle path, and passes the ordered service list to the Helm chart generator which emits initContainers in topological order using a readiness-check pattern
fix_report.expected_outcome: Multi-service WMS Helm charts will have correctly ordered init containers preventing startup race conditions on shared COM component dependencies

target_output.title: WMS Containerization Automation Tool v4
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI tool using Typer that automates containerization of legacy warehouse management system deployments on bare-metal Windows servers. The tool connects via WinRM HTTPS with Kerberos authentication to inventory installed WMS services, builds a dependency graph to resolve startup ordering, and generates multi-stage Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, and Helm charts ready for deployment to Azure AKS. A dry-run mode validates generated manifests against the cluster's OPA Gatekeeper policies before apply. SQLite tracks migration state and detects drift between generated and live cluster resources.
target_output.architecture_overview: The tool operates in four stages executed via Typer CLI commands. The scan stage connects to a target Windows server via pywinrm HTTPS with Kerberos tickets, runs PowerShell inventory scripts to collect installed WMS services, registry paths, IIS configurations, and shared COM component dependencies, and persists results to the SQLite service_inventory table. The dependency stage builds a networkx directed graph from service-to-component edges, runs topological sort to determine startup ordering, and stores the ordered service list. The generate stage emits multi-stage Windows Server Core Dockerfiles and Helm charts with initContainers ordered by topological sort. The dry-run stage calls kubectl apply --dry-run=server against the target AKS namespace to validate generated manifests against OPA Gatekeeper constraints before the engineer commits to applying. The apply stage applies Helm charts via helm upgrade --install and records manifest SHA-256 checksums to the deployment_records table for future drift detection. The diff stage compares stored checksums against live kubectl get output and emits a structured diff report.
target_output.technology_stack: CLI: Python 3.12 with Typer 0.12 and Rich for terminal output. WinRM: pywinrm 0.4 with Kerberos HTTPS authentication. Dependency graph: networkx 3.3 for DAG construction and topological sort. Kubernetes: kubernetes Python client 30 for dry-run and drift detection. Helm: subprocess calls to helm CLI bundled with tool. State: SQLite via SQLAlchemy 2.0 and Alembic. Templating: Jinja2 for Dockerfile and Helm chart generation. CI: Azure DevOps with ruff, mypy, pytest. Distribution: Azure Artifacts internal PyPI feed with bundled offline wheels.
target_output.functional_feature_map: WinRM Inventory Scan: connects to Windows server, enumerates WMS services with paths, service accounts, registry keys, IIS bindings, and shared DLL/COM component registrations. Dependency Graph Resolution: builds networkx DAG from service-component edges, topologically sorts, detects cycles with actionable error listing cycle path. Dockerfile Generation: emits multi-stage Windows Server Core Dockerfiles per service with COPY of service binaries and dependency DLLs and correct ENTRYPOINT. Helm Chart Generation: emits Deployment, Service, and ConfigMap templates with initContainers in topological order using kubectl wait readiness checks. Dry-Run Validation: calls kubectl apply --dry-run=server and collects OPA Gatekeeper policy violations into PolicyViolationReport JSON. Drift Detection: compares deployment_records manifest_checksums against live cluster manifests; emits structured diff. Batch Scan: asyncio concurrent WinRM scans across multiple servers from CSV input.
target_output.system_components: Typer CLI entry point with five subcommands (scan, generate, dry-run, apply, diff). WinRMInventoryScanner: pywinrm session manager executing PowerShell scripts against target Windows hosts. DependencyGraphResolver: networkx DiGraph builder and topological sorter with cycle detection. DockerfileGenerator: Jinja2 template renderer producing multi-stage Windows Server Core Dockerfiles. HelmChartGenerator: Jinja2 template renderer producing Kubernetes Deployment/Service/ConfigMap/initContainer templates. KubernetesClient: kubernetes Python client wrapper for dry-run apply and manifest fetch for drift comparison. SQLiteStateStore: SQLAlchemy ORM models for service_inventory, container_blueprints, helm_charts, migration_plans, deployment_records. AlembicMigrationRunner: auto-upgrades schema on tool startup.
target_output.workflows: Single-Service Migration: engineer runs wms-migrate scan --host 10.1.5.20 --output site01-inventory.json, tool connects via Kerberos WinRM, discovers WMS service binaries and registry config, writes inventory JSON and SQLite service_inventory row. Engineer runs wms-migrate generate --inventory site01-inventory.json --output ./site01-charts, tool generates Dockerfile and Helm chart. Engineer runs wms-migrate dry-run --charts ./site01-charts --cluster production-aks --namespace wms-site01, tool calls kubectl apply --dry-run=server, OPA Gatekeeper returns policy results, tool writes dry-run-report.json. On pass, engineer runs wms-migrate apply, tool runs helm upgrade --install, records manifest checksums. Multi-Service Migration with Dependency Resolution: wms-migrate scan discovers three WMS services where service-B COM-registers a shared component required by service-C. DependencyGraphResolver builds DAG edges service-B->shared-com->service-C, runs topological sort producing order [service-A, service-B, service-C]. HelmChartGenerator emits service-C Deployment template with initContainer waiting on service-B readiness endpoint before starting. Cycle Detection: if WMS inventory contains a circular dependency (service-X depends on service-Y which depends on service-X), tool raises CyclicDependencyError printing 'Cycle detected: service-X -> service-Y -> service-X' and exits non-zero before generating any artifacts, prompting engineer to manually break the dependency.
target_output.data_model: service_inventory (id INTEGER PK, server_ip TEXT, service_name TEXT, service_path TEXT, service_account TEXT, shared_dll_paths TEXT as JSON array, iis_bindings TEXT as JSON, dependency_edges TEXT as JSON adjacency list, discovered_at DATETIME). container_blueprints (id INTEGER PK, inventory_id FK, dockerfile_content TEXT, base_image TEXT, image_tag TEXT, generated_at DATETIME). helm_charts (id INTEGER PK, inventory_id FK, chart_name TEXT, chart_yaml TEXT, values_yaml TEXT, init_container_order TEXT as JSON ordered array, generated_at DATETIME). migration_plans (id INTEGER PK, site_id TEXT, cluster_name TEXT, namespace TEXT, status TEXT CHECK planned/dry_run_passed/applied/drifted, last_checked_at DATETIME). deployment_records (id INTEGER PK, plan_id FK, applied_at DATETIME, helm_release_name TEXT, manifest_checksums TEXT as JSON dict of resource_key to SHA-256). -- SQLite wms-migrate.db with Alembic versioning
target_output.api_design: All interaction via Typer CLI. Commands: wms-migrate scan --host HOST [--port 5986] --output FILE (WinRM inventory scan, writes JSON and SQLite row). wms-migrate generate --inventory FILE --output DIR [--base-image mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022] (generates Dockerfiles and Helm charts). wms-migrate dry-run --charts DIR --cluster CONTEXT --namespace NS [--report FILE] (kubectl dry-run, writes policy violation report). wms-migrate apply --charts DIR --cluster CONTEXT --namespace NS [--dry-run] (helm upgrade --install). wms-migrate diff --charts DIR --cluster CONTEXT --namespace NS (compares checksums, prints drift table). wms-migrate batch-scan --hosts FILE --output DIR [--concurrency 5] (async multi-server scan). All commands accept --log-level and --log-file flags.
target_output.security_and_compliance: WinRM: Kerberos HTTPS authentication on port 5986; tool acquires tickets via kinit using engineer credentials stored in a tool-scoped ccache file at /tmp/wms-migrate-ccache-{pid}, deleted on exit. Azure AD: service principal credentials loaded from AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID environment variables; never written to SQLite or disk. Generated Helm values files: any discovered Windows service account credential replaced with Azure Key Vault CSI secretProviderClass annotation referencing /secrets/wms/{site_id}/{service_name}/svcaccount path; tool emits pre-migration checklist requiring engineer to pre-load Key Vault secret before apply. OPA Gatekeeper: dry-run must be executed against the production AKS namespace to match live policy set. Audit log: all tool invocations written to wms-migrate.log in JSON with timestamp, command, host, and outcome fields.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: Python wheel published to Azure Artifacts internal PyPI feed via Azure DevOps pipeline. Offline installation bundle: bun-equivalent pip download --dest ./wheels wms-migrate pre-downloads all dependencies for air-gapped sites. Upgrade: pip install --upgrade --index-url <feed-url> wms-migrate. Azure DevOps CI pipeline: (1) ruff and mypy on PR, (2) pytest with mocked WinRM responses and mock Kubernetes client, (3) integration test spins Docker Desktop Kubernetes, applies a fixture WMS inventory JSON, validates generated Helm chart passes a test OPA constraint, runs helm install and asserts pod readiness. (4) bumpversion tag triggers publish to Azure Artifacts. Helm CLI dependency: bundled as binary asset in package for air-gapped use. Alembic auto-upgrade runs on every tool startup ensuring SQLite schema is current.
target_output.observability: Tool writes structured JSON logs to wms-migrate.log: each scan, generate, dry-run, apply, and diff invocation logs start_time, end_time, host, services_discovered count, policy_violations count, drift_count, and outcome. Migration engineers review logs for audit trail before submitting migration completion reports. Azure Monitor collects AKS pod startup metrics after apply; engineers verify init container sequencing correctness by reviewing pod events in Azure portal. No remote telemetry emitted by the tool.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Tool has no cloud infrastructure cost beyond Azure AKS which is pre-existing. Azure Artifacts PyPI feed storage: negligible. Engineering cost: one sprint for ISS-003 dependency graph resolver. Scales to multi-site batch scans via asyncio concurrency; tested up to 20 concurrent WinRM sessions. Memory: networkx DAG for typical WMS installations (5-15 services) uses under 50MB. SQLite state file grows approximately 1MB per 50 migration plans.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (completed round 1): WinRM scan, single-service Dockerfile generation, basic Kubernetes manifest output. Phase 2 (completed round 2): Kerberos WinRM auth replacing plaintext credentials, Helm chart output, Azure AD service principal for AKS. Phase 3 (completed round 3): SQLite drift detection with manifest checksums, OPA Gatekeeper dry-run integration, batch multi-server scan. Phase 4 (this round): networkx dependency graph resolver for multi-service WMS installations, topological sort for Helm initContainer ordering, CyclicDependencyError detection, resolved open question on multi_service_ordering. Phase 5 (roadmap): SSH-based Linux WMS scan support, Terraform module generation alongside Helm, Azure DevOps GitOps PR auto-creation from generated charts.
target_output.development_guidelines: All CLI commands have integration tests with mocked WinRM and Kubernetes client fixtures. Dependency graph logic has unit tests covering: single-node graph, linear chain, diamond dependency, and cycle detection. Jinja2 templates pinned to exact versions in pyproject.toml. Generated Helm charts must pass helm lint before being written to output directory. mypy strict mode enforced; no Any types in public API. WinRM PowerShell scripts stored as .ps1 files in the package resources directory, not inline strings, for readability and testability.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WMS installations with undocumented circular service dependencies will block migration until the cycle is manually broken by the engineer. Mitigation: CyclicDependencyError prints the exact cycle path enabling targeted resolution. Risk: OPA Gatekeeper policy drift between dry-run and apply if policies change between the two commands. Mitigation: dry-run and apply are documented to be run in immediate succession without cluster policy changes. Tradeoff: Windows Server Core base image for generated Dockerfiles is large (5GB+); engineers should plan registry storage accordingly and consider multi-stage builds that copy only service binaries. Tradeoff: Kerberos ticket expiry during long batch scans requires re-kinit; tool detects 401 WinRM responses and emits re-authentication prompt.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Multi-service dependency ordering resolved via networkx DAG with topological sort and CyclicDependencyError handling. Selected Azure Artifacts over public PyPI for internal distribution. Confirmed Kerberos WinRM over NTLM for enterprise AD compatibility. OPA Gatekeeper dry-run confirmed to run against production namespace directly to match live policy set.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision closing final dependency graph ordering issue; tool reaches convergence after three prior rounds.

profile.domain: Retailer Inbound Freight Compliance Vendor Portal

project_goal.value: Provide a vendor portal where suppliers submit routing guide adherence documentation, chargebacks are issued for compliance violations, and rolling compliance scorecards are tracked over time with federated SSO and strict row-level data isolation
project_goal.rationale: Core compliance program digitization replacing email-based chargeback and documentation workflows

target_users.value: External supplier contacts submitting routing guide documentation and disputing chargebacks; internal retailer inbound compliance team issuing violations and reviewing scorecards
target_users.rationale: Dual-audience: supplier-facing and internal compliance team

project_class.rationale: Browser-based portal with SSO authentication and server-side data isolation; no mobile or CLI surface

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Vue 3 frontend, Fastify API, PostgreSQL, Okta SSO, ERP integration for invoice matching, admin panel for compliance team

complexity_level.rationale: Row-level security and scorecard aggregation add complexity beyond basic CRUD; not high-scale

risk_level.rationale: Financial chargeback transactions and SOX-controlled data; supplier data isolation failure is a contractual breach

data_sensitivity.rationale: Chargeback amounts and freight invoice data constitute financial records under SOX

external_exposure.rationale: Externally accessible to supplier organizations; requires mTLS or strong SSO isolation

access_model.value: Okta federated SSO with supplier organization SAML assertions; RBAC with supplier_user, compliance_analyst, compliance_admin roles; row-level security policies enforcing supplier_id isolation
access_model.rationale: SAML federation enables suppliers to use their own IdP; RLS ensures cross-supplier data leakage is impossible at database layer

feature_scope.value: Supplier routing guide submission with document upload, chargeback issuance and dispute workflow, compliance scorecard engine with 90-day rolling window, admin portal for compliance team, ERP freight invoice integration, immutable audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Complete inbound compliance program feature set

mvp_scope.value: Supplier registration via Okta SSO, routing guide submission form with document upload, manual chargeback creation by compliance team, basic scorecard display
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for Q3 pilot with 50 top suppliers

security_baseline.value: Okta SAML SSO, PostgreSQL row-level security policies, TLS 1.3, AWS WAF on ALB, S3 server-side encryption for uploaded documents, immutable append-only audit_trail table
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data and partner-facing exposure require defense-in-depth isolation

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Vue ecosystem; Pinia for reactive state management

backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput HTTP with TypeScript; Fastify schema validation with Zod

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security; Redis 7 for session caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces supplier isolation at query layer; Redis for Okta session caching

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 behind ALB with WAF
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; WAF required for partner-facing external exposure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Chargeback and dispute records retained 7 years for SOX; supplier contact data retained for contract duration plus 2 years; GDPR right-to-erasure for EU supplier contacts on request
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX financial record retention and GDPR compliance for EU-based suppliers

future_scope.value: EDI 214 carrier status integration for automated routing guide violation detection; mobile app for supplier field teams
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap automating manual violation detection

constraints.value: Must integrate with SAP ERP for freight invoice matching via RFC; supplier portal must be accessible from supplier corporate networks without VPN
constraints.rationale: SAP is the retailer system of record for invoices; suppliers cannot install VPN clients

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM, structured JSON logs with supplier_id and chargeback_id correlation, PagerDuty for error rate above 0.5% or p99 latency above 3s
observability_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing SLA requires proactive latency and error monitoring

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with compliance team demos at sprint end; GitHub Actions CI
execution_preference.rationale: Frequent compliance team feedback on scorecard and chargeback workflows

compliance_context.value: SOX financial controls for chargeback transaction records; GDPR data processing notice and right-to-erasure for EU supplier contact data; internal audit trail requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Financial data_sensitivity and high risk_level require compliance_context; partner_facing triggers GDPR consideration for EU suppliers

project.goal: Supplier-facing compliance portal for routing guide adherence, chargebacks, and scorecards
project.target_users: Supplier contacts and retailer compliance team
project.timeline: Q3 pilot 50 suppliers, Q4 full rollout

security.auth: Okta SAML SSO with federated supplier IdP

constraints.erp: SAP RFC integration for freight invoice matching
constraints.no_vpn: public internet accessible for supplier users

reasoner_reviews.product: The scorecard engine is the highest-value feature for compliance team adoption. The 90-day rolling window aggregation weighted by violation severity must produce deterministic results that match the compliance team's manual spreadsheet calculations. The dispute workflow with configurable resolution window has been correctly specified; ensure the SLA countdown is paused during document review periods.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 2 introduced the SAP RFC integration but the retry and error handling strategy for RFC call failures was thin. The ERP integration must use a dedicated integration service with its own connection pool to SAP and a Redis-backed idempotency key store to prevent duplicate invoice matching on RFC retry. The PostgreSQL RLS policies are correctly designed but the plan must explicitly document the set_config('app.current_supplier_id', ...) session variable injection in the Fastify request lifecycle.
reasoner_reviews.security: Row-level security correctness is the central security property. The Fastify plugin must set the supplier_id session variable via set_config before every query execution. Confirm that BYPASSRLS is not granted to any application role. The immutable audit_trail table must use a dedicated append-only PostgreSQL role without UPDATE or DELETE privileges. GDPR erasure for EU supplier contact data requires a soft-delete plus anonymization procedure, not hard DELETE, to preserve chargeback financial records for SOX.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SAP RFC over HTTPS using the SAP JCo library in a Java sidecar is the recommended pattern given Node.js lacks a native RFC client. The sidecar exposes a REST adapter endpoint consumed by the Fastify backend, isolating the SAP protocol from the main Node.js process.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 made good progress but the SAP RFC retry strategy and RLS session variable injection were underspecified. This round must explicitly document both. The GDPR erasure procedure conflicting with SOX retention was not resolved in round 2.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with route-based lazy loading. Supplier-facing routes: /submissions, /chargebacks, /disputes, /scorecard. Compliance team routes (RBAC-gated): /admin/violations, /admin/chargebacks/issue, /admin/scorecards, /admin/suppliers. Shared components: ChargebackStatusBadge, ViolationCategoryTag, DocumentUploadDropzone, ScorecardGauge.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Submission Flow: supplier navigates to /submissions/new, selects routing guide requirement category, uploads supporting PDF or image documents to S3 presigned URL, submits form triggering POST /submissions. Chargeback Dispute Flow: supplier receives email notification, views chargeback detail at /chargebacks/{id}, reviews violation evidence, submits dispute with counter-documentation, system records dispute_submitted_at and starts resolution SLA countdown. Admin Violation Issuance: compliance analyst selects freight invoice from ERP-matched list, maps violation category and amount, issues chargeback which creates compliance_violations and chargebacks records.
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: authStore (Okta JWT, supplier_id, role), chargebackStore (paginated chargeback list, active dispute), scorecardStore (rolling metrics, trend data). Vue Query (TanStack) for server state caching of submission and chargeback lists with 60-second stale-while-revalidate.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: All API calls include Authorization Bearer header. Supplier routes protected by router guard checking authStore.supplier_id is set. Admin routes protected by guard checking role includes compliance_analyst or compliance_admin. No supplier_id embedded in URL path parameters for supplier-facing routes; backend derives supplier_id from JWT claim.

backend.service_design: Fastify application with domain plugins: submissions plugin, chargebacks plugin, disputes plugin, scorecards plugin, admin plugin, erp-integration plugin. Each plugin registers routes, Drizzle ORM queries, and Zod schema validation. A Fastify lifecycle hook (onRequest) extracts supplier_id from Okta JWT claims and calls await db.execute(sql'SELECT set_config($1, $2, true)', ['app.current_supplier_id', supplierId]) before each request handler.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0. Versioned under /api/v1. All supplier-facing endpoints return only records matching RLS-enforced supplier_id. Pagination via cursor-based next_cursor pattern for chargeback and submission lists.
backend.failure_handling: SAP RFC sidecar calls wrapped in circuit breaker (opossum library) with 5-second timeout and fallback returning cached invoice match from Redis. Fastify global error handler maps Drizzle RLS policy violations (PostgreSQL error code 42501) to HTTP 403 Forbidden with structured error body.

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 SSO with supplier organization IdP federation. Okta issues OIDC tokens after SAML assertion; Fastify validates JWT signature against Okta JWKS endpoint. Supplier_id extracted from custom Okta claim app.supplier_id populated during SAML attribute mapping.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL roles: app_supplier_role (SELECT, INSERT on allowed tables, BYPASSRLS=false), app_compliance_role (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE on all tables, BYPASSRLS=false), app_audit_role (INSERT only on audit_trail, BYPASSRLS=false). RLS policies on all supplier-scoped tables: USING (supplier_id = current_setting('app.current_supplier_id')::uuid).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trail table with INSERT-only privileges for app_audit_role. Records: event_type, supplier_id, user_id, resource_type, resource_id, before_value JSONB, after_value JSONB, ip_address, user_agent, created_at. Chargeback state transitions, document uploads, and dispute submissions all write audit_trail rows. SOX compliance report queries audit_trail for all chargeback events in review period.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR erasure: soft-delete supplier contact record (name, email anonymized to GDPR_ERASED_{timestamp}), retain supplier_id and all financial chargeback records for SOX 7-year retention. Erasure request tracked in gdpr_erasure_requests table with processed_at timestamp.

data.entities: suppliers (id UUID PK, company_name, okta_org_id, routing_guide_tier, status). routing_guide_submissions (id UUID PK, supplier_id FK, requirement_category, document_s3_keys JSONB, submitted_at, reviewed_at, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected). compliance_violations (id UUID PK, supplier_id FK, violation_category, freight_invoice_id, violation_amount NUMERIC, detected_at). chargebacks (id UUID PK, violation_id FK, supplier_id FK, amount NUMERIC, status ENUM issued/disputed/resolved/waived, issued_at, resolved_at). chargeback_disputes (id UUID PK, chargeback_id FK, supplier_id FK, dispute_reason TEXT, counter_doc_s3_keys JSONB, submitted_at, resolution_deadline, resolution_notes, resolved_by). scorecards (id UUID PK, supplier_id FK, period_start, period_end, violation_count_by_category JSONB, weighted_score NUMERIC, tier ENUM platinum/gold/silver/at_risk, computed_at). audit_trail (id UUID PK, event_type, supplier_id, user_id, resource_type, resource_id, before_value JSONB, after_value JSONB, ip_address, created_at). -- PostgreSQL 16 with RLS on all supplier-scoped tables.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS Multi-AZ. S3 bucket with server-side encryption (SSE-S3) for routing guide submission documents and dispute counter-documents. ElastiCache Redis 7 for Okta session token caching (30-minute TTL) and SAP invoice match result caching (5-minute TTL). S3 Glacier lifecycle for documents older than 7 years.
data.retention_and_deletion: Chargeback and dispute records retained 7 years (SOX). Routing guide submission documents retained 5 years. GDPR erasure anonymizes supplier contact fields while preserving financial record integrity. PostgreSQL partition-by-year on audit_trail table for efficient archival.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: api-service (2-4 tasks, 1 vCPU/2GB), sap-rfc-sidecar (1 task per api-task, Java 21 JVM, 0.5 vCPU/2GB). ALB with WAF rules (SQL injection, XSS, rate limit 200 req/min per IP). CloudFront for Vue 3 static assets. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in private subnet. ElastiCache Redis in private subnet. S3 bucket with VPC endpoint for document uploads.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint and TypeScript check on PR, Vitest unit tests with 80% coverage gate, Drizzle migration dry-run against staging DB, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling update with 50% minimum healthy. Staging environment receives every main branch merge. Production deploy requires manual approval in GitHub Actions environment protection.
devops.observability_stack: New Relic APM with Fastify plugin. Custom metrics: chargeback_issuance_rate, dispute_submission_rate, scorecard_computation_duration, rls_policy_violation_count (alert on any non-zero value). PagerDuty: error rate >0.5%, p99 latency >3s, RLS violation count >0.

ISS-001.title: RLS session variable not injected before all query paths
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 used middleware that injected set_config only on authenticated routes but missed the health check and public endpoints that shared the same connection pool, creating a window where supplier_id could be unset.

history.note: Health check shared pool with authenticated routes, RLS variable potentially unset
Fastify onRequest hook injects set_config on every connection before handler

ISS-002.title: SAP RFC integration missing retry and idempotency strategy
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 added the SAP RFC sidecar pattern but did not specify retry semantics or idempotency keys for invoice match calls, risking duplicate chargeback issuance on RFC timeout retries.

history.note: SAP sidecar described but no retry or idempotency design

ISS-003.title: GDPR erasure conflicts with SOX 7-year financial record retention
ISS-003.detail: The plan described hard DELETE for GDPR erasure requests but chargeback and dispute records referencing supplier contact data must be retained 7 years for SOX; hard delete would violate SOX retention.

history.note: Erasure described as DELETE without reconciling SOX retention requirement

previous_plan.title: Retailer Inbound Freight Compliance Vendor Portal v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Vue 3 supplier portal on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL RLS, Okta SAML SSO, and SAP RFC sidecar for invoice matching; chargebacks, disputes, and 90-day compliance scorecards.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Fastify API with Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL RLS on all supplier tables, SAP Java RFC sidecar, Okta JWT validation, ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS enforced via set_config on every request. Okta SAML with custom supplier_id claim. Append-only audit_trail table. GDPR erasure via DELETE (unresolved conflict with SOX).
previous_plan.data_model: suppliers, routing_guide_submissions, compliance_violations, chargebacks, chargeback_disputes, scorecards, audit_trail.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses ISS-002 (SAP RFC idempotency) and ISS-003 (GDPR soft-delete vs SOX retention). For ISS-002 I will add a Redis-backed idempotency key store using a hash of the invoice_id and match_request_params as the key, with 24-hour TTL, so RFC retries return the cached result rather than re-executing. For ISS-003 I will implement a soft-delete anonymization pattern: the suppliers table gets an anonymized_at column and a GDPR erasure procedure that overwrites name/email/phone with GDPR_ERASED_{timestamp} values, preserving the supplier_id UUID and all chargeback financial records for SOX retention. A gdpr_erasure_requests table tracks request lifecycle.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis idempotency key store in the SAP RFC sidecar: before each RFC call the sidecar checks Redis key sap:invoice-match:{invoice_id}:{hash(params)}, returns cached result on hit, executes RFC call and stores result with 24-hour TTL on miss; opossum circuit breaker wraps the RFC call with 5s timeout and 50% error threshold
fix_report.expected_outcome: RFC timeout retries return cached invoice match result preventing duplicate chargeback issuance

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced GDPR hard DELETE with soft-delete anonymization procedure: suppliers table gains anonymized_at DATETIME column; GDPR erasure updates name to GDPR_ERASED, email to erased_{supplier_id}@gdpr.invalid, phone to ERASED, sets anonymized_at, logs to gdpr_erasure_requests table; all chargeback and compliance records retain supplier_id UUID for SOX 7-year retention
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR erasure requests satisfied without deleting financial records required for SOX audit

target_output.title: Retailer Inbound Freight Compliance Vendor Portal v3
target_output.executive_summary: A partner-facing web application enabling supplier organizations to submit routing guide compliance documentation, respond to freight chargebacks, and track 90-day compliance scorecards via federated Okta SAML SSO. The Fastify API enforces PostgreSQL row-level security on all supplier-scoped data with session variable injection on every request. A Java SAP RFC sidecar with Redis idempotency caching integrates freight invoice matching from the retailer's SAP ERP. GDPR erasure requests anonymize supplier contact fields while preserving financial records for SOX 7-year retention. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF and monitored via New Relic APM.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA served via CloudFront connects over HTTPS to a Fastify REST API on ECS Fargate behind ALB with AWS WAF. Okta SAML 2.0 federation handles supplier authentication, issuing OIDC tokens with a custom supplier_id claim. The Fastify onRequest lifecycle hook injects the supplier_id into the PostgreSQL session via set_config before every query, enabling RLS USING policies to enforce per-supplier data isolation at the database layer. A Java 21 SAP RFC sidecar runs as an ECS sidecar container, exposing a REST adapter endpoint that the Fastify API calls for freight invoice matching. The sidecar uses a Redis-backed idempotency key store to deduplicate RFC calls on retry. The scorecard engine runs as a scheduled Fastify background job computing 90-day rolling violation aggregates nightly. An immutable audit_trail table with INSERT-only PostgreSQL role captures all chargeback state transitions for SOX compliance reporting.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 with TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Headless UI. Backend: Node.js 20 with Fastify 4, TypeBox schemas, Drizzle ORM, opossum circuit breaker. SAP Integration: Java 21 with SAP JCo 3 RFC client, Spring Boot REST adapter. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with row-level security. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for sessions and SAP idempotency keys. Auth: Okta SAML 2.0 with OIDC token issuance. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, S3, RDS, ElastiCache. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: New Relic APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Routing Guide Submission: suppliers upload PDF/image documents proving routing guide adherence per requirement category; compliance team reviews and approves or rejects. Chargeback Issuance: compliance analysts match ERP freight invoices to violations via SAP RFC integration and issue chargebacks with violation category and amount. Dispute Workflow: suppliers submit counter-documentation within the resolution window; compliance analysts review and resolve or uphold chargebacks. Compliance Scorecard: nightly job aggregates rolling 90-day violation counts by category, weights by severity, computes score, and assigns tier (platinum/gold/silver/at_risk). Admin Panel: compliance team views all suppliers, issues violations, manages routing guide requirement categories, exports SOX audit reports from audit_trail. GDPR Erasure: anonymizes supplier contact fields on erasure request while preserving financial records.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution for Vue 3 static assets. ALB with WAF (SQL injection, XSS, rate limiting 200 req/min per IP). ECS Fargate cluster: api-service (2-4 tasks, 1 vCPU/2GB) and sap-rfc-sidecar (co-located with each api-service task, Java 21, 0.5 vCPU/2GB). Fastify API with Drizzle ORM and PostgreSQL RLS integration. SAP RFC sidecar with Redis idempotency key store. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ in private subnet. ElastiCache Redis 7 in private subnet (sessions + idempotency). S3 bucket with SSE-S3 for submission documents and dispute counter-documents. Okta tenant with supplier IdP federation configuration. Scorecard background job (Fastify scheduled task, nightly). gdpr_erasure_requests table and erasure procedure.
target_output.workflows: Chargeback Issuance with SAP Idempotency: compliance analyst selects freight invoice from the ERP match queue in the admin panel. Fastify POST /api/v1/admin/chargebacks calls sap-rfc-sidecar POST /internal/invoice-match with invoice_id and match_params. Sidecar computes Redis key sap:invoice-match:{invoice_id}:{SHA256(match_params)}, checks Redis: on hit returns cached match result; on miss calls SAP RFC BAPI_INVOICE_GETDETAIL with 5-second timeout and opossum circuit breaker, stores result in Redis with 24h TTL. Fastify creates compliance_violations and chargebacks records. Audit trail records chargeback_issued event. Supplier receives email notification via SES. GDPR Erasure Workflow: EU supplier contact submits erasure request via compliance team admin panel. Compliance admin runs GDPR erasure procedure for supplier_id: UPDATE suppliers SET company_name='GDPR_ERASED', contact_email='erased_{id}@gdpr.invalid', contact_phone='ERASED', anonymized_at=NOW() WHERE id=?. INSERT into gdpr_erasure_requests (supplier_id, requested_at, processed_at, processed_by). All chargeback, violation, scorecard, and audit_trail records referencing supplier_id UUID are retained unchanged for SOX. Scorecard Computation: nightly scheduled job queries compliance_violations for all suppliers in the past 90 days, groups by supplier_id and violation_category, applies category weight matrix, computes weighted_score, upserts scorecards row, updates supplier routing_guide_tier.
target_output.data_model: suppliers (id UUID PK, company_name TEXT, okta_org_id TEXT, contact_email TEXT, contact_phone TEXT, routing_guide_tier TEXT, status TEXT, anonymized_at DATETIME). routing_guide_submissions (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, requirement_category TEXT, document_s3_keys JSONB, submitted_at, reviewed_at, status TEXT). compliance_violations (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, violation_category TEXT, freight_invoice_id TEXT, violation_amount NUMERIC(10,2), detected_at). chargebacks (id UUID PK, violation_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, amount NUMERIC(10,2), status TEXT, issued_at, resolved_at). chargeback_disputes (id UUID PK, chargeback_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, dispute_reason TEXT, counter_doc_s3_keys JSONB, submitted_at, resolution_deadline, resolved_by). scorecards (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, violation_count_by_category JSONB, weighted_score NUMERIC(5,2), tier TEXT, computed_at). gdpr_erasure_requests (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, requested_at, processed_at, processed_by TEXT). audit_trail (id UUID PK, event_type TEXT, supplier_id UUID, user_id UUID, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, before_value JSONB, after_value JSONB, ip_address INET, created_at). -- RLS USING policy on all supplier-scoped tables: USING (supplier_id = current_setting('app.current_supplier_id')::uuid)
target_output.api_design: RESTful under /api/v1. Supplier-facing: GET /submissions (paginated, cursor), POST /submissions (multipart upload), GET /chargebacks (filtered by status), POST /chargebacks/{id}/dispute (submit dispute with documents), GET /scorecard (current supplier scorecard). Admin-facing (compliance_analyst/admin role): GET /admin/suppliers, GET /admin/violations, POST /admin/chargebacks (issue chargeback), PATCH /admin/chargebacks/{id}/resolve, GET /admin/scorecards, GET /admin/audit-report?from=&to= (SOX export). Internal: POST /internal/invoice-match (sap-rfc-sidecar adapter endpoint). All responses include X-Request-ID header for New Relic correlation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS: USING (supplier_id = current_setting('app.current_supplier_id')::uuid) on routing_guide_submissions, compliance_violations, chargebacks, chargeback_disputes, scorecards. Fastify onRequest hook: await db.execute(sql'SELECT set_config($1, $2, true)', ['app.current_supplier_id', jwt.supplierId]). BYPASSRLS not granted to any application role. Okta SAML 2.0: supplier SSO with custom claim app.supplier_id populated via SAML attribute mapping. JWT validation via Okta JWKS endpoint cached in Redis with 10-minute refresh. mTLS not required (Okta SAML provides equivalent federation isolation). AWS WAF: rate limit 200 req/min per IP, SQL injection and XSS rulesets. Audit trail: INSERT-only app_audit_role, no UPDATE/DELETE privileges. SOX report: query audit_trail for chargeback_issued and chargeback_resolved events in review period, exported as CSV. GDPR: soft-delete anonymization preserving supplier_id and all financial records; erasure tracked in gdpr_erasure_requests.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ESLint + TypeScript check, Vitest (80% coverage gate), Drizzle migration dry-run against staging RDS, Docker build and ECR push for api-service and sap-rfc-sidecar images, ECS rolling update (50% minimum healthy). Environments: dev (single task, shared RDS dev instance), staging (multi-AZ RDS, 2 ECS tasks), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-4 ECS tasks on CPU 70%, WAF enabled). Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for RDS password, Okta client secret, SAP JCo credentials. RDS password rotation: 30-day automated rotation with ECS task restart on rotation event via EventBridge. Database migrations: Drizzle Kit applied via ECS one-off task on each deploy. S3 VPC endpoint: document uploads route via VPC endpoint not public internet.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM: Fastify plugin traces all API requests with supplier_id and request_id attributes. Custom metrics: chargeback_issuance_rate (count/min), dispute_submission_rate (count/min), scorecard_computation_duration_ms (p99), sap_rfc_idempotency_hit_rate (%), rls_policy_violation_count (alert on any non-zero). PagerDuty: error rate >0.5% triggers P2, p99 latency >3s triggers P2, rls_policy_violation_count >0 triggers P1. Structured JSON logs with supplier_id, chargeback_id, and request_id correlation fields written to CloudWatch Logs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate api-service 2-4 tasks: ~$400/month. SAP sidecar 2 tasks: ~$200/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$180/month. ElastiCache Redis t3.micro: ~$40/month. CloudFront + WAF: ~$100/month. Total: ~$1,000/month for 50-supplier pilot. At full rollout (500 suppliers), ECS auto-scaling adds tasks on CPU pressure; RDS upgrades to db.r6g.large at ~$450/month. PostgreSQL RLS has negligible query overhead measured at <2ms per query in load tests.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Okta SAML SSO, supplier registration, routing guide submission with S3 upload, manual chargeback issuance by compliance team, basic scorecard display, RLS enforcement, audit_trail, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SAP RFC sidecar with Redis idempotency integration, ERP invoice matching in chargeback issuance workflow, chargeback dispute workflow with resolution SLA countdown, admin analytics dashboard. Phase 3 (this round delivery, 4 weeks): GDPR soft-delete anonymization with gdpr_erasure_requests tracking, SOX audit report export from audit_trail, opossum circuit breaker configuration for SAP sidecar, New Relic custom metrics and PagerDuty escalation rules, production WAF tuning.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both Vue 3 frontend and Fastify backend. Drizzle ORM typed queries; no raw SQL except for set_config RLS injection. Every Drizzle query on supplier-scoped tables has an integration test asserting RLS enforcement. Vitest 80% coverage gate enforced in CI. SAP sidecar Java code has JUnit tests with mocked JCo responses. GDPR erasure procedure has integration test verifying anonymized_at set and financial records intact. PR requires 2 approvals for any change touching RLS policies or audit_trail schema.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SAP JCo library availability on Java 21 ECS container image requires SAP SDK license; non-licensed environments cannot build the sidecar. Mitigation: maintain a mock RFC adapter in the sidecar for non-production environments. Risk: Scorecard computation nightly job runtime grows with supplier count; at 10,000 suppliers with 90-day windows the query may exceed 30 seconds. Mitigation: partition scorecard computation into supplier batches with configurable batch size. Tradeoff: soft-delete GDPR anonymization over hard delete adds permanent phantom records to the suppliers table; mitigated by filtering anonymized_at IS NOT NULL in all active supplier queries. Tradeoff: Okta SAML over custom JWT reduces implementation complexity but binds the platform to Okta for supplier federation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GDPR erasure conflict with SOX retention resolved via soft-delete anonymization. SAP RFC idempotency resolved via Redis key store with 24h TTL. Confirmed Okta SAML with custom supplier_id claim over Azure AD B2C for supplier federation flexibility. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer filtering as the enforcement mechanism.

metadata.notes: Round 3 vendor portal revision closing SAP idempotency and GDPR/SOX retention conflict with soft-delete anonymization pattern.

profile.domain: Customs Brokerage SaaS Marketing Landing Page

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for a customs brokerage SaaS product targeting SME importers, showcasing trade compliance features, CTPAT certification, and a lead capture form for demo scheduling
project_goal.rationale: Top-of-funnel marketing page driving demo requests for the SaaS product

target_users.value: SME importers and supply chain managers evaluating customs compliance SaaS solutions
target_users.rationale: Primary audience for customs brokerage SaaS demo requests

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with no server-side runtime or database

capabilities.rationale: Static HTML/CSS with Google Analytics; no backend or auth

complexity_level.rationale: Static marketing page with form submission to third-party CRM; minimal logic

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored by the page; lead data handled by connected CRM

data_sensitivity.rationale: Page collects only name and email via third-party form service; no PII stored on this platform

external_exposure.rationale: Marketing page must be publicly accessible without authentication

access_model.value: No authentication required; publicly accessible static page
access_model.rationale: Marketing landing page has no access control

feature_scope.value: Hero section with CTPAT certification badge, trade compliance feature highlights, social proof testimonials, demo request form with CRM integration, GDPR cookie consent banner for EU visitors
feature_scope.rationale: Standard marketing landing page feature set

mvp_scope.value: Hero section, 3 feature cards, demo request form posting to HubSpot, Google Analytics 4 page tracking
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for product launch campaign

security_baseline.value: Vercel Edge Network CDN, HTTPS enforced, no cookies beyond GDPR-consented analytics, Content Security Policy header
security_baseline.rationale: Static page security baseline; no server-side processing

frontend_stack.rationale: Astro produces zero-JS static output by default; Tailwind for utility-first styling

backend_stack.value: none; static site with no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Landing page requires no backend; form submission via HubSpot embed

data_platform.value: none; no database
data_platform.rationale: Static page with no data persistence; lead data flows to HubSpot CRM

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with automatic HTTPS
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config static site hosting with global CDN for landing page performance

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data stored by the landing page; GDPR cookie consent required for GA4 analytics cookies for EU visitors
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Analytics cookie requires GDPR consent; no other data retention

future_scope.value: A/B testing via Vercel Edge Middleware; additional product feature sections as SaaS product expands
future_scope.rationale: Conversion optimization roadmap

constraints.rationale: SEO and conversion rate requirements for paid ad campaigns

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for page views, form submission conversion events, and UTM campaign tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: Marketing analytics for campaign ROI measurement

execution_preference.value: Single developer iteration; GitHub Actions for CI and Vercel preview deployments on PR
execution_preference.rationale: Small team; fast iteration with Vercel preview URLs for stakeholder review

project.goal: Marketing landing page for customs SaaS with CTPAT badge and demo lead capture
project.target_users: SME importers evaluating customs compliance software

frontend.rendering: static output, zero client-side JS on initial render
frontend.form: HubSpot embedded form or HubSpot Forms API

security.cookies: GDPR consent gate for GA4 analytics cookies

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Astro build and Lighthouse CI check
devops.environments: preview per PR branch, production on main

open_questions.cms: Headless CMS for marketing content updates under evaluation

reasoner_reviews.product: The page serves a clear conversion goal: drive demo requests from SME importers. The CTPAT certification badge and trade compliance feature highlights are the right trust signals for this audience. The HubSpot form integration for lead capture is standard and appropriate. The GDPR consent banner must gate GA4 cookie loading, not just display a notice.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: For a static landing page on Vercel the architecture is appropriately minimal. Astro's static output is the right choice for Lighthouse performance goals. The remaining gap from round 2 is the headless CMS decision for allowing marketing to update compliance certification badges and feature copy without developer deployments. I recommend adding a Contentful free-tier integration with Astro's content collections for managing the certification badges and feature cards. This resolves the open_questions CMS item.
reasoner_reviews.security: Content Security Policy header must explicitly allowlist the HubSpot forms script origin (js.hsforms.net) and GA4 script origin (www.googletagmanager.com). The round 2 plan had a generic CSP placeholder. GDPR consent must use a compliant CMP (e.g., Cookiebot or cookie-consent npm package) that blocks GA4 script loading until consent is given, not just a banner that loads analytics regardless.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 90+ is achievable with Astro static output. Verify that the HubSpot form embed script does not block initial render; use async defer loading or replace with a native Astro form posting to HubSpot Forms API to eliminate the third-party script dependency.
reasoner_reviews.critic: This plan is a round 3 revision of a simple landing page but has been over-engineered in previous rounds. This round the plan incorrectly includes a Node.js Express backend API for form submission and a PostgreSQL database for lead storage. This violates the contract: backend_stack is 'none' and data_platform is 'none'. The plan must be corrected to remove the backend server and database, using HubSpot directly for lead capture.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages/index.astro as the single page. src/components/ contains HeroSection.astro, FeatureCard.astro, ComplianceBadge.astro (CTPAT + C-TPAT certification badge display), TestimonialBlock.astro, DemoRequestForm.astro, CookieConsentBanner.astro. src/content/features/ Contentful content collection for feature cards and certification badges.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single page: hero with CTPAT badge, 3 feature cards (HTS classification, duty drawback automation, entry filing tracking), testimonials from SME importer case studies, demo request form, footer with privacy policy link. Form submission posts to HubSpot Forms API v3 endpoint via Astro API route (edge function) to keep HubSpot portal ID server-side.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro static output with zero client-side JavaScript on initial render. Google Fonts replaced with system font stack. Images served as WebP with explicit width and height attributes to prevent layout shift. HubSpot form script loaded async defer only after GDPR consent granted. Target Lighthouse performance 95+.
frontend.frontend_risks: HubSpot embed script is a third-party performance risk; mitigated by native form with Astro API route calling HubSpot Forms API server-side.

security.auth_design: No authentication; public page.
security.abuse_prevention: HubSpot Forms API rate limiting for demo request form submissions. Astro API route for form POST validates honeypot field and required fields before forwarding to HubSpot.
security.privacy_controls: Cookiebot CMP integration gates GA4 gtag.js script injection behind cookie consent. CSP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://consent.cookiebot.com; form-action 'self'; img-src 'self' data: https:
security.security_risks: Third-party script supply chain risk from GA4 and Cookiebot; pinned to specific script versions in Astro layout.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network: static Astro build output deployed to Vercel CDN. Astro API route for form submission deployed as Vercel Edge Function (no Node.js server). Preview deployments auto-created per PR branch for stakeholder review.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: (1) astro check TypeScript validation, (2) astro build, (3) Lighthouse CI against built output asserting performance >90 and accessibility >90, (4) Vercel preview deploy on PR, (5) Vercel production deploy on main merge.

target_output.title: Customs Brokerage SaaS Landing Page v3
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro marketing landing page deployed to Vercel Edge Network for a customs brokerage SaaS product targeting SME importers. The page highlights CTPAT certification, HTS classification automation, and duty drawback features. Demo requests are captured via a native Astro form posting to HubSpot Forms API v3 through an Astro API route deployed as a Vercel Edge Function, with no server-side database or backend runtime. Google Analytics 4 is gated behind a Cookiebot GDPR consent banner for EU visitors. Content for certification badges and feature cards is managed via a Contentful content collection integration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 builds a fully static HTML/CSS site with zero client-side JavaScript on initial render, deployed to Vercel Edge Network CDN across global PoPs. The demo request form POSTs to a single Astro API route at /api/demo-request which runs as a Vercel Edge Function; it validates the honeypot field and required inputs, then calls the HubSpot Forms API v3 endpoint with the lead data. No Express server, no Node.js process, and no database are involved. Certification badge content and feature card copy are fetched from Contentful at build time via Astro content collections, enabling marketing updates through a Contentful webhook that triggers a Vercel redeploy without developer intervention. GA4 tracking script is injected into the page only after the visitor grants cookie consent via the Cookiebot CMP banner.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with Tailwind CSS, zero client-side JS by default, TypeScript component props. CMS: Contentful free tier with Astro content collections SDK. Form: native Astro API route calling HubSpot Forms API v3 via fetch. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with gtag.js gated by Cookiebot CMP. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with automatic HTTPS and preview deployments. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI. No backend runtime. No database.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: headline, subheadline, CTPAT certification badge from Contentful, primary CTA button scrolling to demo form. Feature Cards: 3 cards (HTS code lookup automation, duty drawback claim tracking, AES filing integration) with icons and copy managed in Contentful. Compliance Trust Section: CTPAT, C-TPAT, ISA certification badge grid sourced from Contentful compliance_certifications collection. Testimonial Block: 2 SME importer testimonials with company name and industry. Demo Request Form: name, company, email, importer-of-record country, monthly shipment volume fields; honeypot field for spam prevention; posts to /api/demo-request Vercel Edge Function. Cookie Consent Banner: Cookiebot CMP blocking GA4 loading until consent granted.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network: global CDN serving pre-built Astro HTML/CSS/WebP assets. Vercel Edge Function: /api/demo-request route validating form data and calling HubSpot Forms API v3. Contentful CMS: stores product_features, compliance_certifications, and demo_page_copy content types fetched at build time. HubSpot Forms API: receives lead data from Edge Function and creates contact in HubSpot CRM with UTM source attributes. Cookiebot CMP: manages GDPR cookie consent and conditionally injects GA4 script. Google Analytics 4: page view and form_submission conversion event tracking post-consent. GitHub Actions: CI pipeline with Astro build and Lighthouse CI assertions.
target_output.workflows: Demo Request Submission: visitor fills out form fields (name, company, email, country, shipment volume), form POST goes to /api/demo-request Vercel Edge Function, Edge Function checks honeypot field is empty and required fields are present, calls HubSpot Forms API v3 POST https://api.hsforms.com/submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formId} with field values and UTM parameters extracted from cookie, HubSpot creates contact and triggers CRM email sequence, Edge Function returns 200 and form displays success message. Content Update Flow: marketing manager edits a compliance certification badge in Contentful, publishes the entry, Contentful webhook fires POST to Vercel deploy hook URL, Vercel triggers new Astro build fetching updated Contentful content, new static HTML deployed to Edge Network within 2 minutes.
target_output.data_model: No database. Lead data: submitted via HubSpot Forms API and stored as HubSpot CRM contacts with properties name, email, company, hs_analytics_source (UTM), country, monthly_shipment_volume. Contentful content types: product_features (title, description, icon_name, display_order), compliance_certifications (cert_name, badge_image_url, cert_description), demo_page_copy (hero_headline, hero_subheadline, cta_text). No PostgreSQL tables. No server-side data persistence.
target_output.api_design: Single Astro API route: POST /api/demo-request (Vercel Edge Function). Request body: {name: string, company: string, email: string, country: string, shipment_volume: string, _honeypot: string (must be empty), utm_source?: string, utm_campaign?: string}. Validates inputs, forwards to HubSpot Forms API v3. Returns {success: true} or {error: string}. No other API endpoints. HubSpot Forms API called server-side to protect portalId. No authentication required on the Edge Function; honeypot and input validation provide abuse resistance.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel for all requests. Content Security Policy header in vercel.json: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{vercel-nonce}' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://consent.cookiebot.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; form-action 'self'; connect-src 'self' https://api.hsforms.com. GDPR: Cookiebot CMP with category consent; analytics cookie category blocked until visitor grants consent; Cookiebot injects GA4 gtag.js only after consent. No cookies set by the Astro application itself. Privacy policy page linked in footer describing HubSpot lead data processing. No PII stored on Vercel infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository. GitHub Actions CI: (1) astro check for TypeScript errors, (2) astro build, (3) Lighthouse CI against built dist/ directory asserting performance >=90 and accessibility >=90, (4) Vercel preview deployment auto-created per PR (Vercel GitHub integration). Production deploy: Vercel auto-deploys main branch on merge. Contentful webhook configured to POST to Vercel deploy hook URL on publish events for product_features and compliance_certifications content types triggering automatic content refresh builds. HubSpot portal ID stored as Vercel environment variable HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID and HUBSPOT_FORM_ID; not exposed to client-side code. No ECS Fargate, no containers, no server infrastructure.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 custom events: page_view (auto), form_submission (custom event on demo request success), cta_click (hero button). UTM parameters tracked via HubSpot contact source attribution. Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) monitoring. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions asserts performance and accessibility scores. No APM or PagerDuty; static site has no server-side error surface.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby tier: free for static deployments with Edge Functions included. Contentful free tier: sufficient for 3 content types. HubSpot free CRM: included in HubSpot starter. Google Analytics 4: free. Total infrastructure cost: $0/month for expected traffic volume. Vercel Pro tier at $20/month if team preview deployments or bandwidth limits are reached. Astro static output scales globally via Vercel Edge CDN without capacity planning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Astro project setup with Tailwind CSS, Hero section with CTPAT badge, 3 hardcoded feature cards, demo request form with HubSpot Forms API Edge Function, Cookiebot GDPR consent, GA4 integration, Vercel deployment, Lighthouse CI gate. Phase 2 CMS Integration (1 week): Contentful content types for features and certification badges, Astro content collection integration, Contentful webhook Vercel redeploy hook, marketing documentation. Phase 3 Optimization (ongoing): A/B testing via Vercel Edge Middleware on hero headline copy, additional testimonials, UTM campaign tracking expansion.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro TypeScript strict mode. No inline JavaScript in .astro files except deferred non-blocking scripts. All images as WebP with explicit dimensions. Tailwind purge configured to minimize CSS bundle. HubSpot portal ID and form ID injected via Vercel environment variables only; never committed to repository. Contentful API key stored as Vercel environment variable. Lighthouse CI must pass on every PR before merge. Single developer workflow with Vercel preview URLs for stakeholder review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HubSpot Forms API rate limiting under high-traffic ad campaigns. Mitigation: Vercel Edge Function adds 1-second debounce on form submit button client-side. Risk: Contentful webhook latency means certification badge updates take up to 2 minutes to appear on production. Mitigation: acceptable for marketing content; document expected delay. Tradeoff: native Astro form with Edge Function over HubSpot embed script eliminates third-party JS blocking risk at the cost of maintaining the Edge Function integration. Tradeoff: Contentful free tier limits to 25,000 API calls/month; acceptable for a single landing page with infrequent builds.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CMS choice resolved: Contentful free tier with Astro content collections. HubSpot selected for lead CRM over Mailchimp for richer sales qualification fields. Confirmed no backend server and no database per landing_page project class constraints. GDPR consent via Cookiebot CMP confirmed over cookie-consent npm package for enterprise-grade consent logging.

profile.domain: Last-Mile Delivery Failure Risk ML Research Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype predicting last-mile delivery failure risk using ML on historical delivery attempt outcomes, address quality scores, time-window compliance patterns, and driver behavior signals to validate feature importance before productionizing a courier risk model
project_goal.rationale: Validates ML approach and feature set before committing to production infrastructure investment

target_users.value: Logistics research team and data scientists evaluating feature importance and model candidates for a production courier risk scoring system
target_users.rationale: Internal research use; no external consumers

project_class.rationale: Experimental ML system for research validation; not a production service

capabilities.rationale: Feature engineering pipeline, model training batch jobs, MLflow experiment tracking, FastAPI for prediction endpoint

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-feature ML pipeline with experiment tracking; not high-scale production system

risk_level.rationale: Internal research system with no external exposure; operational risk is low

data_sensitivity.rationale: Historical delivery data is internal operational data; no PII or financial data in feature set

external_exposure.rationale: Research prototype accessible only within GCP project; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT for Cloud Run API endpoint; research team authenticated via GCP IAM for direct DuckDB and MLflow access
access_model.rationale: Lightweight auth appropriate for internal research tool

feature_scope.value: Feature engineering pipeline computing delivery attempt features, gradient boosting classifier training with configurable feature subsets, MLflow experiment tracking with feature importance charts, FastAPI prediction endpoint for model evaluation, DuckDB feature store
feature_scope.rationale: Full research validation feature set

mvp_scope.value: Feature engineering pipeline for 5 core features, baseline gradient boosting model training, MLflow experiment logging, basic prediction endpoint
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for initial model evaluation meeting

security_baseline.value: GCP IAM for Cloud Run service account; no public Cloud Run endpoint; VPC Service Controls; custom JWT for API access
security_baseline.rationale: Internal research system baseline security

frontend_stack.value: none; research prototype with no web UI; MLflow UI accessed directly
frontend_stack.rationale: No web frontend; researchers access MLflow tracking UI and FastAPI /docs endpoint

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for prediction API
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI provides typed prediction endpoint; Python ML ecosystem for scikit-learn and LightGBM

data_platform.value: DuckDB for local feature store and training dataset materialization
data_platform.rationale: DuckDB handles columnar analytics on historical delivery data without infrastructure dependencies

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for prediction API; Cloud Storage for model artifacts and training data
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run zero-cost-at-rest for intermittent research API usage; GCS for durable artifact storage

privacy_retention_policy.value: Historical delivery data retained for research duration; no personal courier or customer data in feature set
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Internal operational data used in anonymized aggregate form

future_scope.value: Productionize winning model as a real-time risk scoring API integrated with the courier dispatch system
future_scope.rationale: Research prototype is the first step toward production risk scoring capability

constraints.value: Must run on GCP; research team has no MLOps infrastructure; prototype must be reproducible from a single requirements.txt and config file
constraints.rationale: Reproducibility required for research team handoff to production engineering team

observability_baseline.value: MLflow experiment tracking for all model runs; structured logging to Cloud Logging for pipeline execution
observability_baseline.rationale: MLflow is the primary research observability tool; Cloud Logging for operational pipeline monitoring

execution_preference.value: Iterative research sprints; GitHub Actions CI for code quality; no strict delivery timeline
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype pace driven by experiment results not delivery deadlines

llm_integration.value: LLM used for automated feature importance narration: after each experiment run, GPT-4o generates a natural language summary of the top-10 feature importances and any notable correlations for the research log
llm_integration.rationale: Capabilities includes ai_llm; LLM accelerates researcher interpretation of feature importance outputs across many experiment runs

project.goal: Validate ML feature set and model approach for last-mile delivery failure risk prediction

backend.ml_stack: LightGBM 4, scikit-learn 1.5, Optuna for hyperparameter tuning

security.auth: Custom JWT for Cloud Run API
security.network: No public Cloud Run endpoint; VPC Service Controls
security.iam: Cloud Run service account with GCS read/write

data.db: DuckDB 0.10 for feature store and training dataset
data.artifact_store: GCS bucket for model artifacts and experiment data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with ruff, pytest, and mypy

constraints.reproducibility: single requirements.txt and config.yaml for full pipeline reproduction

open_questions.feature_drift: Strategy for detecting feature distribution drift between training data vintage and live dispatch data under evaluation

reasoner_reviews.product: The core research question is well-scoped: validate which of the candidate features (geocoding confidence, time-window adherence, driver attempt rate, address unit completeness, historical route failure rate) have predictive power for delivery failure. The MLflow experiment structure with tagged feature subsets is the right approach for systematic feature ablation. The round 1 gap was the LLM feature importance narration integration; it was specified in the contract but not implemented in the plan.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The DuckDB feature engineering pipeline is architecturally sound for this research scale. The LightGBM training job should use Optuna for hyperparameter search with MLflow autolog to capture all trial metrics. The FastAPI prediction endpoint is correctly designed for model evaluation but needs the LLM narration integration: after a training run completes, the pipeline should call GPT-4o with the sorted feature importance JSON and receive a paragraph summarizing the model's key predictors for the research log. The feature_drift open question is worth flagging but is lower priority than closing the LLM integration gap.
reasoner_reviews.security: Custom JWT for Cloud Run is appropriate for internal research. Confirm that the OpenAI API key for GPT-4o narration is stored in GCP Secret Manager and loaded at runtime, not committed to the repository. Cloud Run service account should have minimal IAM: roles/storage.objectAdmin on the research GCS bucket only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Reproducibility requirement is satisfied by the config.yaml approach for all pipeline parameters. The DuckDB database file path should be configurable so researchers can point the pipeline at different historical data snapshots. MLflow tracking URI should also be configurable for team members running experiments locally versus on Cloud Run.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan had strong feature engineering and training pipeline design but completely omitted the LLM narration step despite it being explicitly required in llm_integration contract field. This round must add the GPT-4o narration step to the post-training pipeline and document the prompt structure and output format.

backend.service_design: Python package with three modules: feature_pipeline (DuckDB-based feature engineering from raw delivery_attempts CSV), training_pipeline (LightGBM training with Optuna search and MLflow autolog), prediction_api (FastAPI app serving predictions from loaded model artifact). A pipeline runner script orchestrates feature_pipeline then training_pipeline and optionally calls the llm_narration module to generate a research summary after training completes.
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI routes: POST /predict (accepts delivery_attempt JSON, returns failure_probability and top_features), GET /model-info (returns loaded model version, training date, feature list), GET /health. No authentication on /health; custom JWT required for /predict and /model-info.
backend.llm_integration_design: Post-training narration step: after LightGBM model training and MLflow logging, the llm_narration module calls OpenAI GPT-4o via the openai Python SDK with a structured prompt: 'You are a logistics research analyst. Given the following feature importances from a last-mile delivery failure risk model, write a 3-paragraph research summary explaining which features are most predictive, any surprising correlations, and recommended next experiments. Feature importances: {sorted_importance_json}'. The response is logged to MLflow as a text artifact research_narrative.txt and appended to the experiment's research_log.md file in GCS.
backend.failure_handling: DuckDB query failures in feature_pipeline emit structured error logs to Cloud Logging with feature_name and error_reason fields. Training pipeline catches LightGBM convergence warnings and logs them as MLflow tags. GPT-4o API failures are non-blocking: llm_narration catches openai.APIError, logs the failure to Cloud Logging, and continues without halting the experiment run.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT: research team generates tokens via a helper script using a shared HMAC-SHA256 secret stored in GCP Secret Manager path /research/jwt-secret. FastAPI validates JWT on /predict and /model-info routes using python-jose.
security.secrets_management: OpenAI API key stored in GCP Secret Manager path /research/openai-api-key, loaded at Cloud Run startup via Secret Manager environment variable injection. No API keys in config.yaml or requirements.txt.
security.security_risks: Shared HMAC JWT secret is a research-grade control; insufficient for production. Documented in prototype scope limitations as requiring upgrade to per-user tokens before productionization.

data.entities: delivery_attempts DuckDB table: attempt_id VARCHAR PK, tracking_number, route_id, driver_id, scheduled_window_start TIMESTAMP, scheduled_window_end TIMESTAMP, actual_attempt_ts TIMESTAMP, outcome ENUM success/failed/rescheduled, failure_reason TEXT, address_geocoding_confidence FLOAT, address_unit_present BOOLEAN, historical_route_failure_rate FLOAT. feature_sets DuckDB view: computed features per delivery_attempt including time_window_deviation_minutes, geocoding_confidence_bucket ENUM high/medium/low, driver_attempt_rate_30d FLOAT (attempts per day rolling 30 days), unit_completeness_score INT. model_experiments MLflow tracking: run_id, feature_subset_tag, n_estimators, learning_rate, auc_roc, log_loss, feature_importances JSON.
data.schema_notes: DuckDB feature_sets view recomputed on each pipeline run from raw delivery_attempts table. Training dataset materialized as DuckDB Parquet export to GCS for reproducibility.
data.analytics_events: MLflow logged metrics per run: auc_roc, log_loss, precision_at_10pct, recall_at_10pct, top_feature_name (string tag). Feature importance chart logged as MLflow artifact plotly_feature_importance.html.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service for FastAPI prediction API: 1 instance minimum 0, max 3, 1 vCPU/2GB, ingress internal-only. GCS bucket research-delivery-risk/ with subdirectories: /data (raw delivery_attempts CSV), /models (LightGBM .pkl artifacts), /experiments (feature set Parquet exports, research_log.md per experiment). MLflow tracking server on Cloud Run (separate service) with GCS backend store.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest unit tests for feature engineering functions and prediction endpoint, Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy via gcloud CLI. No production approval gate; research environment only.
devops.observability_stack: MLflow: all experiment metrics, parameters, and artifacts. Cloud Logging: structured JSON pipeline execution logs with run_id, feature_count, training_duration_s, narration_status. No APM; research prototype does not require SLA monitoring.
