project.goal: AI-assisted sanctions screening and HS classification for import/export compliance
project.target_users: Licensed customs officers and compliance specialists

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for review session state

backend.llm: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via azure-openai SDK

security.auth: Azure AD with RBAC via MSAL
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest via Azure Disk Encryption, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.cache: Redis 7 for hold queue and list lookup cache

reasoner_reviews.product: The hold resolution workflow is the regulatory linchpin. Customs officers must be able to document their resolution rationale in structured form (not free text) to satisfy CBP examination requirements. The LLM classification output must include cited HS schedule chapter headings in the justification to be usable in audit exports.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The two-round remaining issues center on LLM prompt PII stripping reliability and hold queue performance under transaction volume spikes. The PII stripping preprocessor must be unit-tested against known entity patterns. The hold queue Redis implementation needs a documented processing guarantee: at-least-once with idempotency key prevents duplicate hold creation on retry.
reasoner_reviews.security: Immutable audit logs are non-negotiable for OFAC examination. PostgreSQL append-only audit table with row-level security preventing DELETE and UPDATE by application role is the right approach. LLM response caching by sanitized_product_hash in Redis must not cache results that include counterparty data leaked through imperfect sanitization.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour BIS list sync SLA is achievable with a scheduled Azure Function polling the OFAC XML feed and BIS CSV endpoint hourly. The sync job must complete an atomic swap of the restricted_entity_lists table to avoid partial list states during screening.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 improved the hold workflow but the LLM HS classification confidence routing threshold (0.85) is hardcoded in the plan. It should be a database-configurable parameter so compliance admins can tighten it before regulatory examinations without a code deployment.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with server components for compliance dashboard; client components for interactive hold review and classification editor
frontend.pages_and_flows: ComplianceQueue (pending holds list), HoldReviewPage (hold detail with screening match evidence and LLM classification card), ClassificationEditorPage (manual HS code override with justification), AdminPanel (list sync status, config thresholds), AuditExportPage (date-range report generator)
frontend.state_management: React Query for hold queue, screening results, and audit data; Zustand for active review session state including unsaved justification draft
frontend.accessibility_notes: Hold resolution buttons must have explicit aria-labels citing hold ID; compliance queue must be keyboard navigable for operators who do not use mouse

backend.service_design: FastAPI with four domain routers: /screening, /classification, /holds, /admin; async SQLAlchemy for all DB operations; Celery workers for batch screening and list sync jobs
backend.llm_integration_design: PII stripper preprocessor removes counterparty names and addresses using regex + spaCy NER before constructing GPT-4o prompt; prompt template requests HS chapter, heading, subheading, and reasoning with citation; response parsed to ClassificationResult with confidence float; results cached in Redis by sanitized_product_hash with 24h TTL
backend.business_modules: ScreeningEngine: fuzzy Levenshtein match on party names against restricted_entity_lists with configurable match_threshold; HoldManager: hold lifecycle state machine (open/pending_review/resolved/escalated); ListSyncJob: atomic upsert of OFAC/BIS records with sync_run audit entry
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat: hourly OFAC/BIS list sync, nightly audit log archival to Azure Blob, daily hold aging report

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL token validation on every FastAPI request; RBAC claims extracted from token for route-level permission checks
security.authorization_model: compliance_officer: read holds, submit resolution; customs_specialist: trigger classification, approve HS code; admin: manage list sync config, export audit logs; no role can DELETE from audit_log or screening_results
security.audit_and_logging_controls: PostgreSQL audit_log table with row-level security DENY DELETE/UPDATE for app_user role; every screening decision, hold state change, and LLM classification stored with user_id, timestamp, and action_payload
security.privacy_controls: PII stripping enforced in ScreeningEngine before any LLM prompt construction; LLM prompt text stored in prompt_log with 90-day retention and automatic purge via pg_cron job

data.entities: trade_transactions (id, importer_id, exporter_id, goods_description_sanitized, hs_code, transaction_date, status), screening_results (id, transaction_id, list_type ENUM ofac/bis, matched_entry_id, match_score, screened_at, screened_by), compliance_holds (id, transaction_id, hold_type, status ENUM open/pending_review/resolved/escalated, created_at, resolved_by, resolution_justification, resolution_at), hs_classifications (id, transaction_id, sanitized_description, recommended_hs_code, confidence, reasoning_text, model_version, reviewed_by, override_hs_code), restricted_entity_lists (id, list_type, entity_name, aliases_json, country, program, effective_date, sync_run_id), audit_log (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, payload_json, ip_address, ts)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL with zone-redundant HA; Redis 7 for hold queue and classification cache; Azure Blob for audit log archives beyond 1 year
data.retention_and_deletion: screening_results and audit_log retained 7 years via pg_cron archival to Azure Blob; prompt_log purged after 90 days; no hard DELETE on compliance records

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in Azure West Europe: api deployment (3 replicas, HPA on CPU 60%), celery-worker deployment (2 replicas), celery-beat deployment (1 replica); Azure Container Registry for images
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: lint + pytest (90% coverage gate) + Docker build + ACR push + AKS rolling deploy; separate pipeline for list sync Celery image; SOC 2 evidence package generated on each release
devops.observability_stack: Azure Application Insights for APM; structured JSON logs via structlog; custom metrics: hold queue depth, list sync lag seconds, LLM classification latency p95, PII stripper false-negative alert rate
devops.rollback_strategy: AKS rollout undo on failed health probe; database migrations via Alembic with explicit down migrations tested in CI

ISS-001.title: LLM confidence threshold hardcoded; not admin-configurable
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan hardcodes 0.85 confidence threshold for human review routing in application code. Compliance admins cannot tighten it before regulatory examinations without a code deployment.

history.note: Critic identified configuration inflexibility
Still hardcoded in plan despite critic feedback

ISS-002.title: Hold queue idempotency not specified for retry scenarios
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan now documents idempotency_key on hold creation preventing duplicate holds on Celery task retry.

history.note: Architect identified missing idempotency guarantee
Hold creation POST documents idempotency_key header

ISS-003.title: Atomic list sync swap not specified; partial list state possible during screening
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 plan now uses a staging table approach: sync writes to restricted_entity_lists_staging, then atomic RENAME to replace live table in a single transaction.

history.note: Constraints reviewer identified partial list state risk
Atomic table swap documented in list sync job design

focus_issues.title: LLM confidence threshold hardcoded; not admin-configurable
focus_issues.detail: Compliance admins cannot tighten the LLM human-review routing threshold before regulatory examinations without a code deployment.

latest_recommendations: Move LLM confidence threshold to a compliance_config table row configurable via admin panel; cache in Redis with 5-minute TTL to avoid per-request DB reads
Add an audit event when an admin changes the threshold so the change is visible in regulatory examination exports

previous_plan.title: Trade Compliance Screening and HS Classification System v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: An AI-assisted trade compliance system on AKS that screens import/export transactions against OFAC SDN and BIS entity lists using fuzzy matching, classifies goods under HS codes via GPT-4o with PII-stripped prompts, and manages compliance holds with documented resolution workflows meeting OFAC 7-year record retention requirements.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI backend on AKS with Celery workers for async screening and list sync. PostgreSQL 16 holds all compliance records with append-only audit_log protected by row-level security. Redis caches hold queue and HS classification results. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o performs HS classification with PII-stripped product descriptions. Next.js 14 frontend provides compliance queue and hold review UI.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD RBAC gates hold resolution to licensed compliance_officer and customs_specialist roles. PostgreSQL audit_log has RLS DENY DELETE/UPDATE for app_user. LLM prompts stripped of counterparty PII. Hold creation uses idempotency_key. List sync uses atomic staging table swap. LLM confidence threshold hardcoded at 0.85 -- flagged for configurability fix in round 3.
previous_plan.data_model: trade_transactions, screening_results, compliance_holds, hs_classifications, restricted_entity_lists, audit_log, prompt_log tables.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the single remaining ISS-001 by moving the LLM confidence threshold from application code to a compliance_config database table with admin panel management and audit-logged changes. The architecture is otherwise production-ready. PostgreSQL RLS append-only audit log, atomic list swap, and GPT-4o PII-stripped pipeline are solid. Need to propagate the configurable threshold through the HS classification service and document the admin change audit event.

fix_report.action_taken: Added compliance_config table with key='hs_confidence_threshold', value NUMERIC, updated_by, updated_at columns. LLMClassificationService reads threshold from Redis cache (key: config:hs_confidence_threshold, 5-min TTL) falling back to DB on cache miss. Admin panel exposes a threshold input gated to admin role; on save, writes to compliance_config and emits audit_log entry with action='config_change', payload {key, old_value, new_value}. Documented in api_design as PATCH /admin/config/hs-confidence-threshold.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compliance admins can tighten the LLM human-review routing threshold via the admin panel without a code deployment, and every threshold change is captured in the 7-year audit log for regulatory examination.

target_output.title: Trade Compliance Screening and HS Classification System v3
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-assisted trade compliance platform deployed on AKS that screens import/export transactions against OFAC SDN and BIS entity lists via configurable fuzzy matching, classifies goods under HS codes using Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with PII-stripped product descriptions, and manages compliance holds through a documented resolution workflow with 7-year audit retention. The LLM human-review routing threshold is now admin-configurable via a compliance_config table, enabling compliance officers to tighten classification sensitivity before regulatory examinations without code deployments.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Next.js 14 SSR frontend on AKS serving the compliance queue and hold review UI; FastAPI backend cluster (3 replicas) exposing /screening, /classification, /holds, and /admin routers; Celery workers handling async batch screening, hourly OFAC/BIS list sync, and nightly audit archival. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL stores all compliance records with an append-only audit_log protected by row-level security denying DELETE/UPDATE for the application role. Redis 7 caches the hold queue, HS classification results by sanitized_product_hash, and the compliance_config threshold value with 5-minute TTL. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o performs HS code classification with product descriptions pre-processed by a PII stripping pipeline using spaCy NER before prompt construction. Azure AD MSAL enforces RBAC on every API request with licensed-personnel role gating on hold resolution actions.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2, Celery 5 with Redis broker, azure-openai SDK, spaCy 3.7 for NER PII stripping. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL with zone-redundant HA. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o in Azure West Europe region. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure DevOps, Azure Blob for audit archives, Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ScreeningEngine: fuzzy Levenshtein name matching against restricted_entity_lists with configurable match_threshold; hit generates compliance_hold with idempotency_key preventing duplicate creation on retry. LLMClassificationService: sanitizes product description via spaCy PII stripper, queries GPT-4o for HS chapter/heading/subheading with cited reasoning, returns ClassificationResult{hs_code, confidence, reasoning_text, model_version}; routes to human review if confidence < compliance_config[hs_confidence_threshold]. HoldReviewWorkflow: compliance_officer reviews hold evidence and submits structured resolution_justification; hold state transitions open->pending_review->resolved or escalated. ListSyncJob: hourly Celery task polls OFAC SDN XML and BIS CSV, writes to restricted_entity_lists_staging, executes atomic table swap in single transaction, records sync_run audit entry. AdminPanel: compliance admins manage match_threshold and hs_confidence_threshold via compliance_config table with threshold change audit logging. AuditExportService: generates structured CSV/PDF audit reports for configurable date ranges including all screening decisions and threshold change events.
target_output.system_components: AKS api Deployment (3 replicas): FastAPI serving /screening /classification /holds /admin; HPA on CPU 60% up to 8 replicas. AKS celery-worker Deployment (2 replicas): processes screening_task and classification_task queues. AKS celery-beat Deployment (1 replica): schedules hourly list sync and nightly archival. PostgreSQL 16 (Azure Database, zone-redundant): compliance_holds, screening_results, hs_classifications, trade_transactions, restricted_entity_lists, compliance_config, audit_log, prompt_log. Redis 7 (Azure Cache): hold queue, classification result cache by sanitized_product_hash, compliance_config threshold cache. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o endpoint (West Europe): HS classification LLM. spaCy NER preprocessor (embedded in api containers): PII stripping before prompt construction. Azure Blob Storage: audit log archives beyond 1-year PostgreSQL retention window. Azure Application Insights: APM, structured log aggregation, custom metrics dashboard.
target_output.workflows: Transaction Screening: trade_transaction submitted via POST /screening/transactions; ScreeningEngine queries restricted_entity_lists with Levenshtein distance <= match_threshold against importer and exporter names; hit creates compliance_hold with idempotency_key=sha256(transaction_id+list_type+matched_entry_id); screening_result stored; hold status set to open; compliance_officer notified via Azure Service Bus event. HS Classification: customs_specialist submits goods_description to POST /classification; spaCy NER strips counterparty entities from description; sanitized_description hashed and checked against Redis classification cache; on miss, GPT-4o prompt constructed with HS schedule instruction and sanitized description; response parsed to ClassificationResult; if confidence >= compliance_config[hs_confidence_threshold] (read from Redis cache), hs_classification stored with status auto_approved; if below threshold, routed to pending_review queue for customs_specialist manual verification. Hold Resolution: compliance_officer opens hold in HoldReviewPage; views screening match evidence with match_score and matched SDN/BIS entry; submits resolution_justification as structured form with fields: resolution_basis ENUM cleared/licensed/error, applicable_program, notes; hold status updated to resolved; audit_log entry written with full payload. Threshold Configuration: admin navigates to AdminPanel config section; inputs new hs_confidence_threshold value; PATCH /admin/config/hs-confidence-threshold validates value in range [0.5, 1.0]; writes to compliance_config table; invalidates Redis config cache key; audit_log entry written with action=config_change, old_value, new_value, admin user_id, timestamp.
target_output.data_model: trade_transactions (id UUID PK, importer_id, exporter_id, goods_description_sanitized TEXT, hs_code, transaction_date, status ENUM pending/screened/cleared/held, created_at). screening_results (id UUID PK, transaction_id FK, list_type ENUM ofac/bis, matched_entry_id FK, match_score NUMERIC, screened_at, screened_by). compliance_holds (id UUID PK, transaction_id FK, idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE, hold_type ENUM sanctions/classification, status ENUM open/pending_review/resolved/escalated, resolution_justification JSONB, resolution_basis ENUM cleared/licensed/error, resolved_by, resolution_at, created_at). hs_classifications (id UUID PK, transaction_id FK, sanitized_description TEXT, recommended_hs_code, confidence NUMERIC, reasoning_text TEXT, model_version, reviewed_by, override_hs_code, status ENUM auto_approved/pending_review/approved, created_at). restricted_entity_lists (id UUID PK, list_type ENUM ofac/bis, entity_name, aliases_json JSONB, country, program, effective_date, sync_run_id). compliance_config (key TEXT PK, value NUMERIC, updated_by, updated_at). audit_log (id BIGSERIAL PK, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id UUID, payload_json JSONB, ip_address, ts TIMESTAMPTZ) -- RLS DENY DELETE/UPDATE for app_user role. prompt_log (id UUID PK, classification_id FK, sanitized_prompt TEXT, response_text TEXT, created_at) -- pg_cron purge after 90 days.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI REST at /api/v1. POST /screening/transactions {transaction_id, importer_name, exporter_name, goods_description, transaction_date} -> 202 Accepted with screening_task_id. GET /holds?status=open&page=1 -> paginated compliance_holds list for queue. GET /holds/{hold_id} -> hold detail with screening match evidence. PATCH /holds/{hold_id}/resolve {resolution_basis, applicable_program, notes} -> 200 resolved hold (compliance_officer role required). POST /classification {transaction_id, goods_description} -> 202 Accepted with classification_task_id. GET /classification/{classification_id} -> ClassificationResult with hs_code, confidence, routing_decision. PATCH /classification/{classification_id}/approve {override_hs_code, justification} -> 200 (customs_specialist role). PATCH /admin/config/hs-confidence-threshold {value: 0.90} -> 200 (admin role; writes compliance_config and audit_log). GET /admin/audit-export?from=2025-01-01&to=2025-12-31 -> streaming CSV/PDF (admin role). All endpoints require Azure AD MSAL Bearer token.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MSAL enforces authentication on every request; RBAC claims gate hold resolution to compliance_officer/customs_specialist and config changes to admin role. PostgreSQL audit_log protected by row-level security policy DENY DELETE and UPDATE for app_user database role; only audit_insert_role (write-only) can INSERT. Append-only audit_log captures all screening decisions, hold state changes, HS classification overrides, and compliance_config threshold changes with user_id and timestamp for OFAC 7-year examination readiness. LLM prompts: spaCy NER removes counterparty names and addresses before GPT-4o prompt construction; sanitized_description stored, not original; prompt_log records prompt text with 90-day auto-purge. List sync atomicity: OFAC/BIS updates written to restricted_entity_lists_staging table; atomic RENAME in single transaction replaces live table; no partial list state visible during screening. Hold idempotency: compliance_holds.idempotency_key = sha256(transaction_id+list_type+matched_entry_id) has UNIQUE constraint; Celery retries receive 409 Conflict and skip re-creation. SOC 2 Type II: Azure DevOps pipeline generates evidence package on each release including test coverage report, SAST scan results, and deployment manifest.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster in Azure West Europe: api (3 replicas min, HPA to 8 on CPU 60%), celery-worker (2 replicas), celery-beat (1 replica). Azure Container Registry for all images. Azure DevOps pipeline: ruff lint + pytest (90% coverage gate) + safety dependency scan + Docker build + ACR push + AKS rolling deploy with health probe gate. Environments: dev (single replica, Azure OpenAI dev endpoint, PostgreSQL Flexible Server dev), staging (multi-replica, production data subset, full SOC 2 evidence run), production (zone-redundant PostgreSQL, Redis Premium tier, AKS 3-node pool). Database migrations via Alembic with explicit down migrations tested in CI staging run. Celery beat list sync SLA monitored with Azure Monitor alert on sync_lag_seconds > 14400 (4-hour SLA breach).
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights APM tracing on all FastAPI requests with operation_id correlation. Structured JSON logging via structlog with fields: user_id, action, entity_id, list_type, match_score, confidence, latency_ms. Custom metrics: hold_queue_depth (alert >100), list_sync_lag_seconds (alert >14400), llm_classification_latency_p95 (alert >5s), pii_stripper_entity_count (alert =0 as proxy for NER failure), compliance_config_threshold (dashboard). PagerDuty escalation on: list sync failure, hold queue depth >100, LLM endpoint 5xx rate >5%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS: ~$800/month for baseline 3-node pool with HPA. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o: ~$0.015 per classification prompt estimated at 5,000 classifications/day = ~$2,250/month; Redis cache at 85% hit rate reduces to ~$340/month LLM cost. PostgreSQL Flexible Server zone-redundant: ~$400/month. Redis Premium: ~$300/month. Total estimated ~$2,000-2,500/month at 5,000 daily transactions. Scaling: HPA handles 3x traffic spikes on api deployment; Celery worker count tunable for screening batch throughput.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Azure AD RBAC auth, OFAC SDN fuzzy screening, GPT-4o HS classification with PII stripping, compliance hold creation and resolution with structured justification form, PostgreSQL append-only audit_log with RLS, basic compliance queue UI in Next.js. Phase 2 (4 weeks): BIS entity list sync, atomic staging table swap, LLM classification cache by sanitized_product_hash, admin panel with compliance_config threshold management, AuditExportService CSV/PDF. Phase 3 (4 weeks): SOC 2 Type II evidence package generation in CI pipeline, EU dual-use goods CCL classification module, Azure Monitor alert tuning and PagerDuty integration runbooks.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff lint + mypy strict mode; all LLM prompt functions unit-tested with mock Azure OpenAI responses; ScreeningEngine fuzzy match unit-tested against OFAC test fixture with known true/false positive rates. TypeScript: strict mode; React Query mutations with optimistic updates for hold status. Test coverage: 90% gate enforced in CI for backend; Playwright E2E for hold resolution workflow and threshold change audit log. PR requires compliance officer review of any change to ScreeningEngine match logic or LLM prompt templates.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o HS classification errors leading to incorrect export authorizations. Mitigation: confidence routing at admin-configurable threshold routes low-confidence results to human review; every auto-approved classification stored with model_version for auditability. Risk: OFAC/BIS list sync failure leaving screening running on stale data beyond 4-hour SLA. Mitigation: Azure Monitor alert on sync_lag_seconds >14400 with PagerDuty page to on-call compliance engineer. Tradeoff: spaCy NER PII stripping over rule-based regex accepted for higher recall on entity names at cost of spaCy model weight in container image (~60 MB). Tradeoff: Redis 5-minute TTL on compliance_config threshold accepted as lag window when admin tightens threshold mid-day; documented in operational runbook.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Azure OpenAI West Europe region selected to satisfy data residency constraint; no cross-region LLM calls. PostgreSQL RLS DENY DELETE/UPDATE on audit_log chosen over immutable ledger service for cost and operational simplicity at this scale. Atomic staging table RENAME chosen over version-column list swap to guarantee zero partial-list-state window during screening. LLM confidence threshold moved from application constant to compliance_config table in round 3 to enable admin-configurable examination readiness.

metadata.notes: Trade compliance AI screening system with OFAC/BIS, GPT-4o HS classification, and admin-configurable confidence threshold resolved in round 3.

profile.domain: Freight Brokerage Infrastructure

project_goal.value: Replace aging on-premise Oracle RAC database cluster with managed Aurora PostgreSQL multi-region active-passive setup including schema migration tooling, read replica promotion runbooks, and zero-downtime cutover procedures
project_goal.rationale: Oracle RAC licensing costs are $2.1M annually; Aurora PostgreSQL reduces to $280K while eliminating self-managed patching burden

target_users.value: Freight brokerage engineering and DBA teams executing and maintaining the migration
target_users.rationale: Internal IT project with no end-user-facing changes

project_class.rationale: Pure infrastructure migration with Terraform provisioning, schema migration tooling, and cutover runbooks

capabilities.rationale: Requires Terraform IaC, Python migration scripts, Flyway schema versioning, and batch validation jobs

complexity_level.rationale: 4 TB Oracle RAC schema with 800+ stored procedures and 12 connected application tiers requiring zero-downtime cutover

risk_level.rationale: Mission-critical financial transaction data with active brokerage loads processed 24/7; any data loss causes direct revenue and SOX compliance impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Load records, carrier contracts, and freight invoices contain financial transaction data subject to SOX controls

external_exposure.rationale: Database infrastructure is internal; no public API exposure

access_model.value: IAM roles for AWS service access; database users via AWS Secrets Manager rotation; no shared credentials
access_model.rationale: Eliminate shared Oracle DBA passwords; IAM-based access for RDS authentication

feature_scope.value: Terraform Aurora Global Database provisioning, Flyway versioned schema migration scripts, parallel validation tooling comparing Oracle and Aurora financial aggregates, read replica promotion runbook, zero-downtime application connection string switchover via AWS SSM Parameter Store
feature_scope.rationale: Complete migration infrastructure scope

mvp_scope.value: Aurora cluster provisioned, Flyway migrations applied, parallel write validation passing for load_records and carrier_contracts tables, maintenance-window cutover executed for non-production environments
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum deliverable for stakeholder go/no-go decision on production cutover

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for Aurora connections, AWS Secrets Manager for credential rotation every 30 days, CloudTrail for all Aurora API actions, no Oracle DBA account migration to Aurora
security_baseline.rationale: Eliminate Oracle shared credential patterns; apply AWS-native secrets management

frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project with no UI; operational via CLI and runbooks

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Flyway 10 for schema migrations
backend_stack.rationale: Flyway provides versioned SQL migration management; Python for validation and cutover automation scripts

data_platform.value: Aurora PostgreSQL 15 with Aurora Global Database for multi-region active-passive
data_platform.rationale: Aurora Global Database provides sub-1s replication lag to secondary region for DR failover capability

hosting_target.value: Aurora Multi-AZ in us-east-1 (primary) with Aurora Global Database secondary in us-west-2
hosting_target.rationale: Data residency in US regions; us-west-2 secondary for DR with RTO under 30 minutes

privacy_retention_policy.value: Financial records retained 7 years per SOX requirements; migration validation logs retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX financial data retention requirements

future_scope.value: Aurora Serverless v2 for non-peak environments, pgvector extension for load similarity search, automated schema drift detection post-cutover
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 optimization after successful migration

constraints.value: Production cutover must occur in a 4-hour Saturday maintenance window; no stored procedure rewrite; Oracle-compatible SQL dialects must be adapted without application logic changes; budget $350K for first year including Aurora reserved instances
constraints.rationale: Business availability constraints and budget approval

observability_baseline.value: Datadog RDS integration for Aurora metrics, CloudWatch enhanced monitoring, Buildkite migration pipeline dashboard
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Datadog corporate standard extended to Aurora

execution_preference.value: Staged migration: dev then staging then production with go/no-go gates at each stage
execution_preference.rationale: Risk management for high-stakes database migration

compliance_context.value: SOX financial data integrity controls for load records and carrier contract financials; cross-border freight data residency requirements for US-only storage
compliance_context.rationale: Publicly traded freight brokerage subject to SOX Section 404 IT controls audit

project.goal: Zero-downtime Oracle RAC to Aurora PostgreSQL migration for 4TB freight brokerage database

backend.framework: Flyway 10 for schema versioning, boto3 for AWS automation

security.auth: IAM roles for RDS access, AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 for Aurora connections, KMS encryption at rest

data.global_db: Aurora Global Database secondary in us-west-2
data.validation: Parallel query validation comparing Oracle and Aurora aggregate totals

devops.environments: dev, staging, production with staged go/no-go gates

constraints.no_sproc_rewrite: Oracle stored procedures adapted via compatibility layer

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration delivers no user-facing features; success criteria are data integrity parity, application connectivity restored within the 4-hour window, and zero financial aggregate discrepancies in the 30-day post-migration validation. The go/no-go gate at each stage must be defined with explicit pass/fail criteria, not subjective judgment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Aurora PostgreSQL 15 is the right target. The 800 stored procedures are the highest risk: Oracle PL/SQL uses rownum, CONNECT BY hierarchy queries, and DBMS_* packages that have no direct PostgreSQL equivalents. A dialect compatibility audit should categorize procedures into auto-translatable, needs-wrapper, and requires-rewrite before migration tooling is built. Flyway versioned migrations plus AWS SCT for initial schema conversion is the recommended approach.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOX IT controls require that the migration itself generates an audit trail: every Flyway migration version applied, every validation run result, and the cutover timestamp must be recorded in an immutable migration_audit_log that survives the Oracle decommission. AWS KMS encryption must be applied to the Aurora cluster at provisioning time, not as a post-migration add-on.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour Saturday maintenance window is tight for a 4 TB database. The zero-downtime strategy must use dual-write during the shadow period rather than a hard cutover. Application connection strings should switch via AWS SSM Parameter Store update so all tiers flip atomically without redeployment.

backend.service_design: Python migration orchestrator with commands: dialect-audit, schema-migrate, validate-parity, cutover, rollback; each command idempotent and resumable
backend.business_modules: DialectAuditor: scans Oracle DDL for rownum/CONNECT BY/DBMS_* patterns and classifies each procedure; SchemaConverter: applies AWS SCT output then Flyway versioned patches; ParityValidator: parallel aggregate queries against Oracle and Aurora comparing row counts and financial sums; CutoverOrchestrator: SSM Parameter Store swap with pre/post health checks
backend.background_jobs: Nightly parity validation batch running for 30 days post-cutover comparing financial aggregate totals between Oracle archive snapshot and Aurora
backend.failure_handling: All migration commands are idempotent; partial Flyway runs resume from last successful version; CutoverOrchestrator has 10-minute health check timeout before automatic SSM rollback

security.auth_design: IAM role-based RDS authentication; no static DB passwords; Secrets Manager auto-rotation every 30 days for application user credentials
security.secrets_management: Terraform provisions Secrets Manager secrets for aurora_app_user and aurora_readonly_user; rotation Lambda attached at creation; old Oracle credentials revoked post-cutover
security.audit_and_logging_controls: migration_audit_log table in Aurora records every Flyway version applied, every parity validation run, and cutover events with timestamp and executor identity; CloudTrail captures all RDS API calls

data.entities: load_records (id, broker_id, carrier_id, shipper_id, origin_zip, dest_zip, pickup_date, delivery_date, agreed_rate_cents, invoiced_amount_cents, status, created_at), carrier_contracts (id, carrier_id, effective_date, expiry_date, lane_id, rate_per_mile, fuel_surcharge_pct, contract_doc_s3_key), freight_invoices (id, load_id, carrier_id, invoice_number, amount_cents, paid_at, payment_method, reconciled), broker_transactions (id, load_id, buy_rate_cents, sell_rate_cents, margin_cents, settled_at), schema_versions managed by Flyway, migration_audit_log (id, run_id, event_type, version_or_table, row_count, financial_sum_cents, executed_by, executed_at, outcome)
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL 15 Multi-AZ cluster in us-east-1 (primary writer plus 2 readers); Aurora Global Database secondary cluster in us-west-2 for DR; KMS-encrypted; separate read replica promoted for reporting workloads
data.retention_and_deletion: Financial records (load_records, freight_invoices, broker_transactions) retained 7 years; migration_audit_log retained 2 years; Oracle archive snapshot retained 1 year post-cutover for forensic comparison
data.migration_notes: Flyway V001-V087 scripts apply Oracle-to-PostgreSQL DDL transformations; stored procedure dialect wrappers in V050-V087; checksum validation prevents unauthorized schema drift

devops.deployment_topology: Terraform modules: aurora-cluster (Multi-AZ writer plus readers, parameter group, KMS key, subnet group, security groups), aurora-global (secondary cluster in us-west-2), secrets-rotation (Secrets Manager plus rotation Lambda), ssm-connection-strings (Parameter Store params per application tier)
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite pipeline stages: dialect-audit then schema-migrate-dev then validate-parity-dev then gate (DBA approval) then schema-migrate-staging then validate-parity-staging then gate then cutover-dry-run then gate then production-cutover with rollback step
devops.rollback_strategy: Oracle cluster retained in read-only mode for 30 days post-cutover; SSM Parameter Store rollback script flips connection strings back to Oracle in under 5 minutes; Aurora cluster retained for 90 days post-Oracle-decommission
devops.backup_and_recovery: Aurora automated backups: 35-day retention, point-in-time recovery to 5-minute granularity; Aurora Global Database enables under 30-minute RTO for full-region failure

target_output.thinking_summary: High-risk financial database migration from Oracle RAC to Aurora PostgreSQL for a freight brokerage. Key decisions: Flyway versioned migrations with AWS SCT for dialect conversion, SSM Parameter Store atomic connection string flip across 12 application tiers, and 30-day post-migration parity validation comparing financial aggregates. SOX compliance requires immutable migration_audit_log surviving Oracle decommission.
target_output.title: Oracle RAC to Aurora PostgreSQL Migration Infrastructure
target_output.executive_summary: A staged infrastructure migration replacing a 4 TB Oracle RAC cluster with Aurora PostgreSQL 15 Multi-AZ in us-east-1 and an Aurora Global Database secondary in us-west-2, targeting zero-downtime cutover within a 4-hour Saturday maintenance window. Delivers Terraform-managed Aurora provisioning, Flyway versioned schema migration, AWS SCT dialect conversion for 800+ stored procedures, parallel financial aggregate parity validation, and SSM Parameter Store-based atomic connection string flip across 12 application tiers. SOX compliance preserved through an immutable migration_audit_log recording every Flyway version and parity result for IT controls audit.
target_output.architecture_overview: Migration executed in three infrastructure phases: (1) Provision Aurora PostgreSQL 15 Multi-AZ cluster and Global Database secondary via Terraform with KMS encryption and Secrets Manager credential rotation; (2) Apply Flyway versioned schema migrations from AWS SCT output against Aurora in parallel with live Oracle; (3) Execute zero-downtime cutover by enabling dual-write during shadow period, running parity validation until financial aggregates match, then atomically flipping all 12 application tier connection strings via SSM Parameter Store update within the 4-hour maintenance window. Oracle cluster retained read-only for 30 days. Aurora Global Database provides sub-1s replication lag to us-west-2 secondary for RTO under 30 minutes on full-region failure.
target_output.technology_stack: Migration tooling: Python 3.12 orchestrator using Flyway 10 CLI, AWS SCT, psycopg2 for Aurora validation queries, cx_Oracle for source queries, boto3 for SSM/Secrets Manager/RDS API. Infrastructure: Terraform 1.7 with AWS provider 5.x for Aurora cluster, Global Database, KMS, Secrets Manager, and SSM Parameter Store. CI/CD: Buildkite with stage gates and human approval steps. Monitoring: Datadog RDS integration, CloudWatch enhanced monitoring on Aurora. No application code changes required.
target_output.functional_feature_map: DialectAuditor command: scans Oracle DDL export for rownum, CONNECT BY, DBMS_OUTPUT, DBMS_JOB, NVL, DECODE patterns; classifies each object as auto_convertible/needs_wrapper/requires_review; outputs CSV report for DBA sign-off before schema migration begins. SchemaMigrator: applies Flyway versioned scripts in order against Aurora; failed scripts halt pipeline and require manual resolution. ParityValidator: for each table in validation_manifest.yaml, executes COUNT(*), SUM(amount_cents), MAX(updated_at) queries against Oracle and Aurora simultaneously; fails pipeline if financial sum variance exceeds one cent. CutoverOrchestrator: stops Oracle write traffic, executes final parity check, updates SSM Parameter Store connection string params for all 12 application tiers, triggers health checks per tier, rolls back via SSM revert if any tier fails within 10 minutes. PostMigrationValidator: nightly Buildkite job for 30 days comparing broker_transactions and freight_invoices aggregate totals between Oracle archive and Aurora.
target_output.system_components: Aurora PostgreSQL 15 Writer (us-east-1a): primary database instance for brokerage transactional workloads. Aurora PostgreSQL 15 Reader x2 (us-east-1b, us-east-1c): read replicas for reporting; one promoted to writer on writer failure. Aurora Global Database Secondary Cluster (us-west-2): sub-1s replication lag; promotable to primary within 30 minutes. KMS Customer Managed Key: encrypts Aurora storage, annual key rotation. Secrets Manager: aurora_app_user and aurora_readonly_user secrets with 30-day rotation Lambda. SSM Parameter Store: connection string parameters per application tier atomically updated during cutover. Flyway 10 CLI: schema version management in Git with checksum validation. migration_audit_log table: append-only record of all migration events with INSERT-only grant for migration_executor role. Buildkite Pipeline: orchestrates all migration stages with human approval gates.
target_output.workflows: Schema Migration Pipeline: Buildkite triggers DialectAuditor on Oracle DDL export; DBA reviews CSV report and approves; SchemaMigrator applies Flyway V001 through V087 scripts against Aurora dev cluster; ParityValidator runs on load_records, carrier_contracts, freight_invoices, broker_transactions for row count and financial aggregate parity; human approval gate; repeat for staging cluster; cutover-dry-run exercises CutoverOrchestrator against staging. Zero-Downtime Production Cutover: maintenance window opens Saturday 02:00 UTC; application write traffic throttled via circuit breaker; final parity check executes across all financial tables; CutoverOrchestrator updates 12 SSM Parameter Store connection string params in sequence; each application tier reloads DB config and executes health check; within 10 minutes all tiers confirm healthy; Oracle cluster set to read-only. Rollback: if any application tier health check fails within the 10-minute window, CutoverOrchestrator executes SSM revert script restoring Oracle connection strings across all tiers in under 5 minutes. Post-Cutover Validation: nightly Buildkite job for 30 days queries Oracle archive and Aurora; compares SUM(margin_cents) on broker_transactions and SUM(invoiced_amount_cents) on freight_invoices by settlement date; Datadog alert on variance over one cent.
target_output.data_model: load_records (id BIGSERIAL PK, broker_id INT, carrier_id INT, shipper_id INT, origin_zip CHAR(5), dest_zip CHAR(5), pickup_date DATE, delivery_date DATE, agreed_rate_cents BIGINT, invoiced_amount_cents BIGINT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). carrier_contracts (id BIGSERIAL PK, carrier_id INT, effective_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, lane_id INT, rate_per_mile_cents INT, fuel_surcharge_pct NUMERIC, contract_doc_s3_key TEXT). freight_invoices (id BIGSERIAL PK, load_id BIGINT, invoice_number TEXT UNIQUE, amount_cents BIGINT, paid_at TIMESTAMPTZ, payment_method TEXT, reconciled BOOLEAN). broker_transactions (id BIGSERIAL PK, load_id BIGINT, buy_rate_cents BIGINT, sell_rate_cents BIGINT, margin_cents BIGINT, settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). schema_versions managed by Flyway (installed_rank, version, description, script, checksum, installed_by, installed_on, success). migration_audit_log (id BIGSERIAL PK, run_id UUID, event_type TEXT, object_name TEXT, row_count BIGINT, financial_sum_cents BIGINT, executed_by TEXT, executed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, outcome TEXT, detail JSONB) with INSERT-only grant.
target_output.api_design: No public API. Migration orchestrator exposes CLI commands: 'python migrate.py dialect-audit --oracle-ddl=schema.sql --output=audit.csv'; 'python migrate.py schema-migrate --env=dev|staging|prod --flyway-config=flyway.conf'; 'python migrate.py validate-parity --env=dev|staging|prod --manifest=validation_manifest.yaml --fail-on-variance'; 'python migrate.py cutover --env=prod --window-minutes=240 --dry-run'; 'python migrate.py rollback --env=prod'. All commands log structured JSON to stdout and write events to migration_audit_log. Buildkite steps invoke commands via Docker container with IAM task role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM role-based RDS authentication: application tiers use IAM auth tokens via aurora_app_user; no static passwords in application config. Secrets Manager auto-rotation: aurora_app_user secret rotated every 30 days via Lambda; rotation tested in dev before production. KMS CMK encrypts Aurora storage; key rotation every 365 days. Oracle shared DBA credentials revoked 30 days post-cutover. migration_audit_log: INSERT-only grant for migration_executor IAM role; no DELETE or UPDATE; provides SOX IT controls evidence that every Flyway version applied and every parity validation result is permanently recorded. CloudTrail logs all RDS API calls for SOX audit. Cross-border data residency: us-east-1 and us-west-2 only; Global Database replication stays within US regions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock in us-east-1. Buildkite pipeline stages: dialect-audit (automated) then schema-migrate-dev (automated) then validate-parity-dev (automated) then gate-dev (DBA approval) then schema-migrate-staging then validate-parity-staging then gate-staging (DBA plus Security approval) then cutover-dry-run (staging) then gate-production (VP Engineering approval) then production-cutover (Saturday 02:00 UTC). Each Buildkite step runs in a Docker container with Terraform/Python/Flyway via IAM task role. Rollback artifact: SSM Parameter Store snapshot taken before cutover; revert script restores all 12 connection string params from snapshot. Oracle cluster remains in read-only standby for 30 days post-cutover before decommission.
target_output.observability: Datadog RDS integration monitors Aurora: FreeLocalStorage, DatabaseConnections, ReplicaLag (alert over 5s), WriteIOPS, CPUUtilization. CloudWatch Enhanced Monitoring: OS-level metrics for Aurora writer at 1-second granularity during cutover window. Buildkite pipeline dashboard shows per-stage status with parity validation pass/fail summary. migration_audit_log queried post-run to generate SOX evidence CSV: Flyway versions applied, parity validation outcomes, cutover timestamps. PagerDuty alerts: parity validation variance over one cent, Aurora ReplicaLag over 5s, cutover health check failure, post-cutover validation anomaly.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Aurora reserved instances 1-year: db.r6g.4xlarge writer plus 2 readers approximately $180K/year versus Oracle RAC $2.1M/year; estimated $1.8M annual saving. Aurora Global Database secondary adds approximately $40K/year for DR capability. Secrets Manager approximately $20/month for 2 secrets with rotation. Total year-1 including Buildkite compute and migration engineering approximately $280K versus $350K budget. Scaling: Aurora writer vertically scalable to db.r6g.16xlarge; read replicas scale out for reporting workloads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Terraform Aurora cluster provisioning for dev and staging, Flyway schema migration scripts from AWS SCT output, DialectAuditor classifying 800 stored procedures, dev parity validation passing for all financial tables, Secrets Manager rotation configured. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Staging cutover dry-run, application tier connection string testing via SSM Parameter Store, CutoverOrchestrator dry-run on staging with simulated traffic, parallel write shadow test on staging, go/no-go gate sign-off with DBA and Security teams. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Production cutover execution in Saturday maintenance window, 30-day post-cutover nightly parity validation, Oracle cluster decommission planning, Aurora Global Database DR failover test in us-west-2.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python migration scripts: mypy strict mode, 95% unit test coverage for ParityValidator aggregate logic. SQL migration scripts: every Flyway script reviewed by DBA and Security before merge; no DDL in application code. Terraform: modules versioned and pinned; no manual AWS console changes to Aurora resources after Terraform provisioning. Runbooks: every Buildkite gate has a linked Confluence runbook with explicit pass/fail criteria, escalation contacts, and rollback steps. Pre-cutover rehearsal: CutoverOrchestrator dry-run must pass twice on staging before production gate approval.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle PL/SQL stored procedures with CONNECT BY hierarchy queries cannot be auto-converted by AWS SCT. Mitigation: DialectAuditor categorizes each procedure; requires_review procedures assigned to DBA sprint before Phase 2. Risk: Application tier misses SSM Parameter Store update during cutover causing connection failures. Mitigation: each tier tested for SSM config reload in staging dry-run; health check timeout 10 minutes before rollback trigger. Risk: Aurora Global Database replication lag spike under heavy write load. Mitigation: ReplicaLag Datadog alert at 5s; sustained lag over 30s during cutover triggers cutover abort. Tradeoff: 4-hour maintenance window accepted over continuous online cutover for data integrity certainty on financial tables.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Aurora PostgreSQL 15 chosen over Aurora 16 due to SCT compatibility maturity at project start. Flyway 10 chosen over Liquibase for checksum integrity model and Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration track record. SSM Parameter Store atomic flip chosen over DNS-based cutover to avoid DNS TTL propagation uncertainty across 12 application tiers. Oracle cluster retained read-only 30 days post-cutover as rollback insurance rather than instant decommission.

metadata.notes: Oracle RAC to Aurora PostgreSQL migration for freight brokerage with SOX controls, Flyway versioning, and SSM atomic cutover.

profile.domain: Parcel Network Operations

project_goal.value: Process millions of daily parcel scan events from sortation hubs, detect service failures such as missed scans and out-of-sequence events, and generate per-hub reliability scorecards used by network operations teams to prioritize maintenance interventions
project_goal.rationale: Network operations team currently reviews hub failures manually from raw scan logs; automated scoring will reduce detection latency from days to hours

target_users.value: Network operations engineers and hub maintenance teams
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of hub scorecards and maintenance ticket triggers

project_class.rationale: Batch processing pipeline with rule-based detection and automated maintenance ticket creation, not an interactive application

capabilities.rationale: Requires Spring Batch processing, ClickHouse analytics, scorecard generation, and ServiceNow integration

complexity_level.rationale: Processing millions of scan events daily across thousands of sortation hubs requires partitioned batch design and columnar storage

risk_level.rationale: Internal operations tool; incorrect scorecards could delay maintenance interventions but no direct customer data exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Scan events and hub performance data are internal operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Analytics output consumed by internal network operations dashboards only

access_model.value: IAM roles for ECS task access to S3, ClickHouse, and ServiceNow API credentials via Secrets Manager
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service access only; no human user authentication required for batch jobs

feature_scope.value: Daily batch ingestion of scan event files from S3, per-parcel event sequence reconstruction, missed-scan and out-of-sequence rule detection, per-hub failure rate aggregation, weighted reliability scorecard computation, weekly scorecard publication to ClickHouse analytics schema, automated ServiceNow ticket creation for deteriorating hubs
feature_scope.rationale: Full scorecard pipeline scope

mvp_scope.value: Batch ingestion, sequence reconstruction, missed-scan detection, and hub failure rate aggregation writing to ClickHouse; scorecard computation with manual review before ServiceNow integration
mvp_scope.rationale: Validate scoring accuracy before automating ticket creation

security_baseline.value: IAM task roles for ECS, Secrets Manager for ServiceNow credentials, S3 server-side encryption, ClickHouse TLS connections
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool baseline controls

frontend_stack.rationale: No UI; output consumed by existing network operations dashboards querying ClickHouse

backend_stack.rationale: Spring Batch provides chunk-oriented processing with restart/retry semantics needed for multi-million row daily files

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for analytics storage; S3 Parquet for raw scan event landing zone
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse handles sub-second aggregation queries across billions of scan rows; S3 Parquet for cost-effective raw retention

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for batch jobs
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container execution for daily batch with no always-on compute cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: Scan event data retained 2 years in ClickHouse for trend analysis; raw S3 files retained 90 days then archived to S3 Glacier
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Network operations trend analysis requires 2-year history; raw files archived for audit

future_scope.value: Real-time anomaly detection via Kinesis Data Streams, predictive hub failure scoring using gradient boosting, cross-hub routing impact analysis
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after batch scoring proves value

constraints.value: Daily processing must complete within 3 hours of file delivery; ClickHouse cluster shared with other analytics workloads; ServiceNow API rate limit 200 calls/minute
constraints.rationale: Operational SLA and shared infrastructure constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Spring Batch job metrics, CloudWatch for ECS task execution, PagerDuty for job failure alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Datadog corporate standard

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; batch processing validated on 30-day historical scan file sample before production deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Validate detection accuracy on historical data before processing live daily files

project.goal: Automated hub reliability scoring from daily parcel scan event batches

security.auth: IAM task roles for ECS
security.secrets: Secrets Manager for ServiceNow API credentials

data.landing: S3 Parquet for raw scan events

reasoner_reviews.product: The scorecard must be explainable to hub maintenance teams. Each hub scorecard should surface the top 3 failure categories driving the score change, not just the aggregate score, so maintenance teams know which equipment or process to inspect. The ServiceNow ticket pre-population with specific failure categories and affected scan points is the key adoption driver.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Batch chunk-oriented processing with configurable chunk size of 10,000 scan events is well-suited for the daily file volumes. Partitioning the Spring Batch job by hub_id allows parallel processing across ECS Fargate tasks. The sequence reconstruction step must handle out-of-order event delivery within the same batch file. ClickHouse MergeTree table with hub_id and event_date as partition keys will serve the weekly scorecard aggregation queries at sub-second latency.
reasoner_reviews.security: ECS task role must have S3 GetObject on the scan event landing bucket and ClickHouse write access. ServiceNow API credentials should be fetched from Secrets Manager at job startup, not baked into the container image. Datadog APM agent should not log raw scan event content to avoid operational data leakage in log aggregation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-hour processing SLA with hub-partitioned Spring Batch parallelism should be achievable for 50M daily events across 200 hubs at 10,000-event chunk size. ServiceNow rate limit of 200 calls/minute means ticket creation must be batched and rate-throttled; do not create one API call per deteriorating hub synchronously.

backend.service_design: Spring Batch job: ScanEventIngestionJob (reads S3 Parquet, reconstructs sequences, detects failures) and HubScorecardJob (aggregates failure rates, computes scores, publishes to ClickHouse, triggers ServiceNow). Both jobs partitioned by hub_id for parallel Fargate task execution.
backend.business_modules: ScanSequenceReconstructor: groups scan events by tracking_number within hub partition, sorts by expected_scan_sequence, detects missing mandatory scan points and events arriving after the expected successor. FailureDetector: applies configurable rule set per event_type (missed_induct, missed_sort, out_of_sequence, late_delivery_scan). HubScorecard: aggregates failure counts by hub_id and failure_type for the trailing 7-day window, applies weights per failure_type severity, computes 0-100 reliability score. ServiceNowTicketCreator: batches deteriorating hubs (score dropped over 10 points week-over-week), creates incident tickets via ServiceNow REST API with rate limiter at 180 calls/minute.
backend.background_jobs: ScanEventIngestionJob scheduled at 06:00 UTC daily via ECS scheduled task triggered by EventBridge rule. HubScorecardJob scheduled at 09:00 UTC daily after ingestion SLA window. WeeklyScorecardPublishJob every Monday 10:00 UTC publishes 7-day aggregated scorecards to ClickHouse hub_scorecards table.
backend.failure_handling: Spring Batch restart from last committed chunk on Fargate task failure; job execution metadata in RDS PostgreSQL for restart state; failed hub partitions retried up to 3 times before alerting; dead-letter S3 prefix for unparseable event files

security.auth_design: ECS task role with S3 GetObject on scan-events-landing bucket, ClickHouse write via Secrets Manager credential, ServiceNow API key via Secrets Manager
security.secrets_management: ServiceNow API key stored in Secrets Manager; fetched at ScanEventIngestionJob startup; rotated quarterly by ops team
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs via SLF4J MDC with job_id, hub_id, chunk_offset; no raw scan event content logged; Datadog APM traces per Spring Batch step

data.entities: scan_events (tracking_number, hub_id, scan_point_id, event_type, scanned_at, expected_sequence, actual_sequence, batch_date) stored in ClickHouse scan_events table with MergeTree engine partitioned by toYYYYMM(batch_date). service_failures (id, tracking_number, hub_id, failure_type ENUM missed_induct/missed_sort/out_of_sequence/late_delivery_scan, detected_at, batch_date). hub_scorecards (hub_id, scorecard_week DATE, failure_counts_json, weighted_score FLOAT, previous_score FLOAT, score_delta FLOAT, top_failure_categories_json, published_at). maintenance_tickets (ticket_id, hub_id, servicenow_number, failure_categories_json, created_at, api_response_status). pipeline_runs (run_id, job_name, partition_hub_id, status, records_processed, failures_detected, started_at, completed_at).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse cluster (shared): scan_events (MergeTree, partition by month), service_failures (MergeTree, partition by month), hub_scorecards (ReplacingMergeTree, dedup by hub_id+scorecard_week). Spring Batch job metadata in RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.medium (restart state only). Raw S3 Parquet landing: s3://parcel-scan-events/raw/YYYY/MM/DD/ with 90-day lifecycle to Glacier.
data.analytics_events: hub_scorecards table published to shared ClickHouse analytics schema; network operations dashboards query hub_id, scorecard_week, weighted_score, top_failure_categories_json for trend charts and maintenance prioritization.
data.data_risks: Duplicate scan events in source files must be deduplicated by (tracking_number, hub_id, scan_point_id, scanned_at) before sequence analysis to avoid false failure detections.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with two scheduled task definitions: scan-ingest-task (hub-partitioned, up to 20 parallel Fargate tasks at 2 vCPU/4GB each) and scorecard-task (single Fargate task at 4 vCPU/8GB for aggregation). EventBridge rules trigger both tasks on schedule.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Maven build + JUnit tests (90% coverage gate) + Docker build + ECR push + ECS task definition update. Historical 30-day backfill test on staging before production promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM Java agent on ECS tasks: custom metrics hub_processing_duration_seconds, failure_detection_rate_per_hub, scorecard_score_distribution. CloudWatch ECS task execution logs. PagerDuty: alert on job failure after 3 retries, processing SLA breach (not complete by 09:00 UTC), ServiceNow API error rate over 10%.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous Docker image tag retained in ECR; ECS task definition rollback via deploy script; ClickHouse hub_scorecards ReplacingMergeTree allows re-publish of corrected scorecard data.

target_output.thinking_summary: High-scale daily batch pipeline processing millions of parcel scan events for hub reliability scoring. Key decisions: Spring Batch hub-partitioned parallel processing on ECS Fargate for the 3-hour SLA, ClickHouse MergeTree for sub-second aggregation across billions of rows, ServiceNow rate-limited ticket creation at 180 calls/minute, and Spring Batch restart-from-chunk using RDS for job state to handle Fargate task interruptions.
target_output.title: Parcel Hub Reliability Scoring Batch Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A daily batch automation tool on AWS ECS Fargate that ingests millions of parcel scan events from S3 landing zones, reconstructs per-parcel event sequences within sortation hubs, detects service failures (missed scans, out-of-sequence events), and computes per-hub reliability scorecards stored in ClickHouse. Deteriorating hub scores trigger pre-populated ServiceNow maintenance tickets, enabling network operations teams to prioritize interventions based on quantified failure category data rather than manual log review.
target_output.architecture_overview: Spring Batch 5 jobs deployed as ECS Fargate scheduled tasks, partitioned by hub_id to run up to 20 parallel task instances per daily batch. ScanEventIngestionJob reads S3 Parquet files for each hub partition, deduplications events by tracking_number/hub_id/scan_point_id/scanned_at composite key, reconstructs parcel event sequences, and writes detected service_failures to ClickHouse. HubScorecardJob aggregates failure counts from the trailing 7-day window, applies configurable failure_type weights to compute a 0-100 weighted_score per hub, and writes to ClickHouse hub_scorecards ReplacingMergeTree. WeeklyScorecardPublishJob compares current versus prior-week scores and invokes ServiceNowTicketCreator for hubs with score delta below -10. Spring Batch restart state in RDS PostgreSQL enables chunk-level resumption on Fargate task interruption.
target_output.technology_stack: Batch runtime: Java 21, Spring Batch 5, Spring Boot 3, Jackson for Parquet parsing, clickhouse-jdbc for ClickHouse writes, aws-java-sdk-s3 for S3 access, resilience4j for ServiceNow rate limiting. Analytics: ClickHouse shared cluster with MergeTree and ReplacingMergeTree engines. Job state: RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.medium for Spring Batch metadata schema. Hosting: ECS Fargate with EventBridge scheduled triggers. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Maven and ECR. Monitoring: Datadog APM Java agent.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ScanSequenceReconstructor: groups scan events by tracking_number within hub partition, sorts by expected_scan_sequence ordinal, flags missing mandatory scan points (induct, sort, load) and events arriving after their expected successor scan; outputs service_failure records per anomaly. FailureDetector: applies hub-specific and carrier-wide rule sets classifying each anomaly into missed_induct, missed_sort, out_of_sequence, or late_delivery_scan failure types. HubScorecardComputer: queries ClickHouse service_failures for trailing 7-day window per hub, aggregates failure counts by type, applies weights (missed_induct: 0.35, missed_sort: 0.30, out_of_sequence: 0.25, late_delivery_scan: 0.10) and computes 0-100 score. ServiceNowTicketCreator: batches hubs with score_delta below -10, formats ServiceNow incident payload with hub_id, top_failure_categories_json, affected_tracking_numbers_sample, creates tickets via REST API with resilience4j RateLimiter at 180 calls/minute.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate scan-ingest-task (up to 20 parallel instances at 2 vCPU/4 GB): runs ScanEventIngestionJob for assigned hub_id partition. ECS Fargate scorecard-task (1 instance at 4 vCPU/8 GB): runs HubScorecardJob and WeeklyScorecardPublishJob. EventBridge rules: scan-ingest-trigger at 06:00 UTC, scorecard-trigger at 09:00 UTC daily. S3 landing zone: s3://parcel-scan-events/raw/YYYY/MM/DD/ partitioned Parquet files per hub. ClickHouse cluster (shared): scan_events, service_failures, hub_scorecards tables. RDS PostgreSQL (db.t3.medium): Spring Batch metadata (BATCH_JOB_INSTANCE, BATCH_STEP_EXECUTION tables). Secrets Manager: ServiceNow API credentials. Datadog APM agent sidecar on each Fargate task.
target_output.workflows: Daily Ingestion: EventBridge triggers scan-ingest ECS task at 06:00 UTC; Spring Batch partitioner reads hub_id list from hub registry; spawns up to 20 parallel Fargate tasks each processing one hub_id partition; each task reads S3 Parquet for its hub, deduplicates events, runs ScanSequenceReconstructor, writes service_failures to ClickHouse; job metadata committed to RDS PostgreSQL per chunk of 10,000 events; on task failure, restarts from last committed chunk. Scorecard Computation: EventBridge triggers scorecard-task at 09:00 UTC; HubScorecardJob queries ClickHouse service_failures for all hubs for trailing 7 days; computes weighted scores; writes to hub_scorecards ReplacingMergeTree; WeeklyScorecardPublishJob on Monday compares scores to prior week, identifies hubs with score_delta below -10, batches ServiceNow ticket creation via rate-limited REST calls with hub failure category payload. Maintenance Ticket Content: each ServiceNow incident pre-populated with hub_id, week-over-week score trend, top 3 failure categories by count, sample affected tracking numbers, and recommended inspection checklist linked to failure type.
target_output.data_model: scan_events (tracking_number String, hub_id LowCardinality(String), scan_point_id String, event_type String, scanned_at DateTime, expected_sequence UInt16, actual_sequence UInt16, batch_date Date) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(batch_date) ORDER BY (hub_id, tracking_number, scanned_at). service_failures (id UUID, tracking_number String, hub_id LowCardinality(String), failure_type Enum8('missed_induct'=1,'missed_sort'=2,'out_of_sequence'=3,'late_delivery_scan'=4), detected_at DateTime, batch_date Date) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(batch_date) ORDER BY (hub_id, failure_type, batch_date). hub_scorecards (hub_id LowCardinality(String), scorecard_week Date, failure_counts_json String, weighted_score Float32, previous_score Float32, score_delta Float32, top_failure_categories_json String, published_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree(published_at) ORDER BY (hub_id, scorecard_week). pipeline_runs (run_id UUID, job_name String, partition_hub_id String, status String, records_processed UInt64, failures_detected UInt64, started_at DateTime, completed_at Nullable(DateTime)) ENGINE=MergeTree() ORDER BY started_at.
target_output.api_design: No public API. Internal interfaces: Spring Batch JobLauncher invoked by EventBridge ECS task trigger. ClickHouse JDBC writes from Spring Batch ItemWriter implementations. ServiceNow REST API: POST /api/now/table/incident with JSON payload including hub_id, failure summary, and inspection checklist. Network operations dashboards query ClickHouse hub_scorecards table directly via existing BI tool SQL connections. Pipeline run status queryable via Datadog custom metrics dashboard.
target_output.security_and_compliance: ECS task IAM role: S3 GetObject/ListBucket on scan-events-landing, ClickHouse write via Secrets Manager credential, Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for ServiceNow API key. ServiceNow API credentials stored in Secrets Manager, rotated quarterly, fetched at job startup not baked into image. Datadog APM agent configured to exclude raw scan event content from traces. S3 scan event landing bucket: server-side encryption with SSE-S3, no public access. ClickHouse connection via TLS. No PII in scan events (tracking numbers are internal reference IDs).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Maven build, JUnit 5 tests (90% line coverage gate for ScanSequenceReconstructor and HubScorecardComputer), Docker build, ECR push, ECS task definition update via AWS CLI. Environments: dev (single hub partition, 30-day historical backfill test), staging (full hub set, production S3 data copy), production (live daily schedule). Deployment: ECS task definition update with new image digest; EventBridge rules unchanged. Rollback: previous ECR image tag retained; ECS task definition rollback via deploy script. Historical backfill: one-time Spring Batch JobParametersIncrementer run over 30 days of S3 data to seed ClickHouse before live production schedule starts.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: Java agent on each Fargate task traces Spring Batch step execution with custom metrics hub_processing_duration_seconds (histogram by hub_id), failure_detection_rate (gauge), scorecard_score_p50 (gauge). CloudWatch: ECS task exit code alerts for non-zero exits. Custom Datadog dashboard: daily job completion status, per-hub processing duration, failure detection count, scorecard score distribution. PagerDuty alerts: ScanEventIngestionJob not complete by 09:00 UTC (SLA breach), job failure after 3 retries, ServiceNow API error rate over 10%, ClickHouse write failure rate over 1%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: 20 tasks at 2 vCPU/4 GB for 2.5 hours daily approximately $12/day. ClickHouse shared cluster cost allocated by query volume: scan_events table at 50M rows/day over 2 years is approximately 36B rows, estimated 500 GB compressed. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.medium for Spring Batch metadata approximately $30/month. Total daily operational cost approximately $15. Scaling: hub count growth handled by increasing ECS task partition count; ClickHouse MergeTree partitioning supports linear scan volume growth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (5 weeks): S3 Parquet ingestion with ScanSequenceReconstructor and FailureDetector for missed_induct and missed_sort rules, service_failures writes to ClickHouse, hub failure rate aggregation, Spring Batch restart-from-chunk, 30-day historical backfill validation on staging against manually verified hub failure ground truth. Phase 2 (3 weeks): HubScorecardComputer with weighted scoring, hub_scorecards publication to ClickHouse, network operations dashboard integration via ClickHouse SQL. Phase 3 (2 weeks): ServiceNow ticket creation with rate limiter, out_of_sequence and late_delivery_scan failure detection rules, Datadog custom metrics dashboard and PagerDuty alert tuning.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java: Spring Batch step implementations unit-tested with in-memory JobRepository and fixture Parquet files; ScanSequenceReconstructor tested against known-bad event sequences from 30-day historical backfill. 90% line coverage gate enforced in CI. ClickHouse ItemWriter tested with Testcontainers ClickHouse image. ServiceNow client mocked in unit tests; rate limiter integration tested with WireMock. No raw scan event tracking numbers in Datadog trace attributes. Failure detection rule changes require DBA review of historical backfill impact before production merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Duplicate scan events in source S3 files causing inflated failure detection rates. Mitigation: deduplication step before sequence reconstruction using composite key; deduplicated count logged per batch for monitoring. Risk: ClickHouse shared cluster query contention during peak analytics hours impacting ScanEventIngestionJob write throughput. Mitigation: ClickHouse write buffer settings tuned for batch inserts; ingest tasks scheduled at 06:00 UTC before peak dashboard query window. Risk: ServiceNow API quota exhaustion if many hubs deteriorate simultaneously. Mitigation: resilience4j RateLimiter hard-capped at 180 calls/minute with overflow queued not dropped. Tradeoff: Spring Batch chunk-restart over stateless reprocessing accepted for restart reliability at cost of RDS PostgreSQL dependency for job metadata.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ClickHouse chosen over Redshift for hub scorecard aggregation due to superior sub-second query performance on high-cardinality hub_id/failure_type GROUP BY queries. Spring Batch 5 chosen over Apache Spark for hub-partitioned batch due to lower operational overhead and native chunk-restart semantics. ServiceNow chosen over email alerting for maintenance tickets to integrate with existing maintenance team workflow and enable ticket SLA tracking. Failure type weights (missed_induct: 0.35, missed_sort: 0.30, out_of_sequence: 0.25, late_delivery_scan: 0.10) validated against 30-day historical backfill compared to known maintenance interventions.

metadata.notes: Parcel hub reliability scoring batch pipeline with Spring Batch hub-partitioned Fargate tasks and ClickHouse scorecard analytics.

profile.domain: Logistics Spend Analytics

project_goal.value: Freight spend analytics web app for logistics managers at mid-size manufacturers, aggregating carrier invoice data to surface cost reduction opportunities such as rate renegotiation targets, mode shift recommendations, and lane consolidation candidates
project_goal.rationale: Logistics managers currently analyze freight spend manually in Excel; automated benchmarking will reduce analysis cycle from weeks to hours

target_users.value: Logistics managers and transportation analysts at mid-size manufacturers
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery sessions with three pilot customers

project_class.rationale: Browser-based analytics dashboard with file upload, analysis workflows, and visualization

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires Vue 3 dashboard, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL analytics, Firebase Auth, and carrier invoice parser integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Invoice parsing, lane normalization, and benchmark comparison are non-trivial but manageable within a standard web app architecture

risk_level.rationale: Handles financial invoice data; incorrect analysis could lead to misinformed rate negotiations but no direct transaction processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: Carrier invoice amounts, contracted rates, and spend benchmarks are financially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Logistics managers log in to view their organization's spend data; no public access

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with organization-scoped data isolation; logistics_manager and analyst roles with read-only analyst access
access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant with per-organization data isolation; analysts cannot upload invoices

feature_scope.value: CSV and EDI 210 invoice file upload and parsing, lane-level spend normalization, contract rate variance analysis, benchmark comparison against industry reference rates, cost reduction recommendation engine, mode shift analysis for parcel-to-LTL and LTL-to-FTL consolidation, lane consolidation candidate identification
feature_scope.rationale: Full analytics feature scope for initial product

mvp_scope.value: CSV invoice upload and parsing, lane-level spend database, contract rate variance analysis surfacing top 20 overspend lanes, basic spend dashboard with carrier and mode breakdowns
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable to demonstrate value to pilot customers in 8 weeks

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth with JWT validation on every API request, TLS 1.3, per-organization row-level security in PostgreSQL, no invoice content in logs
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires strict tenant isolation and authentication controls

frontend_stack.rationale: Team has Vue 3 expertise; Composition API suits the dashboard-heavy UI

backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for invoice parsing and analytics; FastAPI for async file upload handling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on GCP Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for invoice normalization and lane analytics; Cloud SQL managed HA

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container scaling for variable invoice upload and analysis workloads

privacy_retention_policy.value: Invoice data retained for contract term plus 7 years per SOX financial record requirements; customer-initiated deletion purges all invoice data within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX financial record retention for publicly traded manufacturer customers

future_scope.value: EDI 210 direct carrier feed integrations, AI-powered rate negotiation script generator, carbon emissions per lane calculation, multi-company consolidated view for 3PL customers
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 product roadmap

constraints.value: MVP in 8 weeks; initial deployment single GCP region (us-central1); invoice file size limit 50 MB per upload; no real-time carrier rate API integrations in MVP
constraints.rationale: Timeline and scope constraints agreed with pilot customers

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Operations (Cloud Logging and Cloud Monitoring), structured logging, PagerDuty for invoice processing failures
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native observability for Cloud Run workloads

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with pilot customer demo at end of each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Pilot customer feedback drives sprint priorities

compliance_context.value: SOX financial controls for freight cost reporting in publicly traded manufacturer customers; GDPR for EU-based logistics managers' personal data
compliance_context.rationale: Pilot customers include publicly traded manufacturers subject to SOX Section 404

project.goal: Freight spend analytics dashboard for logistics managers with carrier invoice upload and cost reduction recommendations
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks, full product in 16 weeks

frontend.state: Pinia for session state and upload progress

backend.parsing: pandas for CSV invoice parsing, custom EDI 210 parser

security.auth: Firebase Auth with JWT verification on every FastAPI request

data.analytics: PostgreSQL aggregation queries for lane-level spend analysis

devops.hosting: Cloud Run with Cloud SQL connector

reasoner_reviews.product: The cost reduction recommendation engine is the core value driver. Logistics managers need ranked recommendations with annualized savings estimates in dollar amounts, not percentages alone. The lane consolidation analysis must show which specific lanes can be combined and at what threshold volume, not just flag high-spend lanes abstractly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL is appropriate for the query patterns.
