The lane normalization step is the most complex: ZIP codes must be converted to carrier zone pairs for benchmark comparison, and carrier-specific accessorial charge tables must be stripped from invoice amounts before rate comparison. A dedicated invoice_normalization_jobs async Cloud Task queue will keep upload responses fast while normalization runs in the background.
reasoner_reviews.security: Row-level security in PostgreSQL must be enforced at the database level, not just application level, since financial invoice data cannot leak between organizations. Firebase JWT validation must be applied on every FastAPI endpoint without exception. Invoice file content should be stored in GCS, not PostgreSQL, to avoid large object storage costs and enable virus scanning.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 50 MB invoice file limit handles approximately 200,000 invoice line items per upload, which is sufficient for monthly uploads from mid-size manufacturers. Cloud Run max request timeout of 60 seconds means large file uploads must use signed GCS upload URLs, not direct API body uploads.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router; Pinia store for auth session, upload state, and active analysis filters; route-level code splitting for dashboard and upload views
frontend.pages_and_flows: LoginPage (Firebase Auth UI), InvoiceUploadPage (drag-drop with upload progress and parsing status polling), SpendDashboardPage (carrier breakdown, mode mix, top lanes by spend), LaneAnalysisPage (overspend lanes table with savings estimates), RecommendationsPage (ranked cost reduction actions), AdminPage (organization settings, user management)
frontend.state_management: Pinia: authStore (Firebase user, org_id), uploadStore (upload job status, parsing progress), analysisStore (active filters, selected date range, cached lane data)
frontend.performance_notes: Lane analysis table paginated at 100 rows; Chart.js spend charts lazy-loaded on tab activation; recommendation list virtualized for orgs with 500+ lane recommendations

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: /invoices (upload, parse, list), /analysis (lane spend, variance, benchmarks), /recommendations (cost reduction, mode shift, consolidation), /admin (org management)
backend.business_modules: InvoiceParser: CSV and EDI 210 parsers normalizing carrier-specific field names to canonical schema (origin_zip, dest_zip, carrier_scac, mode, charge_amount_cents, invoice_date, accessorial_codes_json). LaneNormalizer: converts ZIP codes to carrier zone pairs, strips accessorial charges to produce base_rate_cents per cwt. VarianceAnalyzer: joins normalized invoices against carrier_contracts to compute overspend_cents per lane. CostReductionEngine: ranks lanes by annualized_overspend_cents DESC, generates negotiation recommendation with target_rate and estimated_annual_saving_cents. ModeShiftAnalyzer: identifies parcel lanes with average weight over 150 lbs where LTL would be cheaper based on zone-pair LTL benchmark rates.
backend.failure_handling: Invoice upload uses GCS signed URL; parsing job runs as Cloud Tasks task with retry up to 3 times on transient errors; failed parsing jobs recorded with error detail in invoice_jobs table for user notification

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth issues JWT on login; FastAPI dependency verifies JWT signature and extracts org_id claim on every request; no org_id in URL path params to prevent enumeration
security.authorization_model: logistics_manager: upload invoices, view all analysis, export reports; analyst: view analysis and reports only, no upload; admin: manage org users and settings
security.privacy_controls: PostgreSQL RLS policy: organization_id = current_setting('app.org_id') applied to freight_invoices, normalized_invoice_lines, lane_spend_summaries tables; no cross-org queries possible via application role

data.entities: organizations (id, name, plan_tier, created_at). freight_invoices (id, org_id, original_filename, gcs_path, parse_status ENUM pending/parsing/complete/failed, invoice_count, upload_by, uploaded_at). normalized_invoice_lines (id, invoice_id, org_id, carrier_scac, mode ENUM parcel/ltl/ftl/intermodal, origin_zip, dest_zip, carrier_zone_pair, base_rate_cents BIGINT, accessorial_amount_cents BIGINT, total_charge_cents BIGINT, shipment_weight_lbs INT, invoice_date DATE). carrier_contracts (id, org_id, carrier_scac, lane_origin_zip, lane_dest_zip, mode, contracted_rate_cents BIGINT, effective_date DATE, expiry_date DATE). lane_spend_summaries (org_id, carrier_scac, mode, origin_zip, dest_zip, period_month DATE, total_spend_cents BIGINT, shipment_count INT, avg_weight_lbs INT, avg_rate_cents INT). cost_reduction_recommendations (id, org_id, recommendation_type ENUM rate_negotiation/mode_shift/consolidation, carrier_scac, lane_key, annualized_saving_cents BIGINT, current_rate_cents, benchmark_rate_cents, generated_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (db-custom-2-7680) with RLS enabled. GCS bucket for invoice file storage with CMEK encryption and 7-year retention lifecycle. Cloud Tasks queue for async invoice parsing jobs.
data.analytics_events: lane_spend_summaries materialized monthly by Cloud Scheduler trigger; cost_reduction_recommendations regenerated weekly or on new invoice upload completion

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (api): min 1 instance, max 10 instances, 2 vCPU/4 GB, Cloud SQL connector via Unix socket. Cloud Run Job (invoice-parser): triggered by Cloud Tasks on invoice upload. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private IP and Cloud SQL Proxy.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: ruff lint + pytest (85% coverage gate) + Docker build + Artifact Registry push + Cloud Run deploy. Separate Cloud Build trigger for invoice-parser job image.
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Logging with structured JSON logs including org_id (not invoice content). Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: invoice_parse_duration_seconds, recommendation_generation_latency, lane_analysis_query_duration. PagerDuty: invoice parsing job failure after 3 retries, Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion.

target_output.thinking_summary: Freight spend analytics web app with moderate complexity. Key decisions: GCS signed URL for large invoice file uploads to stay within Cloud Run 60s timeout, PostgreSQL RLS for org-level financial data isolation, async Cloud Tasks parsing job to keep upload response fast, and lane normalization via zone-pair conversion before benchmark comparison. SOX compliance requires 7-year invoice retention and org-isolated audit trail.
target_output.title: Freight Spend Analytics Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A Vue 3 and FastAPI web application deployed on GCP Cloud Run that allows logistics managers to upload carrier invoice exports, normalize shipment data to lane-level spend records, and identify cost reduction opportunities through contract rate variance analysis, mode shift recommendations, and lane consolidation candidates. Invoice parsing runs asynchronously via Cloud Tasks; lane spend summaries are materialized monthly in PostgreSQL; cost reduction recommendations are ranked by annualized savings potential and presented in a prioritized dashboard.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA served via Cloud CDN communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI service on Cloud Run. Invoice uploads use GCS signed URLs generated by the API, bypassing Cloud Run body size limits for files up to 50 MB. Upload completion triggers a Cloud Tasks message that invokes the invoice-parser Cloud Run Job. The parser normalizes CSV and EDI 210 invoice lines into normalized_invoice_lines in PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with row-level security enforcing per-organization isolation. A weekly Cloud Scheduler job regenerates cost_reduction_recommendations by comparing normalized lane rates against carrier_contracts and benchmark reference rates. Firebase Auth JWT validation is enforced on every FastAPI endpoint.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router, Chart.js for spend visualizations. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2, google-cloud-tasks for Cloud Tasks dispatch, firebase-admin for JWT validation, pandas for CSV parsing, custom EDI 210 parser. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with RLS. Storage: GCS for invoice files with CMEK encryption. Hosting: Cloud Run for API and invoice-parser job. CI/CD: Cloud Build with Artifact Registry. Monitoring: Google Cloud Operations with Cloud Logging and Cloud Monitoring.
target_output.functional_feature_map: InvoiceUploadFlow: logistics_manager selects file in Vue InvoiceUploadPage; FastAPI POST /invoices/upload-url returns GCS signed URL and invoice_job_id; Vue uploads directly to GCS; on GCS upload completion trigger, Cloud Tasks enqueues parser job; Vue polls GET /invoices/{id}/status until complete or failed. InvoiceParser: reads GCS file, detects CSV or EDI 210 format, normalizes each line to canonical schema stripping accessorial codes, writes to normalized_invoice_lines with org_id, persists to freight_invoices with parse status. LaneSpendSummary: Cloud Scheduler monthly job aggregates normalized_invoice_lines to lane_spend_summaries by carrier_scac, mode, origin_zip, dest_zip, period_month. VarianceAnalyzer: joins lane_spend_summaries against carrier_contracts for matching carrier/lane/mode, computes overspend_cents per lane. CostReductionEngine: ranks lanes by annualized_overspend_cents DESC, generates recommendation records with target_rate_cents and estimated_annual_saving_cents. ModeShiftAnalyzer: queries parcel lanes where avg_weight_lbs over 150, estimates LTL cost using benchmark zone-pair rates, computes mode_shift_saving_cents.
target_output.system_components: Cloud CDN and Cloud Load Balancing: serves Vue SPA static assets from GCS bucket, routes /api/* to Cloud Run. Cloud Run api service (min 1, max 10 instances, 2 vCPU/4 GB): FastAPI handling auth, invoice management, analysis queries, recommendation retrieval. Cloud Run Job invoice-parser (triggered by Cloud Tasks): reads GCS invoice file, normalizes to PostgreSQL. Cloud Tasks queue invoice-parsing-tasks: at-least-once delivery with 3 retries. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP, db-custom-2-7680): freight_invoices, normalized_invoice_lines, lane_spend_summaries, carrier_contracts, cost_reduction_recommendations, organizations tables with RLS. GCS bucket parcel-freight-invoices: CMEK-encrypted invoice file storage with 7-year lifecycle rule. Firebase Auth: user identity and org_id claim. Cloud Scheduler: monthly lane summary materialization and weekly recommendation regeneration triggers.
target_output.workflows: Invoice Upload: logistics_manager uploads CSV or EDI 210 file via Vue drag-drop; FastAPI generates GCS signed URL and creates freight_invoices record with status=pending; Vue uploads file directly to GCS (up to 50 MB within GCS limits); GCS event notification triggers Cloud Tasks task; invoice-parser Cloud Run Job fetches file, parses line by line, writes normalized_invoice_lines with org_id RLS protection, updates freight_invoices status to complete; Vue polling returns success. Cost Reduction Analysis: analyst opens RecommendationsPage; FastAPI GET /recommendations?type=rate_negotiation returns cost_reduction_recommendations ordered by annualized_saving_cents DESC; each recommendation shows carrier_scac, lane, current vs benchmark rate, estimated_annual_saving_cents; analyst can export to CSV for carrier negotiation preparation. Mode Shift Analysis: logistics_manager opens LaneAnalysisPage and selects mode shift tab; FastAPI queries parcel lanes with avg_weight_lbs over 150 lbs and computes LTL equivalent cost using benchmark zone-pair rates; returns ModeShiftCandidate list with parcel_annual_spend_cents, ltl_estimated_spend_cents, saving_cents sorted by saving_cents DESC.
target_output.data_model: organizations (id UUID PK, name, plan_tier, created_at). freight_invoices (id UUID PK, org_id UUID, original_filename, gcs_path, parse_status TEXT, invoice_line_count INT, error_detail TEXT, uploaded_by UUID, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- RLS: org_id = current_setting('app.org_id'). normalized_invoice_lines (id UUID PK, invoice_id UUID, org_id UUID, carrier_scac CHAR(4), mode TEXT, origin_zip CHAR(5), dest_zip CHAR(5), carrier_zone_pair TEXT, base_rate_cents BIGINT, total_charge_cents BIGINT, shipment_weight_lbs INT, invoice_date DATE) -- RLS: org_id. carrier_contracts (id UUID PK, org_id UUID, carrier_scac, mode, origin_zip, dest_zip, contracted_rate_cents BIGINT, effective_date DATE, expiry_date DATE) -- RLS: org_id. lane_spend_summaries (org_id UUID, carrier_scac, mode, origin_zip, dest_zip, period_month DATE, total_spend_cents BIGINT, shipment_count INT, avg_weight_lbs INT, avg_rate_cents INT, PRIMARY KEY (org_id, carrier_scac, mode, origin_zip, dest_zip, period_month)). cost_reduction_recommendations (id UUID PK, org_id UUID, recommendation_type TEXT, carrier_scac, lane_key TEXT, annualized_saving_cents BIGINT, current_rate_cents INT, benchmark_rate_cents INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- RLS: org_id.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI REST at /api/v1. POST /invoices/upload-url {filename, content_type} -> {upload_url, invoice_id} (logistics_manager role). GET /invoices -> list of freight_invoices for org. GET /invoices/{id}/status -> parse status and line count. GET /analysis/lane-spend?carrier=&mode=&from=&to= -> paginated lane_spend_summaries. GET /analysis/variance -> overspend lanes joined with carrier_contracts ordered by overspend_cents DESC. GET /recommendations?type=rate_negotiation|mode_shift|consolidation -> cost_reduction_recommendations ordered by annualized_saving_cents DESC. POST /admin/contracts (bulk upload carrier contracts CSV). All endpoints require Firebase JWT in Authorization header; org_id extracted from token claims and set as app.org_id session variable before each query.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT verification on every FastAPI endpoint via firebase-admin verify_id_token; org_id extracted from custom claim and set via SET LOCAL app.org_id before each PostgreSQL query. PostgreSQL RLS policies on freight_invoices, normalized_invoice_lines, lane_spend_summaries, carrier_contracts, cost_reduction_recommendations enforce organization isolation at database level. GCS invoice files stored with CMEK encryption and accessed only via invoice-parser service account. No invoice line content in structured logs; only invoice_id, org_id, and status logged. SOX compliance: freight_invoices and normalized_invoice_lines retained 7 years via GCS lifecycle rule on associated files and PostgreSQL archival to GCS Parquet after 2 years. GDPR: user email and name stored only in Firebase Auth; PostgreSQL stores org_id UUID reference only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build triggers: ruff lint, mypy, pytest (85% coverage gate for InvoiceParser, VarianceAnalyzer, CostReductionEngine), Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run deploy (api service), Cloud Run Job update (invoice-parser). Environments: dev (Cloud Run min-instances 0, Cloud SQL dev instance), staging (same topology as production with anonymized invoice data), production (min 1 Cloud Run instance, Cloud SQL with automated backups and PITR). Secrets via Secret Manager: Firebase service account key, Cloud SQL credentials. Cloud SQL automated backup: daily with 30-day retention, PITR to 5-minute granularity.
target_output.observability: Cloud Logging structured JSON with fields: org_id, invoice_id, operation, duration_ms, row_count; no invoice amounts or carrier names in log fields. Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: invoice_parse_job_duration_seconds (histogram), recommendation_generation_latency_seconds, lane_variance_query_duration_ms. Cloud Monitoring alerts: invoice-parser Cloud Run Job failure rate over 20%, Cloud SQL connection pool over 80%, Cloud Run 5xx error rate over 1%. PagerDuty escalation for invoice-parser job failure after 3 retries and Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run api: 1 minimum instance plus autoscaling; at 1,000 monthly active users estimated $150/month. Cloud SQL db-custom-2-7680: approximately $200/month. Cloud Tasks: negligible at invoice volumes. GCS storage at 50 GB invoice files: approximately $10/month. Cloud Build: approximately $20/month. Total estimated $400/month for pilot phase. Scaling: Cloud Run autoscaling handles concurrent upload and analysis traffic; Cloud SQL scales vertically for query-heavy analysis workloads; normalized_invoice_lines table partitioned by invoice_date for query performance at multi-year history.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Firebase Auth with org isolation, CSV invoice upload via GCS signed URL, invoice-parser Cloud Run Job with CSV normalization, lane_spend_summaries materialization, contract rate variance analysis for top overspend lanes, basic Vue 3 spend dashboard with carrier and mode charts. Phase 2 (4 weeks): EDI 210 invoice parser, cost_reduction_recommendations engine with annualized savings estimates, RecommendationsPage in Vue with export to CSV, mode shift analysis with LTL benchmark comparison. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Lane consolidation candidate identification, Cloud Scheduler automated weekly recommendation refresh, SOX-compliant audit log for invoice upload and access events, GDPR data deletion workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff and mypy strict mode; InvoiceParser unit-tested with fixture CSV and EDI 210 files covering accessorial stripping and zone-pair normalization edge cases. 85% line coverage gate in CI. PostgreSQL RLS policies tested in integration tests by asserting cross-org queries return empty result sets. Vue: Pinia store actions unit-tested with vitest; upload flow E2E tested with Playwright including GCS signed URL mock. No invoice amounts or carrier SCAC codes in structured log fields.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Carrier invoice format variation causing InvoiceParser to silently misclassify charges. Mitigation: parser logs unrecognized field names per carrier_scac for analyst review; anomaly detection flags invoices where base_rate_cents is zero or accessorial_amount_cents exceeds 50% of total_charge_cents. Risk: Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion under concurrent analysis queries during peak usage. Mitigation: pgbouncer connection pooling via Cloud SQL Proxy; query timeout of 30s on analysis endpoints. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL aggregation queries over dedicated OLAP store accepted for MVP simplicity at the cost of slower complex cross-carrier analyses at multi-year data volumes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GCS signed URL upload chosen over direct FastAPI body upload to stay within Cloud Run 60-second request timeout for 50 MB files. PostgreSQL RLS chosen for org isolation over application-layer filtering to prevent any bypass risk on financial data. Async Cloud Tasks invoice parsing chosen over synchronous upload handling to keep upload response under 2 seconds. Lane normalization via carrier_zone_pair key chosen over raw ZIP code matching to enable benchmark comparison across carriers with different zone definitions.

metadata.notes: Freight spend analytics web app with Vue 3, FastAPI, PostgreSQL RLS multi-tenancy, and GCS signed URL invoice upload.

profile.domain: 3PL Inventory Data Pipeline

project_goal.value: Transform raw inventory adjustment events from six WMS systems into a unified real-time inventory position feed consumed by customer-facing inventory APIs, reducing query latency from minutes to seconds
project_goal.rationale: Core business goal: eliminate per-WMS polling and provide sub-second inventory reads to customers

target_users.value: 3PL customer-facing inventory APIs and internal pipeline engineers
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are downstream API services; engineers monitor and operate the pipeline

project_class.rationale: Pure streaming and batch data transformation with no user-facing UI

capabilities.rationale: CDC ingestion, Flink stream processing, MongoDB write, Redis cache layer

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source CDC fan-in, stateful stream aggregation, cache coherence across six WMS connectors

risk_level.rationale: Inventory position errors affect 3PL customer SLAs but not financial transactions directly

data_sensitivity.rationale: Inventory counts and SKU positions are internal operational data, not customer PII or financials

external_exposure.rationale: Inventory position API accessed by authenticated 3PL customer integrations only

access_model.value: Service account tokens scoped per customer account; pipeline operators via GCP service accounts
access_model.rationale: Each customer account token restricts reads to their own SKU-location positions

feature_scope.value: CDC ingestion from 6 WMS connectors, Flink streaming aggregation, MongoDB position store, Redis read cache, nightly reconciliation job, pipeline monitoring dashboards
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline feature set required for GA launch

mvp_scope.value: Two WMS connectors live, Flink streaming aggregation, MongoDB write, Redis cache, basic reconciliation
mvp_scope.rationale: Proves latency reduction with two largest WMS clients before expanding connectors

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all data in transit; GCP service account authentication; customer tokens with per-account scoping; no PHI or financial data
security_baseline.rationale: Internal operational data requires transport encryption and proper access isolation

frontend_stack.rationale: Data pipeline has no user interface; monitoring via Grafana dashboards

backend_stack.value: Scala 3 with Apache Flink on Dataproc; Cloud Run for reconciliation and API adapter services
backend_stack.rationale: Flink on Dataproc for stateful streaming; Cloud Run for lightweight batch and API services

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for inventory position store; Redis 7 for read cache; GCS for checkpoint and CDC staging
data_platform.rationale: MongoDB document model fits SKU-location position structure; Redis for sub-10ms API read path

hosting_target.value: Dataproc for Flink cluster; Cloud Run for reconciliation jobs; GCP us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Flink execution on Dataproc eliminates cluster operations overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Inventory positions retained 90 days; raw CDC events retained 30 days in GCS; pipeline audit logs 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational data retention aligned with 3PL client SLA contracts

future_scope.value: Real-time inventory reservation API, predictive low-stock alerts, additional WMS connectors beyond six
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 product roadmap items contingent on GA performance

constraints.value: Sub-10ms p99 read latency for inventory API; six WMS connectors must all be live at GA; pipeline lag must stay under 30 seconds end-to-end
constraints.rationale: Customer SLA requirements for real-time inventory visibility

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Flink job metrics; pipeline lag and throughput dashboards; PagerDuty for lag breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Streaming pipeline health requires continuous lag monitoring

execution_preference.value: Iterative connector rollout: two WMS at MVP, four more in subsequent sprints
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces risk by validating CDC patterns per WMS before full rollout

project.goal: Sub-second inventory position reads from unified 3PL pipeline
project.target_users: Customer inventory APIs and pipeline engineers

backend.framework: Apache Flink Datastream API, Cloud Run for reconciliation

security.auth: GCP service accounts for Dataproc; customer bearer tokens for API layer

data.db: MongoDB 7 for positions, GCS for CDC staging and checkpoints

devops.ci: Cloud Build pipeline; automated Flink job jar build and deploy

reasoner_reviews.product: The core value proposition is valid: eliminating per-WMS polling by maintaining a continuously updated position store dramatically reduces API latency. The nightly reconciliation is a necessary safety net for the streaming pipeline, but the reconciliation cadence should be configurable to catch drift sooner during WMS connector rollout.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flink on Dataproc is well-suited for stateful aggregation across six CDC feeds. Key design risk is partition key choice for the Flink keyed stream: keying by SKU+location_id ensures correct per-position state but requires careful repartitioning to avoid hotspots on high-velocity SKUs. MongoDB document model is a good fit for position reads but write amplification during reconciliation bulk-updates needs evaluation.
reasoner_reviews.security: Customer token scoping to their own account namespace must be enforced at the MongoDB query level with a mandatory tenant_id filter, not just at the API gateway. CDC feeds from WMS systems should be treated as untrusted inputs and validated against expected schema before entering the Flink pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The sub-30-second end-to-end lag constraint is achievable with Flink but requires checkpoint intervals tuned to 10 seconds or less and Kafka-backed CDC with low-latency configurations. The 10ms p99 Redis read target is achievable only if cache hit rates exceed 90 percent for hot SKUs.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The flawed plan from round 1 omitted tenant isolation enforcement at the MongoDB layer, relying solely on API gateway filtering. This is a critical gap: a misconfigured token could expose cross-customer inventory positions. The reconciliation job also lacked schema: it must define what constitutes a discrepancy and at what threshold it pages engineers.

backend.service_design: Flink Datastream job per WMS connector consuming CDC events from a dedicated Pub/Sub subscription; position aggregation operator maintains keyed state per SKU+warehouse_location_id and upserts to MongoDB via JDBC sink; Cloud Run reconciliation service triggers nightly diff against WMS snapshot APIs
backend.background_jobs: Nightly reconciliation Cloud Run job fetches WMS full inventory snapshots, compares against MongoDB positions, logs discrepancies to structured JSON, and publishes mismatch count metrics to Prometheus; ad-hoc reconciliation triggerable via internal API
backend.failure_handling: Flink checkpoints every 10 seconds to GCS; failed CDC message retries with exponential backoff before routing to dead-letter Pub/Sub topic; reconciliation job sends PagerDuty alert if mismatch rate exceeds 0.5 percent of total positions or single SKU delta exceeds 1000 units
backend.scaling_notes: Flink parallelism tuned per WMS connector based on event throughput; Dataproc auto-scales on CPU and Pub/Sub backlog metrics; Redis cluster scaled on memory consumption by hot SKU set size

security.auth_design: GCP service accounts for Dataproc and Cloud Run workload identity; customer-facing API tokens are short-lived JWTs signed by internal auth service with customer_account_id claim
security.authorization_model: MongoDB queryFilter role binding appends mandatory customer_account_id predicate to all position reads; Pub/Sub subscription IAM restricts each WMS connector service account to its dedicated topic
security.secrets_management: WMS CDC credentials in GCP Secret Manager; rotated quarterly; Flink jobs access via workload identity binding with no long-lived credentials in environment variables
security.security_risks: Cross-tenant data leakage if MongoDB tenant_id filter is omitted in any query path; CDC feed injection if WMS connector does not validate event schema before entering Flink pipeline

data.entities: inventory_adjustments (wms_id, sku_id, warehouse_location_id, customer_account_id, delta_quantity, event_type ENUM receive/pick/damage/cycle_count, event_timestamp, source_sequence). sku_positions document store (customer_account_id [leading index], sku_id, warehouse_location_id, on_hand_quantity, last_updated_at, pipeline_sequence). reconciliation_discrepancies (run_id, customer_account_id, sku_id, warehouse_location_id, pipeline_quantity, wms_quantity, delta, mismatch_pct, detected_at).
data.storage_design: MongoDB with compound index on (customer_account_id, sku_id, warehouse_location_id) for O(1) position lookups; Redis key pattern pos:{account}:{sku}:{location} with 5-minute TTL; GCS for Flink checkpoints and CDC staging files with 30-day retention
data.retention_and_deletion: Inventory positions 90 days; raw CDC events in GCS 30 days with lifecycle rule to Coldline after 7 days; reconciliation discrepancy records 1 year
data.data_risks: State divergence between Flink in-memory state and MongoDB if a task manager crashes mid-checkpoint; mitigated by idempotent upsert logic using CDC event sequence numbers

devops.deployment_topology: Dataproc cluster running Flink 1.18 HA with 3 job managers; Cloud Run reconciliation service auto-scaled to zero; MongoDB Atlas M30 in us-central1; Redis Memorystore 10GB
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build triggers on main: compile Scala fat jar, run unit tests with Flink MiniCluster, publish jar to GCS artifact bucket, update Dataproc job config via Terraform
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Flink REST API for throughput, checkpoint duration, and Pub/Sub consumer lag per connector; Grafana pipeline dashboards; PagerDuty for lag and reconciliation alerts
devops.rollback_strategy: Flink savepoint captured before any job upgrade; rollback by submitting prior jar from GCS with savepoint restore path

ISS-001.title: Missing tenant isolation at MongoDB query layer
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan enforced customer scoping only at the API gateway. A direct MongoDB query or misconfigured service could return cross-customer inventory positions without a mandatory tenant_id predicate.

history.note: Identified in security review: no MongoDB-level tenant filter documented

ISS-002.title: Reconciliation job undefined discrepancy threshold and alert criteria
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 reconciliation job stated it logs discrepancies but did not define what delta constitutes actionable versus acceptable float, or at what mismatch rate PagerDuty alerts fire.

history.note: Identified by critic: reconciliation SLA left undefined

ISS-003.title: Flink keyed stream partition key not specified
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 architecture did not document the Flink keyed stream partition key strategy, leaving open hotspot risk on high-velocity SKUs if keying is done solely by SKU without location.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner review

focus_issues.title: Missing tenant isolation at MongoDB query layer
focus_issues.detail: No mandatory tenant_id predicate enforced at MongoDB query level; cross-customer data leakage risk.

focus_issues.title: Reconciliation job undefined discrepancy threshold and alert criteria
focus_issues.detail: Reconciliation job lacks defined mismatch threshold and PagerDuty trigger criteria.

latest_recommendations: Enforce mandatory tenant_id filter at MongoDB query level in all position read paths
Define reconciliation discrepancy threshold in absolute quantity and percentage terms with explicit PagerDuty trigger criteria
Document Flink keyed stream partition key as composite SKU+warehouse_location_id and explain hotspot mitigation strategy

previous_audits.summary: Pipeline architecture is directionally correct but a critical tenant isolation gap at the MongoDB layer and an underspecified reconciliation job prevent acceptance. Flink streaming design needs partition key documentation.

Redis read-through cache design will achieve sub-10ms latency for hot SKUs
CDC event schema and Pub/Sub topic isolation per WMS connector is well-structured

Reconciliation job discrepancy threshold and PagerDuty trigger undefined
Flink keyed stream partition strategy absent from architecture documentation

ISS-002: reconciliation job has no defined acceptance criteria for mismatch rate

Define reconciliation discrepancy threshold at 0.5 percent of total positions with PagerDuty alert criteria
Specify Flink partition key as composite SKU+warehouse_location_id in architecture_overview and system_components

issue_updates.title: Missing tenant isolation at MongoDB query layer

previous_plan.title: 3PL Real-Time Inventory Position Pipeline - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Scala/Flink streaming pipeline ingesting CDC events from six WMS systems, aggregating inventory positions into MongoDB, and fronting reads with a Redis cache to achieve sub-second customer API latency.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Flink Datastream jobs consume WMS CDC events from Pub/Sub, aggregate per-SKU-location positions, and write to MongoDB. Redis cache fronts API reads. Tenant isolation enforced at API gateway only.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: API gateway enforces customer token scoping. TLS 1.3 in transit. GCP service accounts for workload identity. No MongoDB-level tenant filter documented.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Dataproc Flink cluster with GCS checkpoints. Cloud Run for reconciliation. Nightly reconciliation job compares WMS snapshots to MongoDB but mismatch threshold undefined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing ISS-001 by enforcing mandatory MongoDB tenant_id predicate via queryFilter role binding so no code path can return cross-customer positions. Fixing ISS-002 by specifying reconciliation mismatch threshold at 0.5 percent with explicit PagerDuty P2/P1 escalation tiers. Intentionally introducing two subtle flaws per the manifest plan_quality=flawed directive: architecture_overview cites sub-5ms Redis target inconsistent with the sub-10ms contract constraint, and the Flink checkpoint interval is omitted from deployment_and_operations despite the constraints reviewer requiring it to be under 10 seconds.

fix_report.action_taken: Added MongoDB queryFilter role binding on sku_positions collection enforcing customer_account_id as mandatory leading predicate on all reads; documented compound index (customer_account_id, sku_id, warehouse_location_id) in data_model; added query-layer enforcement description to security_and_compliance
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-customer data leakage eliminated; MongoDB rejects any query path missing tenant_id predicate at authorization layer

fix_report.action_taken: Defined reconciliation discrepancy threshold at 0.5 percent of total active positions per customer account; specified PagerDuty P2 fires on two consecutive runs above threshold; P1 escalation if any single SKU delta exceeds 1000 units; documented in deployment_and_operations and observability sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Reconciliation job has quantitative acceptance criteria; on-call engineers receive actionable alerts with defined severity tiers

target_output.title: 3PL Real-Time Inventory Position Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Scala/Flink streaming data pipeline on GCP Dataproc ingesting inventory adjustment CDC events from six WMS connectors via Cloud Pub/Sub, aggregating real-time on-hand positions per SKU-location pair keyed by composite SKU+warehouse_location_id, and materializing results into MongoDB 7 with mandatory per-tenant query enforcement. A Redis 7 read-through cache fronts the inventory position API targeting sub-5ms reads for hot SKUs. A nightly Cloud Run reconciliation job validates pipeline accuracy against WMS full snapshots with a defined 0.5 percent mismatch threshold and automated PagerDuty P2/P1 escalation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Six WMS systems emit inventory adjustment events via CDC connectors into dedicated Cloud Pub/Sub topics. A Flink Datastream application on Dataproc consumes all six subscriptions, validates event schema, and routes into a keyed stream partitioned by composite key SKU+warehouse_location_id to prevent hotspots on high-velocity items. The aggregation operator maintains stateful on-hand quantity per key and upserts positions to MongoDB via JDBC sink with sequence-number deduplication for idempotency. API reads are served from Redis 7 read-through cache targeting sub-5ms latency for hot SKUs; cache misses fall through to MongoDB with mandatory customer_account_id predicate enforced at the MongoDB role layer. Note: this architecture targets sub-5ms Redis reads, which is more aggressive than the contracted sub-10ms constraint.
target_output.technology_stack: Stream processor: Scala 3 with Apache Flink 1.18 on GCP Dataproc 2.1. CDC ingestion: Debezium 2.5 for SQL-backed WMS; custom Python 3.12 adapters on Cloud Run for proprietary WMS APIs. Position store: MongoDB 7 Atlas on GCP us-central1 M30. Read cache: Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore 10GB. Checkpoints and staging: GCS with Coldline lifecycle after 7 days. Reconciliation: Python 3.12 on Cloud Run. CI/CD: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus with Flink JMX exporter.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CDC Ingestion: dedicated Pub/Sub topic and Flink source per WMS; schema validator rejects malformed events to dead-letter topic before pipeline entry. Stream Aggregation: Flink keyed stream on SKU+warehouse_location_id accumulates inventory deltas and upserts sku_positions on each event. Tenant Isolation: MongoDB compound index leading with customer_account_id; queryFilter role binding mandatory on all read paths. Redis Cache: read-through keyed as pos:{account}:{sku}:{location} with 5-minute TTL, invalidated on Flink write. Reconciliation Engine: nightly comparison against WMS snapshots with 0.5 percent mismatch threshold and PagerDuty escalation. Pipeline Monitoring: per-WMS throughput, lag, and checkpoint duration in Grafana.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Pub/Sub: 6 topics with 7-day retention and dedicated subscriptions. Flink Datastream Job on Dataproc: schema validator operator, keyed aggregation operator with stateful position accumulation, MongoDB JDBC sink with sequence deduplication. MongoDB 7 Atlas: sku_positions collection with compound index (customer_account_id, sku_id, warehouse_location_id) unique, write concern majority, queryFilter role binding. Redis 7 Memorystore: 10GB read-through cache cluster. Cloud Run Reconciliation Service: Python job diffing WMS snapshots against MongoDB. GCS: Flink checkpoint storage and CDC event archives. Prometheus + Grafana: pipeline lag, throughput, checkpoint duration dashboards.
target_output.workflows: Inventory Adjustment Flow: WMS emits stock change on pick, receive, damage, or cycle count. CDC connector captures event and publishes to dedicated Pub/Sub topic with event_type, sku_id, warehouse_location_id, customer_account_id, delta_quantity, source_sequence. Flink validates schema, routes to keyed stream partitioned by SKU+warehouse_location_id. Aggregation operator accumulates delta into position state and writes idempotent upsert to MongoDB sku_positions. Redis cache entry for the affected position key is invalidated. Customer API reads Redis in under 5ms for cached positions; cold reads fall through to MongoDB with mandatory tenant predicate. Reconciliation Flow: Cloud Scheduler triggers Cloud Run job nightly at 02:00 UTC. Job fetches full WMS inventory snapshot per customer account, loads into pandas dataframe, queries MongoDB for matching positions with customer_account_id filter, computes absolute and percentage delta per SKU-location. If mismatch rate exceeds 0.5 percent of account total positions, fires PagerDuty P2 with discrepancy CSV attached. Two consecutive P2 runs escalate to P1. Any single SKU delta exceeding 1000 units fires P1 immediately.
target_output.data_model: inventory_adjustments (wms_id VARCHAR, sku_id VARCHAR, warehouse_location_id VARCHAR, customer_account_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, delta_quantity INT, event_type ENUM receive/pick/damage/cycle_count/transfer, event_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, source_sequence BIGINT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sku_positions MongoDB collection: {_id ObjectId, customer_account_id String [compound index position 1], sku_id String [position 2], warehouse_location_id String [position 3], on_hand_quantity Int, last_updated_at ISODate, pipeline_sequence Long}. Unique compound index enforces one position doc per tenant+SKU+location. MongoDB role customerPositionReader has queryFilter {customer_account_id: '$$USER.account_id'} on sku_positions collection. pipeline_checkpoints (job_id, checkpoint_id, committed_pub_sub_offset, checkpoint_timestamp). reconciliation_discrepancies (run_id UUID, customer_account_id VARCHAR, sku_id VARCHAR, warehouse_location_id VARCHAR, pipeline_quantity INT, wms_quantity INT, delta INT, mismatch_pct NUMERIC, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Internal Cloud Run management API: GET /health returns {flink_job_status, last_checkpoint_at, lag_seconds_by_connector}. POST /reconciliation/trigger body {customer_account_id, date_range} initiates ad-hoc reconciliation run, returns {run_id}. GET /reconciliation/runs/{run_id} returns {status, mismatch_count, total_positions, mismatch_pct, discrepancies: [...]}. GET /metrics Prometheus scrape endpoint returning lag_seconds{connector}, events_processed_total{connector}, checkpoint_duration_ms, reconciliation_mismatch_count{account}. No external API surface; downstream inventory position API is a separate service consuming Redis and MongoDB.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Tenant isolation enforced at two layers: API gateway validates customer JWT and extracts customer_account_id claim; MongoDB queryFilter role binding appends customer_account_id equality predicate to every sku_positions query for the position-read service account, making bypass impossible without role reassignment. GCP workload identity: Flink on Dataproc and Cloud Run services use attached service accounts with minimum IAM permissions; no long-lived credentials in environment variables. WMS CDC credentials in GCP Secret Manager with quarterly rotation. TLS 1.3 enforced on all Pub/Sub, MongoDB Atlas private endpoint, and Redis Memorystore connections. Dead-letter Pub/Sub topic for malformed CDC events reviewed daily by pipeline engineers; events with missing customer_account_id rejected before any state mutation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Dataproc cluster: 1 HA job manager pair on n1-standard-4, 4-8 auto-scaling task managers on n2-standard-4 scaled on Pub/Sub backlog lag metric; sustained use mode 06:00-22:00 UTC to avoid 3-5 minute cold start. Flink job submitted via Cloud Build on merge to main: compile Scala fat jar, run Flink MiniCluster unit tests, publish jar to GCS, update Dataproc job config via Terraform, capture savepoint before upgrade. Cloud Run reconciliation: zero-scale, triggered by Cloud Scheduler at 02:00 UTC nightly. MongoDB Atlas M30 in us-central1 with 3-node replica set write concern majority. Redis Memorystore 10GB standard with replica. Environments: dev (single task manager, M10 MongoDB), staging (2 task managers, production-identical schema and IAM), production. Terraform manages all GCP, Atlas, and Memorystore resources. Note: Flink checkpoint interval configuration is not specified in this deployment section.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Flink REST API every 15 seconds for: records_in_per_second per connector source, checkpoint_duration_ms, consumer_lag_records per Pub/Sub subscription, MongoDB sink write_latency_p99. Grafana dashboards: per-WMS event throughput, aggregated pipeline lag (SLA <30s), MongoDB write latency p99, Redis cache hit rate percentage. PagerDuty alerts: pipeline lag >30s sustained 5 minutes fires P2; Flink job failure fires P1 immediately; reconciliation mismatch_pct >0.5 percent fires P2 with discrepancy report attached; two consecutive P2 reconciliation alerts escalate to P1; single SKU delta >1000 units fires P1. Structured JSON logs from Cloud Run include run_id, customer_account_id, mismatch_count, total_positions, mismatch_pct for each reconciliation run.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Dataproc sustained-mode cluster at 4 n2-standard-4 task managers approximately $1,800/month; scales to 8 during peak WMS activity windows. MongoDB Atlas M30 approximately $600/month. Redis Memorystore 10GB approximately $250/month. Cloud Run reconciliation near-zero at zero-scale. GCS storage for CDC archives and checkpoints approximately $80/month. Flink parallelism scales horizontally by adding task managers; Pub/Sub partition count increased before scaling to avoid rebalancing spikes on high-throughput connectors.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Two largest WMS CDC connectors live with Pub/Sub ingestion; Flink aggregation job with tenant-isolated MongoDB writes enforced via queryFilter role; Redis read-through cache; reconciliation job with 0.5 percent threshold and PagerDuty P2/P1 tiers; Grafana pipeline lag and throughput dashboards. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Four remaining WMS connectors added and validated with per-connector integration tests; dead-letter queue monitoring and replay tooling; Flink savepoint-based upgrade process tested in staging. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Flink parallelism auto-scaling on Pub/Sub backlog metric enabled; Redis cluster sizing reviewed against hot SKU growth; reconciliation cadence made configurable per customer account SLA tier.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Flink operators implement CheckpointedFunction for state serialization compatibility across upgrades. CDC event schema versioned and validated against schema registry before Pub/Sub publish. MongoDB position writes use upsert with pipeline_sequence check for idempotency against Pub/Sub at-least-once delivery. All MongoDB queries in position-read paths audited quarterly to confirm customer_account_id predicate presence. Cloud Build enforces Flink MiniCluster unit tests for aggregation logic before any Dataproc deploy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Dataproc cluster preemption during peak inventory adjustment windows causing pipeline lag spike above 30-second SLA. Mitigation: sustained use mode during business hours; Flink HA job manager recovers from last GCS savepoint automatically. Risk: MongoDB write throughput saturation on high-velocity SKUs during peak WMS batch operations. Mitigation: Flink mini-batch sink groups upserts per checkpoint interval rather than per-event to reduce write amplification. Tradeoff: Redis TTL of 5 minutes means cold SKU positions can be up to 5 minutes stale for infrequent reads; acceptable given 30-second pipeline lag SLA applies to hot paths only. Risk: WMS snapshot API rate limits causing reconciliation job timeouts for large accounts. Mitigation: reconciliation fetches accounts sequentially with configurable per-account delay and partial run recovery.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected MongoDB over PostgreSQL for sku_positions due to document model flexibility for per-SKU metadata extensions without migrations. Selected Dataproc over self-managed Kubernetes Flink to eliminate cluster upgrade overhead. Decided Redis TTL of 5 minutes balances cache freshness against memory cost for the expected 2M active SKU-location keys across all customer accounts.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: architecture_overview cites sub-5ms Redis target inconsistent with sub-10ms contract; Flink checkpoint interval omitted from deployment_and_operations despite constraints reviewer requirement.

profile.domain: Regional Parcel Carrier Shipment Tracking Website

project_goal.value: Public shipment status website displaying live tracking timelines, estimated delivery windows, and exception notifications for consumers, with an embeddable JavaScript widget for third-party e-commerce merchants
project_goal.rationale: Core product: give consumers and merchant partners a branded tracking experience without building their own integrations

target_users.value: End consumers tracking parcels and e-commerce merchants embedding the tracking widget in their order pages
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user segments: direct consumer lookup and programmatic merchant embed

project_class.rationale: Static Astro build deployed to CDN; no server-side runtime; all live data fetched client-side from public tracking API

capabilities.rationale: Client-side rendering and GA4 analytics only; no backend runtime or database in this project

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with client-side API calls; complexity lies in the embeddable widget isolation, not server architecture

risk_level.rationale: No user authentication, no data storage; risk limited to CDN availability and tracking API uptime

data_sensitivity.rationale: Site displays only publicly accessible tracking status; no PII stored or processed server-side

external_exposure.rationale: Fully public consumer-facing site with no authentication required

access_model.value: No authentication for consumer tracking; merchant widget authenticated via carrier account token in embed script
access_model.rationale: Consumer lookups are anonymous; merchant branding and carrier account scoping controlled via script token

feature_scope.value: Tracking number lookup page, delivery timeline component, exception notification display, embeddable merchant widget with branding config, GDPR cookie consent banner
feature_scope.rationale: Complete set of consumer and merchant features

mvp_scope.value: Tracking lookup page with timeline and exception display; basic embeddable widget with carrier account token and custom logo support
mvp_scope.rationale: Core consumer and merchant value with minimal scope

security_baseline.value: Content Security Policy headers; no server-side secrets; carrier API key handled server-side in tracking API, not exposed to browser; HTTPS enforced via Vercel Edge
security_baseline.rationale: Static site security controlled entirely through HTTP headers and CDN configuration

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with Tailwind CSS and vanilla JavaScript for widget
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro for static generation; vanilla JS widget avoids React dependency in merchant third-party embeds

backend_stack.rationale: Static site with no server-side runtime; data fetched client-side from tracking API

data_platform.value: none
data_platform.rationale: No database; tracking data served by external carrier tracking API

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN distribution
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config static hosting with global edge distribution and automatic HTTPS

privacy_retention_policy.value: No server-side data retention; GDPR cookie consent required for GA4 analytics tracking; consent stored in localStorage only
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR requires explicit consent before analytics cookies for EU visitors

future_scope.value: SMS/email delivery notification signup, multi-carrier tracking aggregation, white-label merchant portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features requiring backend services outside this project scope

constraints.value: Widget must load in under 50KB gzipped to avoid performance penalty on merchant order pages; no React or heavy framework in widget bundle
constraints.rationale: Merchant pages often already have large bundles; widget must be lightweight

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for page views and tracking lookup events; Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard static site observability via GA4 and CDN analytics

execution_preference.value: Ship MVP in 3 weeks; iterate on widget configurability based on merchant feedback
execution_preference.rationale: Static site allows fast iteration with zero-downtime deploys on every commit

project.goal: Public parcel tracking site with embeddable merchant widget

security.auth: none for consumers; carrier account token in merchant embed script

devops.ci: GitHub Actions build and Vercel preview deploys on PR
devops.environments: preview per PR, production on main

reasoner_reviews.product: The tracking lookup page and delivery timeline are the core consumer value. The merchant embed widget is a high-leverage B2B feature that can drive adoption without requiring merchants to build their own tracking pages. GDPR consent banner is non-negotiable for EU consumer traffic given that GA4 uses cookies.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 is an excellent fit for this project: it generates pure static HTML with optional client-side islands for the tracking lookup fetch. The vanilla JS widget isolation is the right call to avoid forcing React onto merchant pages. Key risk is the tracking API CORS policy: the carrier tracking API must allow browser-side requests from consumer browsers and merchant embed domains, or a lightweight proxy edge function will be needed.
reasoner_reviews.security: Content Security Policy headers are the primary security control for a static site. The CSP must restrict script sources to prevent XSS via CDN compromise. Merchant carrier account tokens should be treated as public-facing credentials with rate limiting enforced server-side in the tracking API, not in the static site. GDPR consent must gate GA4 initialization, not just display a banner.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 50KB widget bundle constraint is achievable with vanilla JS but requires discipline: no moment.js for date formatting, inline SVG for icons rather than icon libraries. Vercel free tier handles expected consumer traffic volumes; paid plan needed if merchant widget adoption drives high origin request volumes.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with pages/index.astro for tracking lookup, pages/track/[number].astro for shareable tracking URL, and components/TrackingTimeline.astro for the delivery event display. Widget built as a separate vanilla JS entry point bundled independently with Rollup.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home page: tracking number search form posts to /track/{number}. Tracking page: fetches live status from carrier tracking API on client side, renders delivery timeline with event markers, exception banners, and estimated delivery window. Widget page: /widget.js script tag loads the tracking timeline inline on any merchant order page.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro static generation pre-renders all non-dynamic pages at build time. Tracking page uses client-side island hydration for the live fetch component. Widget bundle target under 50KB gzipped enforced in Rollup config with bundle analyzer check in CI.
frontend.frontend_risks: Carrier tracking API CORS policy may block browser-direct requests from consumer browsers or merchant embed domains; requires API to support wildcard or merchant-domain CORS headers.

security.auth_design: No authentication for consumer tracking lookups. Merchant embed scripts use a carrier account token as a URL parameter in the script src; token validates against tracking API rate limit bucket per merchant account.
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel Edge rate limiting on tracking lookup requests by IP: 60 requests per minute per IP to prevent scraping. Tracking number format validation client-side before API call to reduce invalid requests.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR consent banner initializes GA4 only after explicit opt-in; consent state stored in localStorage with 12-month expiry; no cookies set before consent. EU visitor detection via Accept-Language header for banner display logic.
security.security_risks: Merchant carrier account tokens embedded in public script src URLs are inherently low-privilege and rate-limited at the tracking API; token rotation capability should be documented for merchants.

data.entities: tracking_lookups (client-side only, not persisted). delivery_timelines (fetched from carrier API: tracking_number, carrier_name, origin, destination, current_status, estimated_delivery, events[]). exception_notifications (derived from delivery_timeline events: delay_type, delay_reason, new_eta). merchant_embed_configs (carrier_account_token, custom_logo_url, accent_color, locale -- passed as script attributes).
data.retention_and_deletion: No server-side data retention. GA4 analytics data retained 14 months per Google default. GDPR consent state in localStorage cleared on browser data wipe.
data.analytics_events: GA4 custom events: tracking_lookup_submitted (tracking_number_length, source: direct/merchant_embed), tracking_result_viewed (has_exception: bool, carrier_name), widget_loaded (merchant_domain), consent_accepted, consent_rejected.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repo; automatic preview deploys on every PR for stakeholder review; production deploy on merge to main; global edge CDN distribution with automatic SSL.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR, run Astro build and Rollup widget bundle, enforce widget bundle size < 50KB gzipped via bundlesize check, run Lighthouse CI for Core Web Vitals regression detection.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback to any previous deployment via Vercel dashboard or CLI; no database state to reconcile.
devops.devops_risks: Tracking API CORS changes by carrier team could silently break consumer lookups; monitoring should include synthetic tracking lookup tests from Vercel cron.

ISS-001.title: CORS policy not addressed for browser-direct tracking API calls
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan assumed browser-direct fetch to carrier tracking API but did not document whether the API supports CORS for consumer browser origins. If CORS is not supported, all tracking lookups will fail in production.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner: CORS dependency on carrier API not documented or mitigated

ISS-002.title: GA4 consent gate not described as blocking initialization
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 security section said GDPR banner displayed but did not confirm GA4 is blocked from initializing before consent, which would make the consent banner cosmetic rather than functionally compliant.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer: consent must gate GA4 init, not just show a banner

focus_issues.title: CORS policy not addressed for browser-direct tracking API calls
focus_issues.detail: Browser-direct fetch to carrier API will fail if CORS is not enabled; mitigation strategy absent from round 1 plan.

focus_issues.title: GA4 consent gate not described as blocking initialization
focus_issues.detail: GDPR consent banner must block GA4 initialization, not just display; round 1 did not confirm this.

Explicitly state that GA4 gtag initialization is deferred until after consent callback fires, not just that a banner is shown
