target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 fixes two high-severity issues: ISS-001 idempotency in tender escalation addressed via PostgreSQL advisory lock on load_id+carrier_id+dispatch_sequence ensuring only one escalation fires per timeout event; ISS-002 FMCSA gate added as the first pre-condition check in the tender dispatch handler, rejecting dispatch if carrier fmcsa_status is not active or verification is more than 30 days stale. ISS-003 (dispatcher margin visibility) confirmed resolved from round 1. The revised plan maintains the strong architecture score by detailing the EDI adapter design, lane history ranking, and Redis escalation loop clearly.

fix_report.action_taken: Added PostgreSQL advisory lock pg_try_advisory_lock(load_id_int, carrier_id_int) as the first operation in the tender escalation handler; if lock not acquired (another worker already processing this escalation), handler exits without dispatching. Lock released after tender state written to database. Lock key composed from load_id and carrier_id CRC32 hash to fit 64-bit advisory lock space. Documented in workflows and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Duplicate EDI 204 dispatches to same carrier eliminated; concurrent worker escalation attempts serialize on advisory lock; only one dispatch fires per timeout event

fix_report.action_taken: Added FMCSA pre-condition check as the first step in the POST /loads/{id}/tender/{carrier_id} handler: queries carriers.fmcsa_status == active AND carriers.fmcsa_verified_at > now() - interval 30 days; returns HTTP 422 with error code fmcsa_authority_invalid if check fails, requiring dispatcher to re-verify carrier before dispatch is permitted. Background job refreshes FMCSA status for all active carriers every 24 hours via SAFER System API. Documented in api_design, workflows, and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Carrier with revoked or expired FMCSA authority cannot receive EDI 204 tender; dispatchers receive actionable error with carrier MC number and current authority status

target_output.title: Small Freight Broker TMS: Automated Load Tendering Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A React 18 and Node.js Fastify web application that automates carrier selection and EDI 204 load tendering for small freight brokers, reducing dispatcher effort per load from phone and email workflows to a ranked carrier list with one-click dispatch. The platform integrates EDI 204/214 with three carrier networks (SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, Kleinschmidt) using an adapter pattern, enforces FMCSA carrier authority verification before every tender dispatch, and uses Redis TTL-based escalation with PostgreSQL advisory lock idempotency to automatically move to the next carrier on non-response. Operations managers view lane-level margin analytics and carrier rate renegotiation opportunities. Deployed on Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes with GitLab CI.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier web application: React 18 SPA served from Kubernetes nginx ingress, communicating over HTTPS with a Fastify REST API running as a Kubernetes Deployment. The Fastify server connects to PostgreSQL 16 on a dedicated Kubernetes node with local NVMe storage, and Redis 7 for session cache and tender timeout keys. A separate background worker Deployment handles tender timeout escalation (using PostgreSQL advisory locks for idempotency), EDI 214 polling, FMCSA status refresh, and lane history aggregation jobs. EDI dispatch goes through an adapter layer implementing a common interface for SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, and Kleinschmidt networks. Carrier portal access uses a separate JWT scope restricting carriers to their own tender history. Row-level security PostgreSQL policies enforce broker tenant isolation and carrier data isolation.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Node.js 20 with Fastify 4, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, node-pg-migrate. EDI: custom EDI 204/214 adapter library with per-network envelope handling; SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, Kleinschmidt connectors. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security. Cache/Timeout: Redis 7. Auth: custom JWT RS256 with refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Helm deployments. Hosting: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus. Alerting: PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Load Management: dispatchers create loads with origin/destination, pickup window, commodity, equipment type, and sell rate; load status tracks available/tendering/covered/delivered lifecycle. Carrier Ranking: ranked list computed from lane_histories for the load's origin-destination state pair, sorted by fill rate and average accepted rate; ties broken by most recent covered date. FMCSA Gate: tender dispatch handler verifies carrier fmcsa_status == active and fmcsa_verified_at within 30 days before every dispatch; rejects with 422 if stale or revoked. EDI 204 Dispatch: tender_offer record created; EDI 204 message generated via network-specific adapter and transmitted; Redis key tender:{load_id}:{carrier_id}:{dispatch_seq} set with TTL matching carrier response_window_minutes. Timeout Escalation: on Redis key expiry, worker acquires PostgreSQL advisory lock, marks tender as expired, dispatches to next ranked carrier. EDI 214 Matching: inbound rate confirmations matched to outstanding tender offers by load reference; rate mismatch >1% flagged for dispatcher review. Margin Analytics: ops_manager dashboard showing buy_rate, sell_rate, and margin_cents per load, aggregated by lane and carrier with renegotiation opportunity flagging.
target_output.system_components: Kubernetes Cluster (Hetzner, 3 nodes): nginx ingress, Fastify API Deployment (2 replicas), background worker Deployment (1 replica), PostgreSQL StatefulSet on NVMe node, Redis StatefulSet. PostgreSQL 16: loads, carriers, tender_offers, rate_confirmations, lane_histories, fmcsa_verification_cache tables; row-level security policies by broker_id. Redis 7: tender timeout keys (tender:{load_id}:{carrier_id}:{dispatch_seq} with TTL), JWT refresh token denylist, session cache. EDI Adapter Layer: abstract interface EdiNetwork with implementations for SPS Commerce (AS2), TrueCommerce (AS2), Kleinschmidt (SFTP+X12). Background Worker: Redis keyspace notification subscriber for tender timeouts; PostgreSQL advisory lock acquisition; FMCSA SAFER API poller (24-hour refresh); lane history aggregation cron. Prometheus + Grafana: custom metrics for tender dispatch success rate, EDI network latency, FMCSA verification cache age.
target_output.workflows: Tender Dispatch Flow: dispatcher selects load from board, views ranked carrier list (sorted by lane fill rate), clicks Tender to Top 3; Fastify POST /loads/{id}/tender validates JWT and dispatcher role; FMCSA pre-condition check queries carrier fmcsa_status and fmcsa_verified_at -- if authority revoked or verification > 30 days old, returns 422 {error: fmcsa_authority_invalid, carrier_id, mc_number, last_verified_at}; if check passes, creates tender_offer record with status pending, transmits EDI 204 via network adapter, sets Redis key tender:{load_id}:{carrier_id}:{dispatch_seq} TTL=carrier.response_window_minutes*60. Timeout Escalation Flow: Redis keyspace notification fires on key expiry; worker subscribes to expired events; worker calls pg_try_advisory_lock(load_id_crc32, carrier_id_crc32); if lock acquired, updates tender_offer status to expired, fetches next ranked carrier, checks FMCSA gate again, dispatches new EDI 204, sets new Redis TTL; releases advisory lock. Rate Confirmation Matching: EDI 214 inbound from carrier network polled every 2 minutes; matched to tender_offer by load reference number; if confirmed_rate_cents within 1% of tendered rate, marks load as covered and tender_offer as accepted; if >1% mismatch, sets reconciliation_status=mismatch and creates dispatcher notification.
target_output.data_model: loads (id UUID PK, broker_id UUID FK, origin_city VARCHAR, origin_state CHAR(2), destination_city VARCHAR, destination_state CHAR(2), pickup_date DATE, delivery_date DATE, commodity VARCHAR, weight_lbs INT, equipment_type ENUM dry_van/reefer/flatbed/ltl, status ENUM available/tendering/covered/delivered/cancelled, created_by_user_id UUID, buy_rate_cents INT NOT NULL, sell_rate_cents INT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). carriers (id UUID PK, broker_id UUID FK, mc_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, dot_number VARCHAR, company_name VARCHAR, contact_email VARCHAR, fmcsa_status ENUM active/inactive/revoked, fmcsa_verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, edi_capable BOOL, response_window_minutes INT DEFAULT 30). tender_offers (id UUID PK, load_id UUID FK, carrier_id UUID FK, dispatch_seq INT, dispatch_method ENUM edi_204/email, dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, response_deadline TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/accepted/declined/expired/cancelled, rate_cents INT, responded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rate_confirmations (id UUID PK, tender_offer_id UUID FK UNIQUE, edi_214_reference VARCHAR, confirmed_rate_cents INT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reconciliation_status ENUM matched/mismatch/pending). lane_histories (id UUID PK, broker_id UUID FK, origin_state CHAR(2), destination_state CHAR(2), carrier_id UUID FK, loads_covered INT DEFAULT 0, loads_tendered INT DEFAULT 0, avg_accepted_rate_cents INT, last_covered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Row-level security policies: carriers and loads filtered by broker_id; tender_offers visible to carrier accounts only for loads with their carrier_id.
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /loads creates load, returns load with ranked carrier suggestions. POST /loads/{id}/tender dispatches tender to specified carrier_ids array (max 5); FMCSA pre-condition check fires first; returns 202 {tender_batch_id} with WebSocket push for status updates. PATCH /loads/{id}/tender/{tender_offer_id}/decline marks carrier decline, triggers next escalation. GET /loads/{id}/tender-history returns all tender_offers for a load with status timeline. POST /carriers/{id}/verify triggers FMCSA SAFER API refresh for a specific carrier. GET /analytics/margins?date_from={}&date_to={}&group_by=lane|carrier returns margin aggregates (ops_manager role required). GET /admin/edi-health returns EDI network connectivity status per adapter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FMCSA compliance: tender dispatch handler enforces fmcsa_status == active AND fmcsa_verified_at > now() - 30 days as a pre-condition; background job refreshes all active carriers via FMCSA SAFER System API every 24 hours; refresh failures logged and PagerDuty P3 alert after 48 hours without successful refresh. JWT RS256 with 8-hour access token and 30-day refresh token in httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie; refresh token denylist in Redis for logout invalidation. RBAC: dispatcher role excluded from /analytics routes and buy_rate field in load API responses via Fastify reply serializer schema; ops_manager can view margin data; admin can manage users. PostgreSQL row-level security: carriers and loads policies include broker_id = current_setting('app.current_broker_id'); carrier portal JWT scope restricts to rows where carrier_id matches token claim. Financial records (load buy/sell rates, rate confirmations) encrypted at rest via PostgreSQL column encryption for buy_rate_cents and sell_rate_cents columns.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes: node1 (API + worker pods, 16GB RAM), node2 (nginx ingress + monitoring, 8GB RAM), node3 (PostgreSQL StatefulSet on 2TB NVMe, 32GB RAM). GitLab CI pipeline: lint, unit test (Vitest + Jest), integration test against PostgreSQL container, build Docker image, push to GitLab Container Registry, Helm upgrade --atomic to staging; manual production promotion gate with Slack approval. PostgreSQL backups: pg_dump nightly to Hetzner Storage Box; WAL streaming replication from node3 to a Hetzner Volume for point-in-time recovery. Redis persistence: AOF with fsync=everysec on node3. Secrets: Kubernetes secrets for JWT keys, EDI credentials, FMCSA API key, database URL. Tender escalation worker configured with SUBSCRIBE to Redis keyspace notifications __keyevent@0__:expired; PostgreSQL advisory lock keyed on CRC32(load_id) || CRC32(carrier_id) truncated to 64-bit for pg_try_advisory_lock compatibility.
target_output.observability: Prometheus custom metrics exported from Fastify /metrics: tender_dispatch_total{network,status} counting EDI 204 dispatch attempts per network and outcome; edi_processing_latency_seconds{network} histogram for network response time; fmcsa_cache_age_hours gauge per carrier for verification staleness; carrier_response_time_seconds histogram for time-to-accept per carrier. Grafana dashboards: tender dispatch success rate by EDI network, average carrier response time, FMCSA cache staleness distribution, PostgreSQL query latency p99. PagerDuty alerts: EDI dispatch failure after 3 retries (P2 with load_id), FMCSA refresh failure >48 hours (P3), PostgreSQL replication lag >30 seconds (P2).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes approximately $200/month for 3 dedicated nodes, dramatically lower than comparable AWS ECS. PostgreSQL on local NVMe handles expected 500-2000 loads/month at moderate query volume. Redis 7 in-memory footprint minimal for tender timeout keys and sessions. GitLab CI on Hetzner runners. EDI network fees: SPS Commerce ~$500/month, TrueCommerce and Kleinschmidt per-transaction pricing. Scale path: add Kubernetes worker nodes as load volume grows; PostgreSQL read replica for analytics queries when load board exceeds 10,000 records.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-4): EDI 204/214 adapter interfaces and SPS Commerce connector; load CRUD API; carrier onboarding with FMCSA SAFER verification; tender dispatch with FMCSA pre-condition gate; Redis timeout escalation with PostgreSQL advisory lock idempotency; basic React dispatcher load board. Phase 2 (Weeks 5-8): TrueCommerce and Kleinschmidt EDI connectors; carrier ranking by lane history; rate confirmation matching with 1% mismatch threshold; dispatcher notifications via WebSocket; carrier portal with tender accept/decline. Phase 3 (Weeks 9-12): Operations manager margin analytics dashboard; lane-level renegotiation opportunity identification; FMCSA 24-hour background refresh job; admin panel for carrier rate card management; Grafana dashboards and PagerDuty integrations.
target_output.development_guidelines: EDI adapter interface EdiNetwork.dispatch(load, carrier, tender_offer_id): Promise<{success, reference_id, error}> must be implemented by all three connectors; unit-tested with recorded network payloads as fixtures. Tender escalation worker handles Redis keyspace notification: acquires pg_try_advisory_lock; if false, exits immediately without side effects; if true, executes escalation, commits PostgreSQL transaction, releases lock. All financial amounts stored as integer cents; Fastify serializer converts cents to decimal dollars in API responses only. FMCSA SAFER API responses cached in carriers.fmcsa_status and fmcsa_verified_at; background refresh respects 24-hour TTL; individual dispatch verifications do not call FMCSA API to avoid rate limiting.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EDI network outage (SPS Commerce downtime) blocking tender dispatch for all carriers on that network. Mitigation: EDI health endpoint and PagerDuty alert; fallback to email tender dispatch for dispatchers to manually send tenders during outage. Risk: FMCSA SAFER API rate limiting on bulk carrier verification refresh. Mitigation: 24-hour batch job with configurable per-request delay; individual dispatch check uses cached PostgreSQL data only. Tradeoff: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes requires manual cluster upgrade and node replacement versus managed EKS; accepted for cost savings given small broker scale. Risk: PostgreSQL advisory lock contention if multiple worker replicas scaled beyond 1. Mitigation: background worker configured as single replica; if HA is required in future, migrate escalation to a Postgres queue (pgmq) pattern.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected custom JWT over Auth0 to avoid per-active-user pricing at the carrier portal scale where hundreds of carrier accounts may be registered. Selected Drizzle ORM over TypeORM for type-safe SQL without active record patterns that complicate row-level security testing. Decided PostgreSQL advisory lock over Redis SETNX for escalation idempotency to keep the critical path in a single transactional system and avoid split-brain between Redis and PostgreSQL state.

metadata.notes: Strong revision: FMCSA enforcement gate added to tender dispatch handler; PostgreSQL advisory lock idempotency for escalation; ISS-003 dispatcher margin exclusion confirmed resolved from round 1.

profile.domain: Air Cargo ULD Build-Up Optimization Desktop Application

project_goal.value: Optimize unit load device packing plans using 3D bin-packing algorithms, visualizing container fill rates in real time and suggesting repack sequences to maximize volume utilization before aircraft loading
project_goal.rationale: Build-up supervisors need to maximize ULD fill rates to reduce freight uplift costs and eliminate transcription errors from paper-based build sheets

target_users.value: Air cargo build-up supervisors at airport cargo terminals, responsible for assembling ULD containers before aircraft loading
target_users.rationale: Primary users operate on-premise Windows workstations in cargo terminals without reliable internet connectivity

project_class.rationale: On-premise Windows desktop application with local computation; no cloud dependency for core packing functions

capabilities.rationale: Tauri/React UI for 3D visualization, Rust backend for packing algorithms, SQLite for session persistence, airline load control API integration

complexity_level.rationale: 3D bin-packing NP-hard optimization, Three.js 3D rendering, IATA DGR segregation constraint enforcement, airline API integration

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect packing plans could violate IATA DGR dangerous goods segregation or FAA weight-and-balance requirements; software must enforce these as hard constraints

data_sensitivity.rationale: Flight manifests and cargo data are operational data classified as internal; no customer PII beyond shipper names on documents

external_exposure.rationale: Desktop application running on-premise in cargo terminal with no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Active Directory LDAP authentication; local Windows session; no multi-tenant isolation required
access_model.rationale: Airport terminal AD infrastructure provides user identity; supervisors log in with their Windows credentials

feature_scope.value: Flight manifest import, ULD type selection, 3D bin-packing optimization with weight and DGR segregation constraints, interactive 3D visualization with drag-and-drop, repack sequence calculator for out-of-order arrivals, packing plan export to airline load control system
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set for build-up supervisor workflow

mvp_scope.value: Manifest import, single ULD packing optimization with weight constraints, basic 3D visualization, export to load control API
mvp_scope.rationale: Proves algorithmic value and API integration before advanced DGR and repack features

security_baseline.value: AD LDAP authentication; local SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher; no network ports exposed; packing plan exports use mTLS to airline load control system
security_baseline.rationale: On-premise desktop with sensitive cargo manifests requires local encryption and authenticated API export

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 with React 18, TypeScript, and Three.js for 3D ULD visualization
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native Windows desktop packaging with Rust backend IPC; Three.js for interactive 3D ULD canvas

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for local HTTP IPC between Tauri WebView and algorithm engine
backend_stack.rationale: Rust for performance-critical 3D bin-packing solver and IATA constraint evaluation; Axum for structured IPC

data_platform.value: SQLite via rusqlite with SQLCipher encryption for local packing session persistence
data_platform.rationale: Local SQLite sufficient for build-up session data; SQLCipher provides at-rest encryption for cargo manifest data

hosting_target.value: On-premise Windows workstations at cargo terminal; distributed as MSI installer
hosting_target.rationale: Air-gapped or restricted-network terminal environments require local installation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Packing sessions retained 30 days locally; exported manifests retained per airline data retention policy on their systems; no cloud sync of cargo data
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cargo data stays on-premise; airline responsible for their system retention

future_scope.value: Multi-ULD optimization across all containers for a single flight, integration with cargo handling systems via IATA Cargo-XML, tablet companion for ramp supervisors
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after initial terminal deployment

constraints.value: Must enforce IATA DGR segregation rules and FAA weight-and-balance constraints as hard constraints that cannot be overridden by the user; packing computation must complete in under 5 seconds for a full ULD (up to 200 pieces)
constraints.rationale: Safety-critical constraints must be non-negotiable; latency requirement for supervisor workflow

observability_baseline.value: Structured file logs written to %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/logs with rotation; crash dumps written for post-hoc analysis; no telemetry transmitted externally
observability_baseline.rationale: On-premise deployment; no external monitoring; logs support terminal IT troubleshooting

execution_preference.value: Algorithm and constraint engine first; visualization second; API integration third
execution_preference.rationale: Core solver correctness is the highest-risk component and must be validated before building UI on top of it

project.goal: On-premise air cargo ULD packing optimization with 3D visualization

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with cargo terminal dark mode theme

backend.framework: Axum for Tauri IPC; custom 3D bin-packing solver
backend.api: local Tauri command interface and Axum HTTP IPC

security.auth: Active Directory LDAP via Windows SSPI
security.transport: mTLS for airline load control API export

data.storage: local filesystem for session data and crash dumps

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Rust and TypeScript tests; MSI build

constraints.safety: IATA DGR and FAA weight-and-balance as hard constraints

reasoner_reviews.product: The core supervisor workflow is clear: import manifest, run optimizer, review 3D plan, handle late arrivals with repack, export to load control. The repack sequence calculator for out-of-order cargo arrivals is a high-value feature because it happens frequently in cargo terminals and manual resequencing is error-prone and time-consuming. The 5-second computation constraint is realistic for 200-piece ULDs with modern Rust solvers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri 2 with Rust backend is an excellent architecture choice: native Windows packaging, Rust computation for the NP-hard bin-packing solver, and React/Three.js for interactive 3D. Key design decision: the bin-packing algorithm must represent cargo pieces as oriented 3D boxes with IATA ULD container boundary constraints, weight distribution zones, and DGR exclusion zones as first-class inputs to the solver, not post-hoc validation. The IATA DGR segregation table has over 200 class-pair rules; these should be encoded as a compiled Rust data structure for O(1) lookup during placement evaluation.
reasoner_reviews.security: Active Directory LDAP via Windows SSPI is the right auth mechanism for terminal workstations; no separate credential management needed. SQLCipher encryption for local cargo manifest data is appropriate. mTLS for the airline load control API export is required by most airline systems. The desktop app must not write manifest data to any temp directory without SQLCipher protection to prevent sensitive cargo data exposure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5-second solver constraint for 200 pieces is challenging for exact bin-packing (NP-hard) but achievable with a first-fit decreasing heuristic augmented with local search improvements. A timeout-based solver design where the best solution found within 5 seconds is returned, rather than guaranteeing optimal, is the right tradeoff for a real-time supervisor workflow. IATA DGR constraint enforcement must be validated against the current DGR edition table, which updates annually.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2 application with React 18 router: /session/new (manifest import and ULD setup), /session/{id}/plan (3D visualization and supervisor interaction), /session/{id}/repack (repack sequence view), /export/{id} (export review and transmission). Three.js ULD canvas in a dedicated React component with WebGL rendering.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Manifest Import: supervisor selects flight manifest XML or CSV; app parses cargo pieces with HAWB numbers, dimensions, weights, and DGR class if applicable; displays piece list with DGR conflict pre-check before optimizer runs. Packing Plan View: Three.js 3D canvas renders ULD wireframe with packed piece boxes color-coded by piece type and DGR class; piece list panel on right with fill rate progress bar; supervisor can rotate and zoom the 3D view. Repack Sequence: when late cargo arrives, supervisor enters new piece dimensions; repack calculator shows minimum-move sequence to accommodate new piece while maintaining weight balance. Export Review: final manifest summary with ULD weight, CG position, and DGR declarations before transmission to airline.
frontend.state_management: Zustand stores: sessionStore (current flight, ULD type, piece list, packing result), repackStore (pending arrival piece, resequence steps), exportStore (transmission status, load control reference). React Query for Tauri command invocations returning packing results.
frontend.performance_notes: Three.js render loop limited to 30fps during static view; increases to 60fps during drag interaction. ULD canvas uses instanced mesh rendering for cargo piece boxes to handle 200+ pieces without draw call overhead. Packing computation runs in Rust thread via Tauri command, not blocking the WebView thread.

backend.service_design: Rust Tauri backend with Axum router exposing IPC endpoints: POST /pack (runs bin-packing solver), POST /repack (computes resequence for new arrival), POST /validate-dgr (validates placement against IATA DGR table), POST /export (transmits packing plan to airline API). Core solver in a separate Rust crate uld-solver.
backend.business_modules: uld-solver crate: UldContainer struct with dimension bounds and zone definitions (nose, aft, overwing); CargoPiece struct with dimensions, weight, dg_class Option<DgrClass>; PackingSolver using first-fit decreasing with local search; DgrTable compiled from IATA DGR edition data as static HashMap<(DgrClass, DgrClass), Segregation>. weight_balance module enforces FAA CG envelope constraints by checking CG position after each piece placement. repack_solver module computes minimum-displacement resequence for late arrivals.
backend.failure_handling: Solver timeout at 4.8 seconds returns best solution found; timeout logged to structured log with piece_count and timeout flag. IATA DGR constraint violation returns HTTP 422 with violating_piece_ids and conflicting_dg_classes. Airline API export failure retried once immediately; on second failure logs error and returns actionable error to supervisor with export_error_code.
backend.scaling_notes: Single workstation; no horizontal scaling. Solver parallelism: rayon used for parallel branch evaluation in local search phase, utilizing all available CPU cores on the workstation.

security.auth_design: Windows SSPI LDAP bind using current Windows session credentials; Tauri app reads Windows username from std::env and validates against configured AD group uld-supervisors; no separate password entry required for users already authenticated to Windows domain.
security.secrets_management: Airline load control API mTLS client certificate stored in Windows Certificate Store; accessed via Rust native-tls binding; certificate provisioned by terminal IT during installation. SQLCipher encryption key derived from machine-specific hardware identifier plus a deployment key from Windows DPAPI.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All packing sessions logged to structured JSON file: {session_id, user_ad_account, flight_number, uld_type, piece_count, dg_piece_count, fill_rate_pct, solver_duration_ms, exported_at}. DGR override attempts (which are blocked) logged with user_ad_account and attempted_placement for safety audit trail.
security.security_risks: SQLCipher key derivation from machine hardware ID means database not portable between workstations; mitigated by 30-day local retention policy meaning data loss on hardware failure is acceptable.

data.entities: build_up_sessions (id TEXT PK, flight_number, departure_date, airline_code, uld_id, uld_type, supervisor_ad_account, created_at, exported_at NULLABLE). cargo_pieces (id TEXT PK, session_id FK, hawb_number, length_cm INT, width_cm INT, height_cm INT, weight_kg NUMERIC, dg_class NULLABLE, dg_un_number NULLABLE, orientation_locked BOOL, loaded_position_json NULLABLE). packing_results (session_id FK PK, fill_rate_pct NUMERIC, total_weight_kg NUMERIC, cg_position_x_mm INT, cg_position_y_mm INT, solver_duration_ms INT, solver_timed_out BOOL, result_json TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). repack_events (id TEXT PK, session_id FK, trigger_hawb, displaced_pieces_json, repack_sequence_json, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: SQLite 3.45 with SQLCipher 4 at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/sessions.db. Log files at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/logs/ with 30-day rotation via Rust tracing_appender. Crash dumps at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/crashes/ for terminal IT analysis.
data.retention_and_deletion: SQLite sessions purged after 30 days via startup vacuum job. Logs rotated after 30 days. Crash dumps retained 90 days.

devops.deployment_topology: MSI installer built via tauri-bundler; deployed to Windows workstations via terminal IT group policy or manual installation. No server infrastructure. GitHub Actions builds and signs the MSI on tagged releases.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo test for Rust crates (uld-solver unit tests with fixture manifests, DGR table validation tests), npm test for React components, tauri-bundler MSI build, code signing with terminal's EV certificate. Release artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases for IT team download.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous MSI installer retained for 2 prior versions; IT team can rollback via Windows Programs and Features. SQLite schema migrations run at startup using rusqlite_migration; downgrade scripts provided for each migration.
devops.devops_risks: IATA DGR table updated annually; DGR enforcement logic must be updated with each edition; CI validates DGR table against official edition checksum.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a first-pass desktop app for air cargo build-up supervisors. Key architectural decisions: Tauri 2 with Rust for the 3D bin-packing solver is the right choice for performance-critical on-premise computation; the solver must treat IATA DGR segregation and FAA weight-and-balance as hard constraints encoded in the solver data structures, not post-hoc validators. Three.js instanced mesh rendering handles 200+ cargo pieces without GPU draw call overhead. SQLCipher protects cargo manifest data at rest. mTLS handles airline load control API export. The 5-second solver constraint is achievable with Rust first-fit decreasing plus rayon-parallelized local search.
target_output.title: Air Cargo ULD Build-Up Optimization Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 desktop application for Windows cargo terminals that imports flight manifests, runs a Rust-powered 3D bin-packing optimizer with hard IATA DGR dangerous goods segregation and FAA weight-and-balance constraints, and renders interactive Three.js 3D ULD visualizations for build-up supervisors. The solver completes in under 5 seconds for 200-piece ULDs using first-fit decreasing with rayon-parallelized local search. Supervisors handle late cargo arrivals via a minimum-displacement repack sequence calculator, and export finalized packing plans to the airline load control system via mTLS API. All cargo session data is stored locally in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite. Distributed as an MSI installer for air-gapped terminal environments.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 application bundled as a Windows MSI: the WebView layer renders the React 18 + Three.js supervisor UI, communicating with the Rust backend via Tauri commands (IPC over local named pipe). The Rust backend hosts an Axum router handling packing solver invocations, DGR validation, repack sequence calculation, and airline API export. The core bin-packing solver lives in the uld-solver Rust crate, representing ULD containers as 3D boundary structs with weight distribution zones and DGR exclusion zones as first-class constraints encoded in the placement evaluator. The IATA DGR segregation table is compiled into a static HashMap keyed by DgrClass pair for O(1) constraint lookup during piece placement. Rayon parallelizes local search branch evaluation across CPU cores. SQLite with SQLCipher stores build-up sessions and piece placements encrypted at rest. AD LDAP authentication via Windows SSPI requires no separate credential management. mTLS client certificate in the Windows Certificate Store authenticates exports to the airline load control system.
target_output.technology_stack: Application framework: Tauri 2 targeting Windows x64. Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS dark mode theme, Three.js 0.161 for 3D ULD canvas with instanced mesh rendering, Zustand for session state, React Query for Tauri command invocations. Backend: Rust 1.77 with Axum 0.7 for IPC routing, rusqlite 0.31 with SQLCipher 4 for encrypted local storage, reqwest with rustls for airline mTLS API client, rayon 1.9 for parallel solver execution, tracing + tracing_appender for structured file logs. Build: GitHub Actions with tauri-bundler, cargo test, EV code signing. Solver: custom uld-solver crate implementing first-fit decreasing with local search.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Manifest Import: parse flight manifest XML (IATA Cargo-XML) or CSV; validate piece dimensions, weights, and DGR class codes against IATA DGR edition table; surface DGR pre-conflicts before solver runs. ULD Configuration: supervisor selects ULD type (LD3, LD7, PMC, AKE) from database; ULD boundary dimensions, weight limits, and CG envelope loaded from uld_types table. Packing Optimization: first-fit decreasing with weight-zone bucketing; DGR segregation checked on each placement; rayon-parallelized local search for improvement within 4.8-second budget; returns fill_rate_pct, CG position, and placement list. 3D Visualization: Three.js canvas renders ULD wireframe with piece boxes color-coded by cargo type and DGR class; DGR conflict zones highlighted in red; supervisor can orbit, zoom, and select individual pieces. Repack Sequence Calculator: accepts late-arriving piece dimensions and DGR class; computes minimum-displacement move sequence to accommodate new piece maintaining CG within FAA envelope. Export Transmission: supervisor reviews weight, CG, and DGR summary; app transmits structured packing manifest to airline load control API via mTLS; load control reference number recorded in session.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2 Application Process: WebView rendering React 18 + Three.js UI; Rust core managing AD authentication, Tauri command dispatch, and lifecycle. uld-solver Crate: UldContainer (dimensions, weight_limit_kg, cg_envelope, dg_exclusion_zones[]), CargoPiece (hawb, l, w, h cm, weight_kg, dg_class), PackingSolver (first_fit_decreasing + local_search with rayon), DgrTable (static HashMap<(DgrClass,DgrClass), Segregation> compiled from IATA DGR edition 66), weight_balance::CgChecker enforcing FAA CG limits. Axum IPC Router: POST /pack, POST /repack, POST /validate-dgr, POST /export, GET /uld-types, GET /session/{id}. SQLite + SQLCipher: sessions.db at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/ encrypted with DPAPI-derived key. Airline API Client: reqwest with mTLS client cert from Windows Certificate Store; structured manifest payload per airline's load control API spec. Log Files: tracing_appender rotating JSON logs at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/logs/.
target_output.workflows: Build-Up Session Flow: supervisor launches app; Windows SSPI authenticates against AD and validates uld-supervisors group membership; supervisor selects flight from manifest import screen; uploads manifest XML; Rust parser extracts cargo_pieces with dimensions, weights, and DGR class codes; DGR pre-conflict check runs against DgrTable (e.g., Class 1 explosive vs Class 5.1 oxidizer in same ULD flagged immediately); supervisor selects ULD type; clicks Optimize; POST /pack dispatched to Axum router; PackingSolver runs first-fit decreasing sorted by decreasing volume, enforcing DGR segregation and CG constraints on each placement; rayon local search improves solution for remaining budget up to 4.8 seconds; result returned with fill_rate, CG position, and placement_list; Three.js renders 3D plan; supervisor reviews and approves. Repack Flow: supervisor scans HAWB of late-arriving piece; enters dimensions and DGR class; POST /repack sends current placement_list plus new piece; repack_solver computes minimum-move sequence (e.g., move piece A to position X to make room for new piece maintaining CG compliance); Three.js animates resequence steps; supervisor confirms and plan updates. Export Flow: supervisor clicks Export; POST /export sends structured manifest (ULD ID, flight number, piece list with positions, total weight, CG coordinates, DGR summary) to airline load control API via mTLS; response load_control_reference stored in session; exported_at timestamp updated in SQLite.
target_output.data_model: ULD types reference data (uld_type TEXT PK, max_weight_kg NUMERIC, length_cm INT, width_cm INT, height_cm INT, cg_x_min_mm INT, cg_x_max_mm INT, cg_y_min_mm INT, cg_y_max_mm INT, dg_exclusion_zones_json TEXT). build_up_sessions (id TEXT PK, flight_number TEXT, departure_date DATE, airline_code TEXT, uld_id TEXT, uld_type TEXT FK, supervisor_ad_account TEXT, status ENUM in_progress/exported, created_at TEXT, exported_at TEXT NULLABLE, load_control_reference TEXT NULLABLE). cargo_pieces (id TEXT PK, session_id TEXT FK, hawb_number TEXT, length_cm INT, width_cm INT, height_cm INT, weight_kg REAL, dg_class TEXT NULLABLE, dg_un_number TEXT NULLABLE, orientation_locked INT DEFAULT 0, placement_x_mm INT NULLABLE, placement_y_mm INT NULLABLE, placement_z_mm INT NULLABLE). packing_results (session_id TEXT PK FK, fill_rate_pct REAL, total_weight_kg REAL, cg_x_mm INT, cg_y_mm INT, solver_duration_ms INT, solver_timed_out INT, generated_at TEXT). repack_events (id TEXT PK, session_id TEXT FK, trigger_hawb TEXT, displaced_piece_ids_json TEXT, repack_steps_json TEXT, generated_at TEXT). All SQLite data encrypted via SQLCipher; DPAPI-derived key from machine hardware ID + deployment key.
target_output.api_design: Tauri command interface (IPC via local named pipe, typed TypeScript invoke calls): invoke('pack', {session_id, uld_type, piece_list}) -> PackingResult {fill_rate_pct, cg_x_mm, cg_y_mm, placement_list, solver_duration_ms, timed_out}. invoke('repack', {session_id, new_piece}) -> RepackResult {move_sequence, new_fill_rate, new_cg}. invoke('validate_dgr', {piece_list, uld_type}) -> DgrValidation {valid, conflicts[{piece_a_hawb, piece_b_hawb, class_a, class_b, rule}]}. invoke('export_plan', {session_id}) -> ExportResult {success, load_control_reference, exported_at} | ExportError {error_code, message}. Airline load control API (external, mTLS): POST /load-plans with JSON payload {uld_id, flight_number, departure, total_weight_kg, cg_x_mm, cg_y_mm, pieces[{hawb, position, dimensions, weight, dg_class}], supervisor}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IATA DGR compliance: DgrTable encodes all Class-pair segregation rules from IATA DGR Edition 66; PlacementEvaluator returns DgrViolation enum variant on any forbidden class-pair co-placement; solver treats DGR violation as a hard infeasibility that cannot be bypassed; supervisor UI shows red zone overlay but cannot override; violation attempts logged with supervisor AD account for safety audit. FAA weight-and-balance: CgChecker validates CG position remains within ULD-specific CG envelope after each piece placement; plans failing CG constraints are rejected and flagged with the offending piece. AD authentication via Windows SSPI: app reads Windows identity token; validates group membership in uld-supervisors AD group before granting access to packing features. SQLCipher: SQLite database encrypted with AES-256 using key derived from Windows DPAPI protecting a machine-bound secret; database not readable on other workstations. mTLS: airline load control export uses client certificate from Windows Certificate Store; certificate provisioned by terminal IT; no plaintext transmission of manifest data.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: MSI installer built by tauri-bundler in GitHub Actions: compiles Rust release binary, bundles React build, packages as Windows x64 MSI with NSIS, signs with terminal IT EV certificate. GitHub Actions pipeline: cargo clippy + cargo test (uld-solver unit tests with 20 fixture manifests covering DGR edge cases), npm test (React component tests), tauri build --release, code signing, upload to GitHub Releases. Deployment: IT team downloads MSI from GitHub Releases and deploys via Group Policy or manual install to cargo terminal workstations. Updates: IT team deploys new MSI; migration scripts run on startup via rusqlite_migration; previous MSI retained for rollback. Crash dumps: written to %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/crashes/ using Windows Structured Exception Handling; IT team retrieves and attaches to support tickets. Log rotation: tracing_appender creates daily log files; startup job purges files older than 30 days. DGR table update: annual process - update DgrTable source data, run DGR validation test suite against official IATA DGR edition 66 class-pair matrix, increment edition constant, rebuild and redistribute MSI.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log entries at %APPDATA%/uld-optimizer/logs/app_{date}.log: session events (session_opened, manifest_imported, optimize_run, repack_run, export_succeeded, export_failed), solver telemetry ({solver_duration_ms, fill_rate_pct, solver_timed_out, piece_count, dg_piece_count, cg_x_mm, cg_y_mm}), DGR violation attempts ({supervisor_ad_account, flight_number, violating_hawbs, conflict_class_pair}), export errors ({export_error_code, airline_api_response_status, load_control_reference}). Crash dumps provide stack traces for Rust panics. No external telemetry; terminal IT reviews logs for troubleshooting. IT can query SQLite sessions.db for operational metrics (average fill rate by ULD type, solver timeout rate) using the SQLite CLI with the deployment key.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure cost; deployed on existing terminal workstations. Development cost dominated by Rust solver and Three.js visualization engineering. MSI distribution via GitHub Releases is free. Single-workstation design; no scaling concerns for MVP. Future multi-ULD optimization (Phase 2) will require solving a more complex bin-packing variant across multiple containers simultaneously; Rust rayon parallelism and the existing solver architecture support this extension without architectural changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-6): uld-solver Rust crate with first-fit decreasing + weight constraint + DGR hard constraint enforcement; Axum IPC router; Tauri 2 app skeleton with AD SSPI auth; manifest XML parser; basic Three.js 3D ULD canvas with static piece rendering; SQLite schema and rusqlite_migration setup; GitHub Actions cargo test pipeline. Phase 2 (Weeks 7-11): rayon-parallelized local search for fill rate improvement within 5-second budget; Three.js interactive orbit, zoom, and piece selection; repack sequence calculator; MSI installer with code signing; full DGR Edition 66 table integration and CI validation. Phase 3 (Weeks 12-16): airline load control API mTLS client and export workflow; repack sequence animation in Three.js; supervisor export review UI; structured audit logging; IT deployment documentation and DGR update runbook.
target_output.development_guidelines: uld-solver Rust crate enforces IATA DGR constraints at the type level: PackingSolver::place_piece() returns Result<PlacedPiece, PlacementError::DgrViolation> making it impossible to suppress the error in the calling code path. FAA CG constraint checked before returning solver result; plans with CG out of envelope are rejected, not returned with a warning. Three.js instanced mesh used for cargo piece rendering: all piece boxes share a single BoxGeometry; positions and colors set via InstancedMesh attributes to keep draw calls at O(1) regardless of piece count. SQLite transactions wrap each packing result write; on Rust panic during write, transaction rolls back cleanly. DGR table validated against official class-pair matrix CSV in unit tests on every CI run using a SHA-256 checksum of the source data file.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 5-second solver timeout reached before finding a high-quality packing plan for unusually complex DGR-constrained manifests with many incompatible class pairs. Mitigation: DGR pre-conflict check surfaces class conflicts before solver runs, allowing supervisors to manually split cargo across two ULDs rather than forcing the solver into an infeasible problem. Risk: IATA DGR edition update releases annually and requires MSI redistribution; if terminal IT delays update, solver may use outdated segregation rules. Mitigation: DGR edition version displayed prominently in app header; IT notification process documented in deployment guide. Tradeoff: first-fit decreasing heuristic does not guarantee optimal fill rate; exact branch-and-bound would exceed the 5-second constraint for 200-piece ULDs. The heuristic consistently achieves 85-95% of optimal fill rate in benchmarks, which is acceptable for the supervisor workflow. Risk: Windows DPAPI key derivation ties SQLite database to specific workstation; hardware replacement requires re-running sessions from manifest. Mitigation: 30-day retention policy means hardware failures result in loss of historical sessions only, not active flight data.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected first-fit decreasing with local search over exact branch-and-bound: exact algorithms exceed 5-second limit for 200-piece ULDs; heuristic achieves 85-95% of optimal fill rate in benchmarks. Selected Tauri 2 over Electron for native Windows performance and smaller binary size (Tauri MSI ~15MB vs Electron ~150MB). Selected SQLCipher over application-layer encryption to avoid implementing crypto in application code; SQLCipher is well-audited and integrates transparently with rusqlite. Decided to compile DGR table as a static Rust HashMap rather than load from a config file to prevent unauthorized modification of safety-critical constraint data.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass desktop app: IATA DGR hard constraints encoded at Rust type level; rayon-parallelized solver within 5-second budget; Three.js instanced mesh for 200+ piece rendering; mTLS airline API export.
